idea_spark.idea: Treat augmentation strategy selection as a reinforcement learning problem, dynamically optimizing processing for each sample through environmental feedback.
idea_spark.risk: Training stability and generalization capability of the RL policy require thorough validation, potentially increasing system complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires rethinking static data processing pipelines as dynamic decision-making processes to better accommodate individual differences.

papers.title: Evaluating LLMs as Human Surrogates in Controlled Experiments
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to simulate human responses in behavioral research, yet it remains unclear when LLM-generated data support the same experimental inferences as human data. We evaluate this by directly comparing off-the-shelf LLM-generated responses with human responses from a canonical survey experiment on accuracy perception. Each human observation is converted into a structured prompt, and models generate a single 0--10 outcome variable without task-specific training; identical statistical analyses are applied to human and synthetic responses. We find that LLMs reproduce several directional effects observed in humans, but effect magnitudes and moderation patterns vary across models. Off-the-shelf LLMs therefore capture aggregate belief-updating patterns under controlled conditions but do not consistently match human-scale effects, clarifying when LLM-generated data can function as behavioral surrogates.

ai.tldr: 评估现成大型语言模型在受控调查实验中作为人类行为代理的可行性，发现其能复现方向性效应，但效应大小和调节模式存在差异。
ai.motivation: LLM 越来越多地被用于模拟人类行为研究中的响应，但尚不清楚 LLM 生成的数据何时能支持与人类数据相同的实验推断。
ai.method: 将人类观察转化为结构化提示，让模型生成 0-10 的结果变量，无需任务特定训练；对人工和合成响应应用相同的统计分析。
ai.result: LLM 复现了人类中观察到的几个方向性效应，但效应大小和调节模式因模型而异。
ai.help_to_user: 明确了 LLM 生成的数据在受控条件下可以捕捉总体信念更新模式，但不能一致地匹配人类尺度的效应。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个框架，用于系统评估不同 LLM 在特定实验范式下作为人类代理的保真度。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖 LLM 代理可能导致对效应大小的错误估计，尤其是在调节效应复杂的实验中。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可以探索特定微调或提示工程技术，以改善 LLM 在效应大小和调节模式上与人类数据的一致性。

en.tldr: Evaluates the feasibility of off-the-shelf LLMs as human behavioral surrogates in controlled survey experiments, finding they reproduce directional effects but differ in effect magnitudes and moderation patterns.
en.motivation: LLMs are increasingly used to simulate human responses in behavioral research, but it remains unclear when LLM-generated data support the same experimental inferences as human data.
en.method: Convert human observations into structured prompts, have models generate a single 0–10 outcome variable without task-specific training; apply identical statistical analyses to human and synthetic responses.

idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on LLM surrogates may lead to misestimation of effect sizes, especially in experiments with complex moderation effects.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explore specific fine-tuning or prompt engineering techniques to improve consistency between LLMs and human data in effect magnitudes and moderation patterns.

papers.title: Housing Decisions under Mobility Risk: A Stochastic Threshold Approach
papers.summary: We develop a stochastic free-boundary model of housing tenure decisions in markets with high mobility risk, such as areas near military installations. Housing prices and rents follow correlated diffusion processes, and households face an uncertain relocation horizon. We derive a closed-form characterization of the optimal buy-versus-rent boundary in terms of the price-to-rent ratio. The model highlights how mobility risk reduces the value of ownership by shortening the effective holding period and increasing uncertainty. As a result, identical price-to-rent ratios can imply different optimal decisions across locations. Numerical illustrations show how variations in volatility and relocation intensity shift the threshold, providing a structural interpretation of observed cross-market heterogeneity. The framework offers a tractable tool for analyzing housing decisions under uncertainty and for interpreting price-to-rent ratios in high-mobility environments.

ai.tldr: 本文开发了一个考虑高流动性风险（如军事基地附近）的住房产权决策随机自由边界模型，推导出价格租金比的最优购房/租房边界闭式解。
ai.motivation: 在高流动性风险的市场中，传统价格租金比可能无法准确指导住房决策，需要量化流动性风险对产权价值的影响。
ai.method: 建立住房价格和租金遵循相关扩散过程的随机模型，引入不确定搬迁时域，通过自由边界问题求解最优决策阈值。
ai.result: 流动性风险通过缩短有效持有期和增加不确定性降低所有权价值，导致相同价格租金比在不同市场对应不同最优决策。数值模拟展示了波动率和搬迁强度对阈值的影响。
ai.help_to_user: 为分析不确定性下的住房决策提供了可处理的理论框架，有助于解释高流动性环境中价格租金比的异质性。

idea_spark.idea: 将搬迁风险建模为随机时域，嵌入资产定价框架。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设扩散过程和固定搬迁强度可能过于简化现实动态。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可扩展至包含就业变动、家庭结构变化等多源流动性风险的研究。

idea_spark.inspiration: 可扩展至包含就业变动、家庭结构变化等多源流动性风险的研究。

en.tldr: This paper develops a stochastic free-boundary model for housing tenure decisions under high mobility risk, deriving a closed-form optimal buy-rent boundary in terms of the price-to-rent ratio.
en.motivation: In markets with high mobility risk, traditional price-to-rent ratios may not accurately guide housing decisions, necessitating quantification of how mobility risk affects ownership value.
en.method: A stochastic model where housing prices and rents follow correlated diffusion processes, incorporating an uncertain relocation horizon, solved as a free-boundary problem to obtain optimal decision thresholds.
en.result: Mobility risk reduces ownership value by shortening the effective holding period and increasing uncertainty, causing identical price-to-rent ratios to imply different optimal decisions across locations. Numerical illustrations show how volatility and relocation intensity shift the threshold.
en.help_to_user: Provides a tractable framework for analyzing housing decisions under uncertainty and interpreting price-to-rent ratio heterogeneity in high-mobility environments.

idea_spark.idea: Model relocation risk as a stochastic horizon embedded within an asset pricing framework.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions of diffusion processes and constant relocation intensity may oversimplify real-world dynamics.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could be extended to incorporate multiple sources of mobility risk, such as job changes or family structure shifts.

papers.title: FD-NL2SQL: Feedback-Driven Clinical NL2SQL that Improves with Use
papers.summary: Clinicians exploring oncology trial repositories often need ad-hoc, multi-constraint queries over biomarkers, endpoints, interventions, and time, yet writing SQL requires schema expertise. We demo FD-NL2SQL, a feedback-driven clinical NL2SQL assistant for SQLite-based oncology databases. Given a natural-language question, a schema-aware LLM decomposes it into predicate-level sub-questions, retrieves semantically similar expert-verified NL2SQL exemplars via sentence embeddings, and synthesizes executable SQL conditioned on the decomposition, retrieved exemplars, and schema, with post-processing validity checks. To improve with use, FD-NL2SQL incorporates two update signals: (i) clinician edits of generated SQL are approved and added to the exemplar bank; and (ii) lightweight logic-based SQL augmentation applies a single atomic mutation (e.g., operator or column change), retaining variants only if they return non-empty results. A second LLM generates the corresponding natural-language question and predicate decomposition for accepted variants, automatically expanding the exemplar bank without additional annotation. The demo interface exposes decomposition, retrieval, synthesis, and execution results to support interactive refinement and continuous improvement.

ai.tldr: FD-NL2SQL 是一个反馈驱动的临床自然语言转SQL助手，用于肿瘤学数据库，通过检索专家验证的示例和逻辑增强来自动改进。
ai.motivation: 临床医生需要从肿瘤学试验库中进行复杂的临时查询，但编写SQL需要专业知识。
ai.method: 系统使用模式感知LLM分解问题，检索语义相似的专家验证示例，合成可执行SQL，并通过临床医生编辑和逻辑增强进行更新。
ai.result: 系统支持交互式细化和持续改进，自动扩展示例库而无需额外标注。
ai.help_to_user: 为临床医生提供了一个无需SQL专业知识即可探索肿瘤学数据库的工具，并通过使用不断改进。

idea_spark.idea: 将临床医生对生成SQL的编辑作为新示例，并应用轻量级逻辑突变来探索有效的查询变体。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于初始示例库的质量和LLM的分解准确性；逻辑突变可能产生语义不正确的查询。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过将用户交互和自动变异结合到学习循环中，实现系统在使用中持续改进。

en.tldr: FD-NL2SQL is a feedback-driven clinical NL2SQL assistant for oncology databases that improves automatically through retrieval of expert-verified exemplars and logic-based augmentation.
en.motivation: Clinicians need to perform complex ad-hoc queries over oncology trial repositories, but writing SQL requires schema expertise.
en.method: The system uses a schema-aware LLM to decompose questions, retrieves semantically similar expert-verified exemplars, synthesizes executable SQL, and updates via clinician edits and lightweight logic-based SQL augmentation.
en.result: The system supports interactive refinement and continuous improvement, automatically expanding the exemplar bank without additional annotation.
en.help_to_user: Provides clinicians a tool to explore oncology databases without SQL expertise, improving with use.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate clinician edits of generated SQL as new exemplars and apply lightweight logic mutations to explore effective query variants.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the quality of the initial exemplar bank and the accuracy of LLM decomposition; logic mutations may produce semantically incorrect queries.
idea_spark.inspiration: Enables continuous system improvement through a learning loop that combines user interaction and automated variation.

papers.title: Frequency-Aware Flow Matching for High-Quality Image Generation
papers.summary: Flow matching models have emerged as a powerful framework for realistic image generation by learning to reverse a corruption process that progressively adds Gaussian noise. However, because noise is injected in the latent domain, its impact on different frequency components is non-uniform. As a result, during inference, flow matching models tend to generate low-frequency components (global structure) in the early stages, while high-frequency components (fine details) emerge only later in the reverse process. Building on this insight, we propose Frequency-Aware Flow Matching (FreqFlow), a novel approach that explicitly incorporates frequency-aware conditioning into the flow matching framework via time-dependent adaptive weighting. We introduce a two-branch architecture: (1) a frequency branch that separately processes low- and high-frequency components to capture global structure and refine textures and edges, and (2) a spatial branch that synthesizes images in the latent domain, guided by the frequency branch's output. By explicitly integrating frequency information into the generation process, FreqFlow ensures that both large-scale coherence and fine-grained details are effectively modeled low-frequency conditioning reinforces global structure, while high-frequency conditioning enhances texture fidelity and detail sharpness. On the class-conditional ImageNet-256 generation benchmark, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance with an FID of 1.38, surpassing the prior diffusion model DiT and flow matching model SiT by 0.79 and 0.58 FID, respectively. Code is available at https://github.com/OliverRensu/FreqFlow.

ai.tldr: FreqFlow通过频率感知条件化改进流匹配模型，在ImageNet-256上实现1.38 FID的SOTA性能。
ai.motivation: 传统流匹配模型在潜空间添加噪声时对不同频率分量影响不均，导致推理时低频结构先出现、高频细节后生成的问题。
ai.method: 提出双分支架构：频率分支分别处理高低频分量以捕获全局结构和细化纹理；空间分支在频率分支引导下在潜空间合成图像，并通过时间依赖的自适应权重进行频率感知条件化。
ai.result: 在ImageNet-256类别条件生成基准上达到1.38 FID，分别超越DiT和SiT模型0.79和0.58 FID。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要高质量图像生成的研究者提供了显式建模频率分量的新框架，改善生成图像的全局一致性和细节保真度。

idea_spark.idea: 在扩散/流匹配过程中引入频域引导，或设计多分辨率协同生成架构。
idea_spark.risk: 频率分支可能增加计算开销；频带划分策略需要针对不同数据域调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将信号处理中的频域分析与生成模型结合，探索感知特性（如对比敏感度）在生成过程中的显式利用。

zh.tldr: FreqFlow通过频率感知条件化改进流匹配模型，在ImageNet-256上实现1.38 FID的SOTA性能。
zh.motivation: 传统流匹配模型在潜空间添加噪声时对不同频率分量影响不均，导致推理时低频结构先出现、高频细节后生成的问题。

zh.result: 在ImageNet-256类别条件生成基准上达到1.38 FID，分别超越DiT和SiT模型0.79和0.58 FID。
zh.help_to_user: 为需要高质量图像生成的研究者提供了显式建模频率分量的新框架，改善生成图像的全局一致性和细节保真度。

idea_spark.idea: 在扩散/流匹配过程中引入频域引导，或设计多分辨率协同生成架构。
idea_spark.risk: 频率分支可能增加计算开销；频带划分策略需要针对不同数据域调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将信号处理中的频域分析与生成模型结合，探索感知特性（如对比敏感度）在生成过程中的显式利用。

en.tldr: FreqFlow improves flow matching models via frequency-aware conditioning, achieving SOTA 1.38 FID on ImageNet-256.
en.motivation: Conventional flow matching models inject noise non-uniformly across frequency components in latent space, causing low-frequency structure to emerge early and high-frequency details only later during inference.
en.method: Proposes a two-branch architecture: a frequency branch separately processes low/high-frequency components to capture global structure and refine textures; a spatial branch synthesizes images in latent domain guided by the frequency branch, with time-dependent adaptive weighting for frequency-aware conditioning.
en.result: Achieves 1.38 FID on class-conditional ImageNet-256 benchmark, surpassing DiT and SiT by 0.79 and 0.58 FID respectively.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel framework with explicit frequency modeling for researchers needing high-quality image generation, improving global coherence and detail fidelity.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce frequency-domain guidance in diffusion/flow matching processes, or design multi-resolution cooperative generation architectures.
idea_spark.risk: Frequency branch may increase computational overhead; frequency band partitioning requires adaptation to different data domains.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining signal processing frequency analysis with generative models, exploring explicit utilization of perceptual properties (e.g., contrast sensitivity) in generation processes.

papers.title: GEN-Graph: Heterogeneous PIM Accelerator for General Computational Patterns in Graph-based Dynamic Programming
papers.summary: While graph-based dynamic programming (DP) is a cornerstone of genomics and network analytics, its efficiency is hampered by fundamentally conflicting computational patterns. Matrix-centric DP drives regular, compute-bound network analytics, while topology-centric DP handles irregular, memory-bound genomic traversals. These two categories of DP have substantially different computation patterns and dataflows, which makes it difficult for a single homogeneous processing-in-memory (PIM) architecture to efficiently support both.   This work presents GEN-Graph, a novel heterogeneous PIM chiplet that integrates two types of specialized compute tiles within a 2.5D package: Matrix-tile, a processing-using-memory (PUM) tile optimized for matrix-centric workloads, such as all-pairs shortest path (APSP); and traversal-tile, a processing-near-memory (PNM) tile optimized for traversal-centric DP workloads, such as DNA sequence alignment. Our hardware-software co-design employs recursive partitioning and reconfigurable windowed bit-parallel logic to ensure exact computation. Results show the matrix tile achieves 42.8x speedup and 392x energy efficiency over the NVIDIA H100 GPU for APSP. For sequence-to-graph alignment, the traversal tile sustains 2.56 million reads/s (short-reads) and 39.3 thousand reads/s (long-reads), outperforming state-of-the-art accelerators by up to 2.56x in throughput. GEN-Graph provides the first scalable, exact solution for general DP dataflows by matching hardware specialization to algorithmic structure.

ai.tldr: GEN-Graph是一种异构存内计算芯片，集成矩阵计算和遍历计算两种专用模块，分别优化图动态规划中的规则矩阵计算和不规则拓扑遍历，实现高性能、高能效的精确计算。
ai.motivation: 图动态规划是基因组学和网络分析的核心，但其计算模式存在根本冲突：矩阵中心型DP是规则、计算密集型，而拓扑中心型DP是不规则、访存密集型。单一同构存内计算架构难以同时高效支持两者。
ai.method: 提出GEN-Graph异构PIM小芯片，在2.5D封装中集成两种专用计算模块：针对矩阵中心型负载（如全对最短路径）优化的存内计算矩阵模块，以及针对遍历中心型负载（如DNA序列比对）优化的近存计算遍历模块。采用软硬件协同设计，包括递归分区和可重构窗口化位并行逻辑，确保精确计算。
ai.result: 矩阵模块在全对最短路径上相比NVIDIA H100 GPU实现42.8倍加速和392倍能效提升。遍历模块在序列到图比对中，短读长维持256万读/秒，长读长维持3.93万读/秒，吞吐量最高达现有先进加速器的2.56倍。
ai.help_to_user: 为图动态规划提供了首个可扩展的精确解决方案，通过硬件专业化匹配算法结构，显著提升基因组学和网络分析的计算效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将算法结构映射到专用硬件模块，通过异构PIM架构同时支持规则和不规则计算模式
idea_spark.risk: 硬件设计复杂度高，需要针对特定算法模式进行深度优化，通用性可能受限
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发针对其他混合计算模式（如规则/不规则混合负载）的专用加速器设计，可考虑将类似异构思想应用于其他领域如稀疏/稠密混合计算

en.tldr: GEN-Graph is a heterogeneous processing-in-memory accelerator that integrates two specialized modules for matrix computation and traversal computation, respectively optimizing regular matrix operations and irregular topological traversals in graph-based dynamic programming, achieving high-performance and energy-efficient exact computation.
en.motivation: Graph-based dynamic programming is fundamental to genomics and network analytics, but its computational patterns are fundamentally conflicting: matrix-centric DP is regular and compute-bound, while topology-centric DP is irregular and memory-bound. A single homogeneous PIM architecture struggles to efficiently support both.
en.method: Proposes GEN-Graph, a heterogeneous PIM chiplet integrating two specialized compute tiles within a 2.5D package: a processing-using-memory matrix tile optimized for matrix-centric workloads like all-pairs shortest path, and a processing-near-memory traversal tile optimized for traversal-centric workloads like DNA sequence alignment. Employs hardware-software co-design with recursive partitioning and reconfigurable windowed bit-parallel logic to ensure exact computation.
en.result: The matrix tile achieves 42.8x speedup and 392x energy efficiency over NVIDIA H100 GPU for APSP. For sequence-to-graph alignment, the traversal tile sustains 2.56 million reads/s (short-reads) and 39.3 thousand reads/s (long-reads), outperforming state-of-the-art accelerators by up to 2.56x in throughput.
en.help_to_user: Provides the first scalable, exact solution for general DP dataflows by matching hardware specialization to algorithmic structure, significantly improving computational efficiency for genomics and network analytics.

idea_spark.idea: Map algorithmic structures to specialized hardware modules, supporting both regular and irregular computational patterns through heterogeneous PIM architecture
idea_spark.risk: High hardware design complexity requiring deep optimization for specific algorithmic patterns, potentially limited generality
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires specialized accelerator designs for other mixed computational patterns (e.g., regular/irregular mixed workloads), suggesting similar heterogeneous approaches could be applied to domains like sparse/dense mixed computing

papers.title: Beyond Surface Statistics: Robust Conformal Prediction for LLMs via Internal Representations
papers.summary: Large language models are increasingly deployed in settings where reliability matters, yet output-level uncertainty signals such as token probabilities, entropy, and self-consistency can become brittle under calibration--deployment mismatch. Conformal prediction provides finite-sample validity under exchangeability, but its practical usefulness depends on the quality of the nonconformity score. We propose a conformal framework for LLM question answering that uses internal representations rather than output-facing statistics: specifically, we introduce Layer-Wise Information (LI) scores, which measure how conditioning on the input reshapes predictive entropy across model depth, and use them as nonconformity scores within a standard split conformal pipeline. Across closed-ended and open-domain QA benchmarks, with the clearest gains under cross-domain shift, our method achieves a better validity--efficiency trade-off than strong text-level baselines while maintaining competitive in-domain reliability at the same nominal risk level. These results suggest that internal representations can provide more informative conformal scores when surface-level uncertainty is unstable under distribution shift.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于大语言模型内部表征的鲁棒保形预测框架，使用层间信息分数作为非一致性度量，在分布偏移下比文本级基线具有更好的有效性-效率权衡。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型在可靠性至关重要的场景中部署时，输出级不确定性信号（如词元概率、熵、自一致性）在校准-部署不匹配下可能变得脆弱。保形预测在可交换性下提供有限样本有效性，但其实际效用取决于非一致性分数的质量。
ai.method: 为LLM问答提出一个保形框架，使用内部表征而非面向输出的统计量：引入层间信息分数，衡量输入条件如何重塑跨模型深度的预测熵，并将其用作标准拆分保形流程中的非一致性分数。
ai.result: 在封闭式和开放域QA基准测试中，尤其在跨域偏移下，该方法在相同名义风险水平下实现了比强文本级基线更好的有效性-效率权衡，同时保持了有竞争力的域内可靠性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种更鲁棒的LLM不确定性量化方法，当表面级不确定性在分布偏移下不稳定时，内部表征可以提供更具信息量的保形分数。

idea_spark.idea: 利用模型不同层的激活变化（如熵的演变）来构建对输入更敏感、对表面噪声更鲁棒的非一致性度量。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于特定模型架构的内部表征，可能在不同模型或变体间泛化性有限；计算层间信息分数可能增加推理开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从模型内部工作机理（而不仅是最终输出）中挖掘更稳定的可靠性信号，为可解释AI与不确定性量化的交叉研究提供新视角。

en.tldr: Proposes a robust conformal prediction framework for LLMs based on internal representations, using Layer-Wise Information scores as nonconformity measures, achieving better validity-efficiency trade-offs under distribution shift compared to text-level baselines.
en.motivation: Large language models are increasingly deployed where reliability matters, yet output-level uncertainty signals (token probabilities, entropy, self-consistency) can become brittle under calibration-deployment mismatch. Conformal prediction provides finite-sample validity under exchangeability, but its practical usefulness depends on the quality of the nonconformity score.
en.method: Introduces a conformal framework for LLM question answering that uses internal representations rather than output-facing statistics: specifically, Layer-Wise Information scores measure how conditioning on the input reshapes predictive entropy across model depth, and are used as nonconformity scores within a standard split conformal pipeline.
en.result: Across closed-ended and open-domain QA benchmarks, with clearest gains under cross-domain shift, the method achieves a better validity-efficiency trade-off than strong text-level baselines while maintaining competitive in-domain reliability at the same nominal risk level.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more robust method for LLM uncertainty quantification; internal representations can yield more informative conformal scores when surface-level uncertainty is unstable under distribution shift.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage activation changes across model layers (e.g., evolution of entropy) to construct nonconformity measures more sensitive to input and more robust to surface noise.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on internal representations of specific model architectures may limit generalization across different models or variants; computing layer-wise information scores may add inference overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires mining more stable reliability signals from the internal workings of models (not just final outputs), offering a new perspective at the intersection of interpretable AI and uncertainty quantification.

papers.title: Uncertainty principles and singular potentials
papers.summary: We establish uncertainty principles on compact Riemannian manifolds without boundary in the setting of Laplace-Beltrami operators, including the case of real-valued singular potentials. We replace the classical homogeneity assumption by a quantitative spectral condition and obtain corresponding stability versions of uncertainty inequalities. In particular, we prove that \[ (1-ε-ε')^2 \leq \frac{|E|}{|M|}\cdot \# X_S \cdot \sup_{x\in E} \frac{A_S(x)}{\frac{\# X_S}{|M|}}, \] which recovers the classical bound in the homogeneous case, quantifies its deterioration in the presence of spectral inhomogeneity, and is shown to be sharp in general. In {\it dimension one}, we show that the homogeneity condition holds automatically, and we complement this rigidity by incorporating Fourier-ratio complexity bounds, yielding a quantitative relationship between spectral complexity and spatial support. In higher dimensions, we derive analogous results using pointwise Weyl laws and the eigenfunction restriction estimates on submanifolds.

ai.tldr: 在无边界紧致黎曼流形上，针对拉普拉斯-贝尔特拉米算子（含奇异势）建立了不确定性原理，用定量谱条件替代经典齐次性假设，并得到稳定性版本的不等式。
ai.motivation: 经典不确定性原理通常依赖齐次性假设，本文旨在放宽此限制，研究谱非齐次性对不确定性不等式的影响，并量化其稳定性。
ai.method: 引入定量谱条件替代齐次性假设；在一维情形利用傅里叶比复杂度界；在高维情形结合点态Weyl定律和子流形上的特征函数限制估计。
ai.result: 证明了一个包含谱非齐次性量化因子的不确定性不等式，该不等式在齐次情形恢复经典界，并证明了一般情形下的尖锐性；一维自动满足齐次性条件，并建立了谱复杂度与空间支撑的定量关系。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理非齐次谱背景下的不确定性原理提供了普适框架，将经典结果推广到含奇异势的紧致流形上，并给出稳定性量化工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将不确定性原理中的齐次性假设松弛为可量化的谱条件，从而容纳更一般的算子（如含奇异势），并研究谱非齐次性对不等式稳定性的影响。
idea_spark.risk: 高维情形依赖点态Weyl定律和限制估计，这些工具在更奇异的流形或算子中可能受限；定量关系的显式常数可能难以计算。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对非均匀介质中波动传播的控制性研究，或为压缩感知中非均匀采样提供数学基础；谱复杂度度量可连接至信息论中的熵概念。

en.tldr: Establishes uncertainty principles on compact Riemannian manifolds without boundary for Laplace-Beltrami operators with singular potentials, replacing classical homogeneity assumptions with quantitative spectral conditions and deriving stability versions of uncertainty inequalities.
en.motivation: Classical uncertainty principles often rely on homogeneity assumptions; this work aims to relax such constraints, study the effect of spectral inhomogeneity on uncertainty inequalities, and quantify their stability.
en.method: Introduces quantitative spectral conditions to replace homogeneity assumptions; uses Fourier-ratio complexity bounds in one dimension; combines pointwise Weyl laws and eigenfunction restriction estimates on submanifolds in higher dimensions.
en.result: Proves an uncertainty inequality incorporating a quantifier of spectral inhomogeneity, which recovers the classical bound in homogeneous cases and is shown to be sharp in general; demonstrates automatic homogeneity in one dimension and establishes a quantitative relationship between spectral complexity and spatial support.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general framework for uncertainty principles under non-homogeneous spectral backgrounds, extending classical results to compact manifolds with singular potentials and offering tools for stability quantification.

idea_spark.idea: Relax the homogeneity assumption in uncertainty principles to a quantifiable spectral condition, accommodating more general operators (e.g., with singular potentials), and investigate how spectral inhomogeneity affects inequality stability.
idea_spark.risk: Higher-dimensional results rely on pointwise Weyl laws and restriction estimates, which may be limited for more singular manifolds or operators; explicit constants in quantitative relations may be hard to compute.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires control studies for wave propagation in inhomogeneous media, or provides a mathematical foundation for non-uniform sampling in compressed sensing; spectral complexity measures could connect to entropy concepts in information theory.

papers.title: Phase Transitions as the Breakdown of Statistical Indistinguishability
papers.summary: We introduce a novel characterization of phase transitions based on hypothesis testing.   In our formulation, a phase transition is defined as the breakdown of statistical indistinguishability under vanishing parameter perturbations in the thermodynamic limit.   This perspective provides a general, order-parameter-free framework that does not rely on model-specific insights or learning procedures.   We show that conventional approaches, such as those based on the Binder parameter, can be reinterpreted as special cases within this framework.   As a concrete realization, we employ a distribution-free two-sample run test and demonstrate that the critical point of the two-dimensional Ising model is accurately identified without prior knowledge of the order parameter.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于假设检验的相变新表征方法，将相变定义为热力学极限下统计不可区分性在微小参数扰动下的崩溃。
ai.motivation: 传统相变表征通常依赖于序参数等模型特定知识，需要一种更通用、不依赖序参数的普适框架。
ai.method: 采用无分布的双样本游程检验作为具体实现，在二维伊辛模型中验证了该框架的有效性。
ai.result: 无需序参数先验知识，即可准确识别二维伊辛模型的临界点，传统方法（如Binder参数）可视为本框架的特例。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个不依赖模型具体细节的相变分析通用框架，适用于缺乏明确序参数的复杂系统。

idea_spark.idea: 将假设检验的统计工具系统性地应用于相变理论，建立统计推断与物理相变的直接联系。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于热力学极限的渐近性质，在有限尺寸系统中可能需要修正；检验统计量的选择可能影响灵敏度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将其他非参数统计检验（如Kolmogorov-Smirnov检验）应用于不同模型的相变检测，或探索该框架在量子相变中的适用性。

en.tldr: This paper introduces a novel characterization of phase transitions based on hypothesis testing, defining phase transitions as the breakdown of statistical indistinguishability under vanishing parameter perturbations in the thermodynamic limit.
en.motivation: Traditional characterizations of phase transitions often rely on model-specific insights like order parameters, necessitating a more general, order-parameter-free universal framework.
en.method: A distribution-free two-sample run test is employed as a concrete realization, validated on the two-dimensional Ising model.
en.result: The critical point of the 2D Ising model is accurately identified without prior knowledge of the order parameter; conventional approaches (e.g., Binder parameter) can be reinterpreted as special cases within this framework.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general framework for phase transition analysis that does not rely on model-specific details, applicable to complex systems lacking clear order parameters.

idea_spark.idea: Systematically applying hypothesis testing statistical tools to phase transition theory, establishing a direct link between statistical inference and physical phase transitions.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on asymptotic properties in the thermodynamic limit and may require modifications for finite-size systems; choice of test statistic may affect sensitivity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying other nonparametric statistical tests (e.g., Kolmogorov-Smirnov) to phase transition detection in different models, or exploring the framework's applicability to quantum phase transitions.

papers.title: Breakout-picker: Reducing false positives in deep learning-based borehole breakout characterization from acoustic image logs
papers.summary: Borehole breakouts are stress-induced spalling on the borehole wall, which are identifiable in acoustic image logs as paired zones with near-symmetry azimuths, low acoustic amplitudes, and increased borehole radius. Accurate breakout characterization is crucial for in-situ stress analysis. In recent years, deep learning has been introduced to automate the time-consuming and labor-intensive breakout picking process. However, existing approaches often suffer from misclassification of non-breakout features, leading to high false positive rates. To address this limitation, this study develops a deep learning framework, termed Breakout-picker, with a specific focus on reducing false positives in automatic breakout characterization. Breakout-picker reduces false positives through two strategies. First, the training of Breakout-picker incorporates negative samples of non-breakout features, including natural fractures, keyseats, and logging artifacts. They share similar characteristics with breakouts, such as low acoustic amplitude or locally enlarged borehole radius. These negative training samples enables Breakout-picker to better discriminate true breakouts and similar non-breakout features. Second, candidate breakouts identified by Breakout-picker are further validated by azimuthal symmetry criteria, whereby detections that do not exhibit the near-symmetry characteristics of breakout azimuth are excluded. The performance of Breakout-picker is evaluated using three acoustic image log datasets from different regions. The results demonstrate that Breakout-picker outperforms other automatic methods with higher accuracy and substantially lower false positive rates. By reducing false positives, Breakout-picker enhances the reliability of automatic breakout characterization from acoustic image logs, which in turn benefits in-situ stress analysis based on borehole breakouts.

ai.tldr: Breakout-picker 是一个深度学习框架，专注于减少声波成像测井中钻孔崩落自动识别的假阳性。
ai.motivation: 现有深度学习方法在自动识别钻孔崩落时，常将非崩落特征误分类，导致高假阳性率，影响地应力分析的可靠性。
ai.method: 采用两种策略：1) 训练时加入天然裂缝、键槽、测井伪影等负样本，提升模型对相似特征的区分能力；2) 对候选崩落进行方位对称性验证，排除不符合对称特征的结果。
ai.result: 在三个不同区域的声波成像测井数据集上评估，Breakout-picker 相比其他自动方法准确率更高，假阳性率显著降低。
ai.help_to_user: 该框架提高了从声波成像测井中自动识别钻孔崩落的可靠性，从而支持更准确的地应力分析。

idea_spark.idea: 在视觉缺陷检测中，可引入易混淆的负样本进行对抗训练，并结合领域知识（如几何对称性）进行结果过滤。
idea_spark.risk: 方位对称性准则可能不适用于所有地质场景或成像条件，需针对具体应用调整阈值。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将数据驱动模型与领域先验知识结合，是提升专业领域AI系统可靠性的有效途径。

en.tldr: Breakout-picker is a deep learning framework specifically designed to reduce false positives in automatic borehole breakout characterization from acoustic image logs.
en.motivation: Existing deep learning approaches for automatic breakout picking often misclassify non-breakout features, leading to high false positive rates and compromising the reliability of in-situ stress analysis.
en.method: Two strategies are employed: 1) Incorporating negative samples (e.g., natural fractures, keyseats, logging artifacts) during training to improve discrimination; 2) Validating candidate breakouts using azimuthal symmetry criteria to exclude detections lacking near-symmetry characteristics.
en.result: Evaluated on three acoustic image log datasets from different regions, Breakout-picker outperforms other automatic methods with higher accuracy and substantially lower false positive rates.
en.help_to_user: The framework enhances the reliability of automatic breakout characterization, thereby benefiting in-situ stress analysis based on borehole breakouts.

idea_spark.idea: In visual defect detection, incorporate confusing negative samples for adversarial training and filter results using domain knowledge (e.g., geometric symmetry).
idea_spark.risk: Azimuthal symmetry criteria may not hold for all geological settings or imaging conditions, requiring threshold adjustments for specific applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining data-driven models with domain priors is an effective way to improve the reliability of AI systems in specialized fields.

papers.title: Current-Induced Dynamics and Instability Pathways of Skyrmioniums in Chiral Magnets
papers.summary: We present a comprehensive study of current-driven dynamics, transformations, and instabilities of skyrmioniums in chiral magnetic films, considering both isolated objects and collective states forming skyrmionium-based meta-matter. Using micromagnetic simulations combined with an analytical description based on the generalized Thiele equation, we clarify how the internal structure of skyrmioniums governs their nonequilibrium response to electric currents. Despite having zero total topological charge, skyrmioniums exhibit a finite transverse velocity under applied currents. We show that this skyrmionium Hall effect originates from an imbalance between positive and negative topological contributions of the inner skyrmion and surrounding ring, which typically occupy different areas. Current-induced deformations further enhance this imbalance, yielding Hall angles comparable to those of skyrmions. At higher current densities, skyrmioniums undergo distinct instabilities depending on magnetic field and uniaxial anisotropy, including elongation, collapse into a skyrmion, transformation into a topologically trivial droplet, and expansion into stripe textures. We map these regimes in current--field and current--anisotropy phase diagrams and resolve their microscopic pathways via the evolution of topological charge and local rotational measures. Beyond isolated textures, mixed skyrmion--skyrmionium lattices display rich collective dynamics, including elastic transport, polymorphic transitions, soliton exchange, and stripe formation. Pulsed currents provide additional control, enabling access to regimes beyond continuous driving. Our results establish skyrmioniums and their meta-matter as tunable nonequilibrium systems probing the topological energy landscape far from equilibrium.

ai.tldr: 本研究通过微磁模拟和广义Thiele方程，系统揭示了手性磁体中斯格明子环在电流驱动下的动力学、相变和不稳定性路径，包括其霍尔效应、拓扑结构演化及集体动力学行为。
ai.motivation: 斯格明子环作为零净拓扑电荷的磁结构，其非平衡响应机制尚不明确，理解其电流驱动动力学对拓扑自旋电子学应用至关重要。
ai.method: 结合微磁模拟和基于广义Thiele方程的解析描述，分析孤立斯格明子环及斯格明子-斯格明子环混合晶格的动力学行为，绘制电流-磁场/各向异性相图。
ai.result: 发现斯格明子环因内外拓扑贡献面积差异产生有限横向速度（斯格明子环霍尔效应）；在高电流密度下出现伸长、塌缩为斯格明子、转变为拓扑平庸液滴或扩展为条纹相等不稳定性；混合晶格展现弹性输运、多态转变等集体动力学。
ai.help_to_user: 为拓扑磁结构非平衡动力学提供系统框架，揭示零净拓扑电荷体系的输运特性，为基于斯格明子环的元物质设计提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 利用脉冲电流突破连续驱动限制，探索拓扑能量景观的非平衡区域；设计斯格明子环基元物质实现可编程动力学响应。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟结果需实验验证；实际材料中缺陷、温度效应可能改变不稳定性阈值。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对“拓扑中性”结构非平庸响应的研究；为拓扑磁存储器、逻辑器件提供多态操作新思路。

en.tldr: This study comprehensively reveals the current-driven dynamics, phase transformations, and instability pathways of skyrmioniums in chiral magnets via micromagnetic simulations and the generalized Thiele equation, including their Hall effect, topological evolution, and collective dynamics.
en.motivation: The non-equilibrium response mechanisms of skyrmioniums—magnetic textures with zero net topological charge—remain unclear, and understanding their current-driven dynamics is crucial for topological spintronics applications.
en.method: Combined micromagnetic simulations with analytical descriptions based on the generalized Thiele equation to analyze isolated skyrmioniums and skyrmion–skyrmionium lattice dynamics, mapping current–field and current–anisotropy phase diagrams.
en.result: Skyrmioniums exhibit finite transverse velocity (skyrmionium Hall effect) due to area imbalance between positive/negative topological contributions; at high current densities, they undergo elongation, collapse into skyrmions, transformation into trivial droplets, or expansion into stripe phases; mixed lattices show elastic transport, polymorphic transitions, and collective dynamics.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic framework for non-equilibrium dynamics of topological magnetic textures, reveals transport properties of zero-net-topology systems, and offers theoretical guidance for designing skyrmionium-based meta-matter.

idea_spark.idea: Use pulsed currents to access regimes beyond continuous driving and explore non-equilibrium topological energy landscapes; design skyrmionium-based meta-matter for programmable dynamic responses.
idea_spark.risk: Simulation results require experimental validation; defects and temperature effects in real materials may alter instability thresholds.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires study of non-trivial responses in 'topologically neutral' structures; offers new approaches for multi-state operation in topological magnetic memory and logic devices.

papers.title: On the role of the slowest observable in one-dimensional Markov processes to construct quasi-exactly-solvable generators with $N=2$ explicit levels
papers.summary: The construction of Quasi-Exactly-Solvable quantum Hamiltonians where only the two first eigenstates $Φ_0(x)$ and $Φ_1(x)$ of energies $E_0$ and $E_1$ are explicit is revisited from the point of view of one-dimensional Markov processes satisfying detailed-balance, whose generators are related to quantum Hamiltonians via similarity transformations. Here the lowest energy vanishes $E_0=0$ and is associated the conservation of probability and to the steady state $P_*(x)$, while $E_1>0$ is the rate that governs the exponential relaxation towards the steady-state, and is associated to the slowest observable $L_1(x)$ that corresponds to the ratio $ \frac{Φ_1(x) }{Φ_0(x)}$ of the two quantum eigenstates. Our main conclusion is that the Markov perspective leads to interesting re-interpretations and that the construction of quasi-exactly-solvable models with $N=2$ explicit levels is more intuitive and technically simpler if one takes the slowest observable $L_1(x)$ as the central object from which all the other properties can be reconstructed. This general approach is then applied to Fokker-Planck generators in continuous space and to Markov jump generators on the lattice.

ai.tldr: 从一维马尔可夫过程视角，重新审视仅前两个本征态显式的准精确可解哈密顿量构造，强调最慢可观测量L1(x)的中心作用。
ai.motivation: 传统构造准精确可解量子哈密顿量的方法较为复杂，本文旨在通过马尔可夫过程视角提供更直观、技术更简单的构造框架。
ai.method: 利用满足细致平衡的一维马尔可夫过程，其生成元通过相似变换与量子哈密顿量关联，并以最慢可观测量L1(x)为核心重构所有性质。
ai.result: 马尔可夫视角为模型提供了新的物理解释，且基于最慢观测量的构造方法在连续空间Fokker-Planck生成元和格点马尔可夫跳跃生成元中均适用。
ai.help_to_user: 为构造仅有两个显式能级的准精确可解模型提供了更清晰的物理图像和简化技术路径。

idea_spark.idea: 以最慢弛豫模式对应的可观测量作为构造核心，而非直接处理哈密顿量本征态。
idea_spark.risk: 该方法可能主要适用于满足细致平衡的系统，对于更一般的非平衡系统扩展性待验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从弛豫时间尺度分离的角度，重新组织近似可解系统的构造逻辑。

en.tldr: Revisits the construction of quasi-exactly-solvable quantum Hamiltonians with only the first two eigenstates explicit from the perspective of one-dimensional Markov processes, emphasizing the central role of the slowest observable L1(x).
en.motivation: Traditional methods for constructing quasi-exactly-solvable quantum Hamiltonians are complex. This work aims to provide a more intuitive and technically simpler framework via the Markov process perspective.
en.method: Utilizes one-dimensional Markov processes satisfying detailed balance, whose generators are related to quantum Hamiltonians via similarity transformations, and reconstructs all properties from the central object—the slowest observable L1(x).
en.result: The Markov perspective offers novel physical reinterpretations, and the construction method based on the slowest observable is applicable to both Fokker-Planck generators in continuous space and Markov jump generators on a lattice.
en.help_to_user: Provides a clearer physical picture and a simplified technical pathway for constructing quasi-exactly-solvable models with only two explicit levels.

idea_spark.idea: Using the observable corresponding to the slowest relaxation mode as the central construction object, rather than directly handling Hamiltonian eigenstates.
idea_spark.risk: The method may primarily apply to systems satisfying detailed balance; its extensibility to more general non-equilibrium systems requires verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires reorganizing the construction logic for approximately solvable systems from the perspective of relaxation time scale separation.

papers.title: Just Type It in Isabelle! AI Agents Drafting, Mechanizing, and Generalizing from Human Hints
papers.summary: Type annotations are essential when printing terms in a way that preserves their meaning under reparsing and type inference. We study the problem of complete and minimal type annotations for rank-one polymorphic $λ$-calculus terms, as used in Isabelle. Building on prior work by Smolka, Blanchette et al., we give a metatheoretical account of the problem, with a full formal specification and proofs, and formalize it in Isabelle/HOL. Our development is a series of experiments featuring human-driven and AI-driven formalization workflows: a human and an LLM-powered AI agent independently produce pen-and-paper proofs, and the AI agent autoformalizes both in Isabelle, with further human-hinted AI interventions refining and generalizing the development.

ai.tldr: 研究在Isabelle中为秩一多态λ演算项生成完整且最小类型标注的问题，通过人类与AI代理协作进行形式化验证与推广。
ai.motivation: 类型标注对于在重新解析和类型推断下保持项的含义至关重要，需要系统的方法来生成完整且最小的标注。
ai.method: 基于Smolka和Blanchette等人的先前工作，给出问题的元理论描述，包括完整的形式化规范和证明，并在Isabelle/HOL中实现。实验流程包括人类和LLM驱动的AI代理独立生成手写证明，AI代理在Isabelle中自动形式化两者，并通过人类提示的AI干预进一步细化和推广。
ai.result: 开发了一个形式化框架，展示了人类驱动和AI驱动的工作流程，实现了从手写证明到Isabelle代码的自动形式化，并通过协作进行了改进和推广。
ai.help_to_user: 为在定理证明器中处理类型标注提供了系统的方法论和工具支持，展示了AI辅助形式化的潜力。

idea_spark.idea: 构建一个混合倡议系统，其中人类提供高级策略或关键洞察，AI负责执行细节形式化和探索替代方案。
idea_spark.risk: AI对复杂元理论概念的理解可能有限，导致形式化错误或过度泛化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将类似的工作流程应用于其他编程语言或证明助手的类型推断或代码合成问题。

en.tldr: Studies the problem of generating complete and minimal type annotations for rank-one polymorphic λ-calculus terms in Isabelle, through human-AI collaborative formalization and generalization.
en.motivation: Type annotations are essential for preserving term meaning under reparsing and type inference, requiring systematic methods for generating complete and minimal annotations.
en.method: Building on prior work by Smolka and Blanchette et al., provides a metatheoretical account with full formal specification and proofs, implemented in Isabelle/HOL. The experimental workflow features human and LLM-powered AI agents independently producing pen-and-paper proofs, the AI agent autoformalizing both in Isabelle, and further human-hinted AI interventions refining and generalizing the development.
en.result: Developed a formalization framework demonstrating human-driven and AI-driven workflows, achieving autoformalization from pen-and-paper proofs to Isabelle code, with refinement and generalization through collaboration.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic methodology and tool support for handling type annotations in theorem provers, showcasing the potential of AI-assisted formalization.

idea_spark.idea: Build a mixed-initiative system where humans provide high-level strategies or key insights, and AI handles detailed formalization and exploration of alternatives.
idea_spark.risk: AI may have limited understanding of complex metatheoretical concepts, leading to formalization errors or over-generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Apply similar workflows to type inference or code synthesis problems in other programming languages or proof assistants.

papers.title: How to unitarily map between any two pure states with a single closed-form exponential
papers.summary: It is well-known that any two pure quantum states (in the same Hilbert space) can be mapped to any other using unitary transformations. However, previous approaches to this problem required two explicit bases for the Hilbert space, one each for the initial and target states, and thus their complexity necessarily scales with the dimension of the Hilbert space. In this Letter, we show how to utilize novel algebraic methods to construct a closed-form exponential unitary transformation which achieves this in general, using only a single unitary generator. This construction is independent of any bases and agnostic to the dimension of the Hilbert space. We highlight the usefulness of this tool for studying relationships between systems of pure states in quantum information theory, as well in elementary analyses of quantum circuits and unitary operators.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种使用单一封闭形式指数酉变换将任意两个纯量子态相互映射的通用方法，该方法不依赖于希尔伯特空间基的选择和维度。
ai.motivation: 已知任意两个纯量子态可通过酉变换相互映射，但传统方法需要为初态和目标态分别构造显式基，复杂度随希尔伯特空间维度增长。
ai.method: 利用新颖的代数方法，构造仅使用单一酉生成元的封闭形式指数酉变换，实现基无关且维度无关的映射。
ai.result: 成功构建了普适的封闭形式指数酉变换，该变换不依赖于任何基，且对希尔伯特空间维度保持不可知。
ai.help_to_user: 为量子信息理论中纯态系统关系的研究提供了通用工具，并可用于量子电路和酉算子的基础分析。

idea_spark.idea: 利用单一生成元实现任意纯态映射，避免了传统方法中基构造的维度依赖性问题。
idea_spark.risk: 方法可能对混合态或开放量子系统的推广存在限制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对量子态转换中维度无关方法的设计，或应用于量子算法中的态制备步骤优化。

en.tldr: This paper presents a universal method to unitarily map any two pure quantum states using a single closed-form exponential unitary transformation, independent of Hilbert space basis and dimension.
en.motivation: While any two pure quantum states can be mapped via unitary transformations, previous approaches require explicit bases for both initial and target states, with complexity scaling with Hilbert space dimension.
en.method: Utilizes novel algebraic methods to construct a closed-form exponential unitary transformation with only a single unitary generator, achieving basis- and dimension-agnostic mapping.
en.result: Successfully constructs a universal closed-form exponential unitary transformation that is independent of any bases and agnostic to the dimension of the Hilbert space.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general tool for studying relationships between systems of pure states in quantum information theory and for elementary analyses of quantum circuits and unitary operators.

idea_spark.idea: Using a single generator to achieve arbitrary pure-state mapping avoids the dimension-dependent basis construction in traditional approaches.
idea_spark.risk: The method may have limitations when extending to mixed states or open quantum systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the design of dimension-agnostic methods for quantum state conversion, or applications in optimizing state preparation steps in quantum algorithms.

papers.title: CollideNet: Hierarchical Multi-scale Video Representation Learning with Disentanglement for Time-To-Collision Forecasting
papers.summary: Time-to-Collision (TTC) forecasting is a critical task in collision prevention, requiring precise temporal prediction and comprehending both local and global patterns encapsulated in a video, both spatially and temporally. To address the multi-scale nature of video, we introduce a novel spatiotemporal hierarchical transformer-based architecture called CollideNet, specifically catered for effective TTC forecasting. In the spatial stream, CollideNet aggregates information for each video frame simultaneously at multiple resolutions. In the temporal stream, along with multi-scale feature encoding, CollideNet also disentangles the non-stationarity, trend, and seasonality components. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in comparison to prior works on three commonly used public datasets, setting a new state-of-the-art by a considerable margin. We conduct cross-dataset evaluations to analyze the generalization capabilities of our method, and visualize the effects of disentanglement of the trend and seasonality components of the video data. We release our code at https://github.com/DeSinister/CollideNet/.

ai.tldr: CollideNet是一种用于碰撞时间预测的新型时空分层Transformer架构，通过多尺度特征编码和趋势/季节性解耦实现最先进性能。
ai.motivation: 碰撞时间预测需要精确的时间预测和对视频中局部与全局时空模式的理解，现有方法难以有效处理视频的多尺度特性。
ai.method: 提出CollideNet：空间流中同时聚合多分辨率帧信息；时间流中进行多尺度特征编码，并解耦视频数据的非平稳性、趋势和季节性成分。
ai.result: 在三个常用公共数据集上达到最先进性能，大幅超越先前工作，并通过跨数据集评估验证了泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 为视频时序预测任务提供了有效的多尺度表示学习框架，特别适用于需要精确时间预测的自动驾驶等场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将时间序列分解思想（趋势/季节性）引入视频表征学习，增强模型对时序模式的理解。
idea_spark.risk: 解耦组件的可解释性需要进一步验证，在极端光照或遮挡场景下性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将信号处理中的时序分析方法与深度学习结合，用于提升视频时序任务的建模能力。

en.tldr: CollideNet is a novel spatiotemporal hierarchical transformer architecture for Time-to-Collision forecasting, achieving state-of-the-art performance through multi-scale feature encoding and trend/seasonality disentanglement.
en.motivation: TTC forecasting requires precise temporal prediction and understanding of both local and global spatiotemporal patterns in videos, with existing methods struggling to handle the multi-scale nature effectively.
en.method: Proposes CollideNet: spatial stream aggregates information at multiple resolutions per frame; temporal stream performs multi-scale feature encoding while disentangling non-stationarity, trend, and seasonality components.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on three public datasets by considerable margins, with cross-dataset evaluations demonstrating generalization capabilities.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective multi-scale representation learning framework for video temporal forecasting tasks, particularly suitable for autonomous systems requiring precise temporal predictions.

idea_spark.idea: Introducing time series decomposition concepts (trend/seasonality) into video representation learning to enhance temporal pattern understanding.
idea_spark.risk: Interpretability of disentangled components requires further validation; performance may degrade under extreme lighting or occlusion scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining temporal analysis methods from signal processing with deep learning to improve modeling for video temporal tasks.

papers.title: APC: Transferable and Efficient Adversarial Point Counterattack for Robust 3D Point Cloud Recognition
papers.summary: The advent of deep neural networks has led to remarkable progress in 3D point cloud recognition, but they remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks. Although various defense methods have been studied, they suffer from a trade-off between robustness and transferability. We propose Adversarial Point Counterattack (APC) to achieve both simultaneously. APC is a lightweight input-level purification module that generates instance-specific counter-perturbations for each point, effectively neutralizing attacks. Leveraging clean-adversarial pairs, APC enforces geometric consistency in data space and semantic consistency in feature space. To improve generalizability across diverse attacks, we adopt a hybrid training strategy using adversarial point clouds from multiple attack types. Since APC operates purely on input point clouds, it directly transfers to unseen models and defends against attacks targeting them without retraining. At inference, a single APC forward pass provides purified point clouds with negligible time and parameter overhead. Extensive experiments on two 3D recognition benchmarks demonstrate that the APC achieves state-of-the-art defense performance. Furthermore, cross-model evaluations validate its superior transferability. The code is available at https://github.com/gyjung975/APC.

ai.tldr: APC是一种轻量级输入级净化模块，通过生成实例特定的对抗性反扰动来中和攻击，实现3D点云识别的鲁棒防御与跨模型可迁移性。
ai.motivation: 现有3D点云识别防御方法在鲁棒性和可迁移性之间存在权衡，难以同时兼顾。
ai.method: 利用干净-对抗点云对，在数据空间强制几何一致性，在特征空间强制语义一致性；采用混合训练策略，使用多种攻击类型的对抗点云提升泛化性。
ai.result: 在两个3D识别基准测试中达到最先进的防御性能，跨模型评估验证了其卓越的可迁移性。
ai.help_to_user: 为3D点云模型提供了一种无需重新训练即可直接迁移到未见模型的高效防御方案，推理时仅需单次前向传播，时间和参数开销可忽略。

idea_spark.idea: 通过生成针对每个点的实例特定反扰动来主动中和攻击，而非被动过滤或检测。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于干净-对抗样本对进行训练，若训练数据攻击类型覆盖不足可能影响对未知攻击的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将对抗防御视为一个动态的‘反攻击’过程，通过强制多空间一致性来提升模型的内在鲁棒性。

zh.tldr: APC是一种轻量级输入级净化模块，通过生成实例特定的对抗性反扰动来中和攻击，实现3D点云识别的鲁棒防御与跨模型可迁移性。

idea_spark.idea: 通过生成针对每个点的实例特定反扰动来主动中和攻击，而非被动过滤或检测。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于干净-对抗样本对进行训练，若训练数据攻击类型覆盖不足可能影响对未知攻击的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将对抗防御视为一个动态的‘反攻击’过程，通过强制多空间一致性来提升模型的内在鲁棒性。

en.tldr: APC is a lightweight input-level purification module that neutralizes attacks by generating instance-specific counter-perturbations, achieving robust defense and cross-model transferability for 3D point cloud recognition.
en.motivation: Existing defense methods for 3D point cloud recognition suffer from a trade-off between robustness and transferability.
en.method: Enforces geometric consistency in data space and semantic consistency in feature space using clean-adversarial pairs; employs a hybrid training strategy with adversarial point clouds from multiple attack types to improve generalizability.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art defense performance on two 3D recognition benchmarks, with cross-model evaluations validating superior transferability.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient defense solution that directly transfers to unseen models without retraining, requiring only a single forward pass at inference with negligible overhead.

idea_spark.idea: Actively neutralizes attacks by generating instance-specific counter-perturbations per point rather than passively filtering or detecting.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on clean-adversarial pairs for training; insufficient coverage of attack types in training data may limit generalization to novel attacks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Frames adversarial defense as a dynamic 'counterattack' process, enhancing intrinsic robustness through enforced multi-space consistency.

papers.title: MOMENTA: Mixture-of-Experts Over Multimodal Embeddings with Neural Temporal Aggregation for Misinformation Detection
papers.summary: The widespread dissemination of multimodal content on social media has made misinformation detection increasingly challenging, as misleading narratives often arise not only from textual or visual content alone, but also from semantic inconsistencies between modalities and their evolution over time. Existing multimodal misinformation detection methods typically model cross-modal interactions statically and often show limited robustness across heterogeneous datasets, domains, and narrative settings. To address these challenges, we propose MOMENTA, a unified framework for multimodal misinformation detection that captures modality heterogeneity, cross-modal inconsistency, temporal dynamics, and cross-domain generalization within a single architecture. MOMENTA employs modality-specific mixture-of-experts modules to model diverse misinformation patterns, bidirectional co-attention to align textual and visual representations in a shared semantic space, and a discrepancy-aware branch to explicitly capture semantic disagreement between modalities. To model narrative evolution, we introduce an attention-based temporal aggregation mechanism with drift and momentum encoding over overlapping time windows, enabling the framework to capture both short-term fluctuations and longer-term trends in misinformation propagation. In addition, domain-adversarial learning and a prototype memory bank improve domain invariance and stabilize representation learning across datasets. The model is trained using a multi-objective optimization strategy that jointly enforces classification performance, cross-modal alignment, contrastive learning, temporal consistency, and domain robustness. Experiments on Fakeddit, MMCoVaR, Weibo, and XFacta show that MOMENTA achieves strong, consistent results across accuracy, F1-score, AUC, and MCC, highlighting its effectiveness for multimodal misinformation detection.

ai.tldr: MOMENTA是一个用于多模态虚假信息检测的统一框架，通过专家混合模块、双向协同注意力、差异感知分支和时间聚合机制，有效捕捉模态异质性、跨模态不一致性、时间动态和跨域泛化能力。
ai.motivation: 社交媒体上多模态内容的广泛传播使虚假信息检测日益困难，误导性叙述不仅来自文本或视觉内容本身，还源于模态间的语义不一致及其随时间演变。现有方法通常静态建模跨模态交互，在不同数据集、领域和叙述设置中鲁棒性有限。
ai.method: 采用模态特定的专家混合模块建模多样化虚假信息模式；使用双向协同注意力在共享语义空间中对齐文本和视觉表示；通过差异感知分支显式捕捉模态间语义分歧；引入基于注意力的时间聚合机制，结合漂移和动量编码，捕捉短期波动和长期趋势；利用域对抗学习和原型记忆库提升域不变性和表示稳定性；采用多目标优化策略联合优化分类性能、跨模态对齐、对比学习、时间一致性和域鲁棒性。
ai.result: 在Fakeddit、MMCoVaR、Weibo和XFacta数据集上的实验表明，MOMENTA在准确率、F1分数、AUC和MCC指标上均取得强劲且一致的结果，验证了其多模态虚假信息检测的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 该框架为研究人员提供了处理多模态虚假信息检测中模态异质性、时间演变和跨域泛化挑战的系统方法，其模块化设计便于适应不同社交媒体平台和内容类型。

idea_spark.idea: 将动量编码思想与注意力机制结合，用于建模信息传播中的长期趋势；利用原型记忆库稳定跨数据集的表示学习。
idea_spark.risk: 模型复杂度较高，需要大量多模态时序数据进行训练；对模态对齐质量敏感，低质量跨模态数据可能影响性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将动态时间建模与跨模态不一致性检测相结合，用于更细粒度的虚假信息演变分析；可探索轻量化版本以适应实时检测场景。

en.tldr: MOMENTA is a unified framework for multimodal misinformation detection that captures modality heterogeneity, cross-modal inconsistency, temporal dynamics, and cross-domain generalization via mixture-of-experts modules, bidirectional co-attention, discrepancy-aware branches, and temporal aggregation mechanisms.
en.motivation: The widespread dissemination of multimodal content on social media makes misinformation detection increasingly challenging, as misleading narratives arise not only from unimodal content but also from semantic inconsistencies between modalities and their evolution over time. Existing methods typically model cross-modal interactions statically and show limited robustness across heterogeneous datasets, domains, and narrative settings.
en.method: Employs modality-specific mixture-of-experts modules to model diverse misinformation patterns; uses bidirectional co-attention to align textual and visual representations in a shared semantic space; incorporates a discrepancy-aware branch to explicitly capture semantic disagreement between modalities; introduces an attention-based temporal aggregation mechanism with drift and momentum encoding over overlapping time windows to capture short-term fluctuations and longer-term trends; utilizes domain-adversarial learning and a prototype memory bank to improve domain invariance and stabilize representation learning; trains the model with a multi-objective optimization strategy jointly enforcing classification performance, cross-modal alignment, contrastive learning, temporal consistency, and domain robustness.
en.result: Experiments on Fakeddit, MMCoVaR, Weibo, and XFacta datasets demonstrate that MOMENTA achieves strong, consistent results across accuracy, F1-score, AUC, and MCC metrics, highlighting its effectiveness for multimodal misinformation detection.
en.help_to_user: The framework provides researchers with a systematic approach to address challenges of modality heterogeneity, temporal evolution, and cross-domain generalization in multimodal misinformation detection, with modular design facilitating adaptation to different social media platforms and content types.

idea_spark.idea: Combining momentum encoding with attention mechanisms to model long-term trends in information propagation; utilizing a prototype memory bank to stabilize representation learning across datasets.
idea_spark.risk: High model complexity requires substantial multimodal temporal data for training; sensitivity to modality alignment quality may affect performance with low-quality cross-modal data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires integrating dynamic temporal modeling with cross-modal inconsistency detection for finer-grained misinformation evolution analysis; lightweight versions could be explored for real-time detection scenarios.

papers.title: SSFT: A Lightweight Spectral-Spatial Fusion Transformer for Generic Hyperspectral Classification
papers.summary: Hyperspectral imaging enables fine-grained recognition of materials by capturing rich spectral signatures, but learning robust classifiers is challenging due to high dimensionality, spectral redundancy, limited labeled data, and strong domain shifts. Beyond earth observation, labeled HSI data is often scarce and imbalanced, motivating compact models for generic hyperspectral classification across diverse acquisition regimes. We propose the lightweight Spectral-Spatial Fusion Transformer (SSFT), which factorizes representation learning into spectral and spatial pathways and integrates them via cross-attention to capture complementary wavelength-dependent and structural information. We evaluate our SSFT on the challenging HSI-Benchmark, a heterogeneous multi-dataset benchmark covering earth observation, fruit condition assessment, and fine-grained material recognition. SSFT achieves state-of-the-art overall performance, ranking first while using less than 2% of the parameters of the previous leading method. We further evaluate transfer to the substantially larger SpectralEarth benchmark under the official protocol, where SSFT remains competitive despite its compact size. Ablation studies show that both spectral and spatial pathways are crucial, with spatial modeling contributing most, and that SSFT remains robust without data augmentation.

ai.tldr: SSFT是一种轻量级光谱-空间融合Transformer，用于通用高光谱分类，在异构数据集上实现SOTA性能，参数量不到先前领先方法的2%。
ai.motivation: 高光谱成像数据维度高、光谱冗余、标记数据有限且存在强域偏移，尤其在非地球观测领域标记数据稀缺且不平衡，需要紧凑的通用分类模型。
ai.method: 将表示学习分解为光谱和空间两条路径，通过交叉注意力整合它们，以捕获互补的波长依赖信息和结构信息。
ai.result: 在异构多数据集基准HSI-Benchmark上取得总体SOTA性能，排名第一；在更大的SpectralEarth基准上迁移评估中保持竞争力；消融研究表明空间建模贡献最大，且无需数据增强仍保持鲁棒性。
ai.help_to_user: 为高光谱分类任务提供了一个参数量极低（<2%）、性能优越的轻量级Transformer架构，特别适用于标记数据稀缺、需要跨域泛化的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将分解表示与轻量级Transformer结合，用于其他高维、多模态或数据稀缺的分类任务（如多光谱成像、医学影像、传感器融合）。
idea_spark.risk: 在特定领域（如非地球观测）的极端数据分布或噪声条件下，轻量级设计的泛化能力仍需验证；交叉注意力的计算开销在嵌入式设备上可能仍是瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何为其他“高维但数据稀缺”的感知任务（如遥感、材料科学、生物医学成像）设计专用的轻量级融合架构，平衡性能与效率。

en.motivation: Hyperspectral data poses challenges due to high dimensionality, spectral redundancy, limited labeled data, and strong domain shifts, especially with scarce and imbalanced labels beyond earth observation, motivating compact generic models.
en.method: Factorizes representation learning into spectral and spatial pathways and integrates them via cross-attention to capture complementary wavelength-dependent and structural information.
en.result: Achieves top overall performance on the heterogeneous multi-dataset HSI-Benchmark; remains competitive when transferred to the larger SpectralEarth benchmark; ablation shows spatial modeling contributes most and robustness without data augmentation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a highly parameter-efficient (<2%) lightweight Transformer architecture for hyperspectral classification, particularly suitable for label-scarce scenarios requiring cross-domain generalization.

idea_spark.idea: Combine factorized representation with lightweight Transformers for other high-dimensional, multimodal, or data-scarce classification tasks (e.g., multispectral imaging, medical imaging, sensor fusion).
idea_spark.risk: Generalization of lightweight design under extreme data distributions or noise in specific domains (e.g., non-earth-observation) needs further validation; computational cost of cross-attention may remain a bottleneck on embedded devices.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking about designing dedicated lightweight fusion architectures for other 'high-dimensional but data-scarce' perception tasks (e.g., remote sensing, materials science, biomedical imaging), balancing performance and efficiency.

papers.title: The World Leaks the Future: Harness Evolution for Future Prediction Agents
papers.summary: Many consequential decisions must be made before the relevant outcome is known. Such problems are commonly framed as \emph{future prediction}, where an LLM agent must form a prediction for an unresolved question using only the public information available at the prediction time. The setting is difficult because public evidence evolves while useful supervision arrives only after the question is resolved, so most existing approaches still improve mainly from final outcomes. Yet final outcomes are too coarse to guide earlier factor tracking, evidence gathering and interpretation, or uncertainty handling. When the same unresolved question is revisited over time, temporal contrasts between earlier and later predictions can expose omissions in the earlier prediction process; we call this signal \emph{internal feedback}. We introduce \emph{Milkyway}, a self-evolving agent system that keeps the base model fixed and instead updates a persistent \emph{future prediction harness} for factor tracking, evidence gathering and interpretation, and uncertainty handling. Across repeated predictions on the same unresolved question, \emph{Milkyway} extracts internal feedback and writes reusable guidance back into the harness, so later predictions on that question can improve before the outcome is known. After the question is resolved, the final outcome provides a \emph{retrospective check} before the updated harness is carried forward to subsequent questions. On FutureX and FutureWorld, Milkyway achieves the best overall score among the compared methods, improving FutureX from 44.07 to 60.90 and FutureWorld from 62.22 to 77.96.

ai.tldr: Milkyway是一个自进化的未来预测代理系统，它通过内部反馈和持久化预测工具链，在问题解决前持续改进预测过程。
ai.motivation: 许多重要决策必须在结果未知时做出。现有方法主要依赖最终结果进行改进，但最终结果过于粗糙，无法指导早期的因素追踪、证据收集和不确定性处理。
ai.method: 系统保持基础模型固定，更新一个持久的未来预测工具链。通过对同一未决问题的重复预测，提取早期与后期预测之间的时间对比作为内部反馈，并将可重用指导写回工具链。问题解决后，最终结果提供回顾性检查，更新后的工具链被用于后续问题。
ai.result: 在FutureX和FutureWorld数据集上，Milkyway取得了最佳总体分数，分别将FutureX从44.07提升至60.90，FutureWorld从62.22提升至77.96。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要在信息不完全时做出预测或决策的用户提供了一个框架，通过利用预测过程中的时间动态进行自我改进，减少对最终监督信号的依赖。

idea_spark.idea: 利用同一问题随时间演变的预测差异作为训练信号，而非仅等待最终结果。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于问题被重复预测的场景；内部反馈可能包含噪声或偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将预测过程本身视为一个可迭代优化的对象，而不仅仅是优化单次预测的输出。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖于问题被重复预测的场景；内部反馈可能包含噪声或偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将预测过程本身视为一个可迭代优化的对象，而不仅仅是优化单次预测的输出。

en.tldr: Milkyway is a self-evolving future prediction agent system that continuously improves the prediction process before question resolution via internal feedback and a persistent prediction harness.
en.motivation: Many consequential decisions must be made before the outcome is known. Existing approaches improve mainly from final outcomes, which are too coarse to guide earlier factor tracking, evidence gathering, and uncertainty handling.
en.method: The system keeps the base model fixed and updates a persistent future prediction harness. Across repeated predictions on the same unresolved question, it extracts internal feedback from temporal contrasts between earlier and later predictions, writing reusable guidance back into the harness. After resolution, the final outcome provides a retrospective check before the updated harness is carried forward.
en.result: On FutureX and FutureWorld, Milkyway achieves the best overall score, improving FutureX from 44.07 to 60.90 and FutureWorld from 62.22 to 77.96.
en.help_to_user: Provides a framework for users who need to make predictions or decisions under partial information, enabling self-improvement by leveraging temporal dynamics in the prediction process, reducing reliance on final supervision.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize the evolving prediction differences over time for the same question as a training signal, instead of waiting solely for the final outcome.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on scenarios where questions are predicted repeatedly; internal feedback may contain noise or bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Treat the prediction process itself as an iteratively optimizable object, rather than just optimizing the output of a single prediction.

papers.title: QuantSightBench: Evaluating LLM Quantitative Forecasting with Prediction Intervals
papers.summary: Forecasting has become a natural benchmark for reasoning under uncertainty. Yet existing evaluations of large language models remain limited to judgmental tasks in simple formats, such as binary or multiple-choice questions. In practice, however, forecasting spans a far broader scope. Across domains such as economics, public health, and social demographics, decisions hinge on numerical estimates over continuous quantities, a capability that current benchmarks do not capture. Evaluating such estimates requires a format that makes uncertainty explicit and testable. We propose prediction intervals as a natural and rigorous interface for this purpose. They demand scale awareness, internal consistency across confidence levels, and calibration over a continuum of outcomes, making them a more suitable evaluation format than point estimates for numerical forecasting. To assess this capability, we introduce a new benchmark QuantSightBench, and evaluate frontier models under multiple settings, assessing both empirical coverage and interval sharpness. Our results show that none of the 11 evaluated frontier and open-weight models achieves the 90\% coverage target, with the top performers Gemini 3.1 Pro (79.1\%), Grok 4 (76.4\%), and GPT-5.4 (75.3\%) all falling at least 10 percentage points short. Calibration degrades sharply at extreme magnitudes, revealing systematic overconfidence across all evaluated models.

ai.tldr: 提出QuantSightBench基准，使用预测区间评估大语言模型的数值预测能力，发现前沿模型均未达到90%覆盖率目标，存在系统性过度自信。
ai.motivation: 现有大语言模型评估局限于简单格式的判断任务，而实际决策需要连续数值估计，需能明确测试不确定性的评估格式。
ai.method: 提出预测区间作为评估接口，要求尺度感知、置信度内部一致性和连续结果校准。构建QuantSightBench基准，在多种设置下评估11个前沿和开源模型，检验经验覆盖率和区间锐度。
ai.result: 所有11个模型均未达到90%覆盖率目标，最佳模型Gemini 3.1 Pro（79.1%）、Grok 4（76.4%）和GPT-5.4（75.3%）均相差至少10个百分点。极端量级下校准急剧恶化，所有模型均表现出系统性过度自信。
ai.help_to_user: 为评估大语言模型数值预测能力提供标准化基准和严谨方法，揭示模型在不确定性量化方面的系统性缺陷。

idea_spark.idea: 开发针对极端值预测的校准增强技术，或设计多尺度不确定性感知的训练目标。
idea_spark.risk: 模型过度自信可能导致实际决策风险，需谨慎对待模型提供的数值区间。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发更细粒度的不确定性评估方法，推动模型在连续数值预测领域的可靠性研究。

zh.result: 所有11个模型均未达到90%覆盖率目标，最佳模型Gemini 3.1 Pro（79.1%）、Grok 4（76.4%）和GPT-5.4（75.3%）均相差至少10个百分点。极端量级下校准急剧恶化，所有模型均表现出系统性过度自信。

idea_spark.risk: 模型过度自信可能导致实际决策风险，需谨慎对待模型提供的数值区间。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发更细粒度的不确定性评估方法，推动模型在连续数值预测领域的可靠性研究。

en.tldr: Proposes QuantSightBench benchmark using prediction intervals to evaluate LLMs' numerical forecasting, finding frontier models all fail to meet 90% coverage target with systematic overconfidence.
en.motivation: Existing LLM evaluations are limited to simple-format judgmental tasks, while real-world decisions require continuous numerical estimates, necessitating an evaluation format that makes uncertainty explicit and testable.
en.method: Proposes prediction intervals as evaluation interface requiring scale awareness, internal consistency across confidence levels, and calibration over continuous outcomes. Introduces QuantSightBench benchmark, evaluating 11 frontier and open-weight models under multiple settings, assessing empirical coverage and interval sharpness.
en.result: None of the 11 evaluated models achieves the 90% coverage target, with top performers Gemini 3.1 Pro (79.1%), Grok 4 (76.4%), and GPT-5.4 (75.3%) all falling at least 10 percentage points short. Calibration degrades sharply at extreme magnitudes, revealing systematic overconfidence across all models.
en.help_to_user: Provides standardized benchmark and rigorous methodology for evaluating LLMs' numerical forecasting capability, revealing systematic deficiencies in uncertainty quantification.

idea_spark.idea: Develop calibration enhancement techniques for extreme-value predictions, or design multi-scale uncertainty-aware training objectives.
idea_spark.risk: Model overconfidence may lead to real-world decision risks, requiring caution when interpreting model-provided numerical intervals.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires more granular uncertainty evaluation methods, advancing research on model reliability in continuous numerical forecasting domains.

papers.title: GaussianFlow SLAM: Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM Guided by GaussianFlow
papers.summary: Gaussian splatting has recently gained traction as a compelling map representation for SLAM systems, enabling dense and photo-realistic scene modeling. However, its application to monocular SLAM remains challenging due to the lack of reliable geometric cues from monocular input. Without geometric supervision, mapping or tracking could fall in local-minima, resulting in structural degeneracies and inaccuracies. To address this challenge, we propose GaussianFlow SLAM, a monocular 3DGS-SLAM that leverages optical flow as a geometry-aware cue to guide the optimization of both the scene structure and camera poses. By encouraging the projected motion of Gaussians, termed GaussianFlow, to align with the optical flow, our method introduces consistent structural cues to regularize both map reconstruction and pose estimation. Furthermore, we introduce normalized error-based densification and pruning modules to refine inactive and unstable Gaussians, thereby contributing to improved map quality and pose accuracy. Experiments conducted on public datasets demonstrate that our method achieves superior rendering quality and tracking accuracy compared with state-of-the-art algorithms. The source code is available at: https://github.com/url-kaist/gaussianflow-slam.

ai.tldr: 提出GaussianFlow SLAM，一种单目3D高斯溅射SLAM系统，利用光流作为几何感知线索来指导场景结构和相机位姿的优化。
ai.motivation: 高斯溅射作为SLAM的地图表示具有吸引力，但应用于单目SLAM时，由于缺乏可靠的几何线索，容易陷入局部最优，导致结构退化和不准确。
ai.method: 通过鼓励高斯投影运动（称为GaussianFlow）与光流对齐，为地图重建和位姿估计引入一致的结构线索。此外，引入基于归一化误差的致密化和剪枝模块来优化非活跃和不稳定的高斯。
ai.result: 在公开数据集上的实验表明，该方法在渲染质量和跟踪精度方面优于现有先进算法。
ai.help_to_user: 为单目SLAM系统提供了一种利用光流增强几何一致性的新方法，改善了基于高斯溅射的地图表示在缺乏深度信息时的鲁棒性和精度。

idea_spark.idea: 利用2D光流场监督3D高斯属性的优化，建立2D运动与3D结构间的桥梁。
idea_spark.risk: 光流估计的准确性直接影响系统性能；在纹理缺失或运动模糊的场景中可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将其他2D视觉线索（如语义分割、边缘检测）作为弱监督信号来约束3D表示的学习。

idea_spark.idea: 利用2D光流场监督3D高斯属性的优化，建立2D运动与3D结构间的桥梁。
idea_spark.risk: 光流估计的准确性直接影响系统性能；在纹理缺失或运动模糊的场景中可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将其他2D视觉线索（如语义分割、边缘检测）作为弱监督信号来约束3D表示的学习。

en.tldr: Proposes GaussianFlow SLAM, a monocular 3D Gaussian Splatting SLAM system that leverages optical flow as a geometry-aware cue to guide the optimization of both scene structure and camera poses.
en.motivation: Gaussian splatting is an attractive map representation for SLAM, but its application to monocular SLAM remains challenging due to the lack of reliable geometric cues, leading to local minima, structural degeneracies, and inaccuracies.
en.method: Introduces consistent structural cues by encouraging the projected motion of Gaussians (termed GaussianFlow) to align with optical flow, regularizing both map reconstruction and pose estimation. Additionally, employs normalized error-based densification and pruning modules to refine inactive and unstable Gaussians.
en.result: Experiments on public datasets demonstrate superior rendering quality and tracking accuracy compared to state-of-the-art algorithms.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel approach for monocular SLAM that enhances geometric consistency using optical flow, improving the robustness and accuracy of Gaussian splatting-based map representations when depth information is unavailable.

idea_spark.idea: Supervising the optimization of 3D Gaussian properties using 2D optical flow fields, bridging 2D motion and 3D structure.
idea_spark.risk: System performance heavily depends on the accuracy of optical flow estimation; may fail in textureless regions or under motion blur.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires consideration of how other 2D visual cues (e.g., semantic segmentation, edge detection) could serve as weak supervision signals to constrain 3D representation learning.

papers.title: SSMamba: A Self-Supervised Hybrid State Space Model for Pathological Image Classification
papers.summary: Pathological diagnosis is highly reliant on image analysis, where Regions of Interest (ROIs) serve as the primary basis for diagnostic evidence, while whole-slide image (WSI)-level tasks primarily capture aggregated patterns. To extract these critical morphological features, ROI-level Foundation Models (FMs) based on Vision Transformers (ViTs) and large-scale self-supervised learning (SSL) have been widely adopted. However, three core limitations remain in their application to ROI analysis: (1) cross-magnification domain shift, as fixed-scale pretraining hinders adaptation to diverse clinical settings; (2) inadequate local-global relationship modeling, wherein the ViT backbone of FMs suffers from high computational overhead and imprecise local characterization; (3) insufficient fine-grained sensitivity, as traditional self-attention mechanisms tend to overlook subtle diagnostic cues. To address these challenges, we propose SSMamba, a hybrid SSL framework that enables effective fine-grained feature learning without relying on large external datasets. This framework incorporates three domain-adaptive components: Mamba Masked Image Modeling (MAMIM) for mitigating domain shift, a Directional Multi-scale (DMS) module for balanced local-global modeling, and a Local Perception Residual (LPR) module for enhanced fine-grained sensitivity. Employing a two-stage pipeline, SSL pretraining on target ROI datasets followed by supervised fine-tuning (SFT), SSMamba outperforms 11 state-of-the-art (SOTA) pathological FMs on 10 public ROI datasets and surpasses 8 SOTA methods on 6 public WSI datasets. These results validate the superiority of task-specific architectural designs for pathological image analysis.

ai.tldr: SSMamba是一个自监督混合状态空间模型，用于病理图像分类，通过领域自适应组件解决ViT基础模型在ROI分析中的三大局限，在多个公开数据集上超越现有方法。
ai.motivation: 基于ViT的ROI级基础模型在病理图像分析中存在三大核心局限：跨放大倍率的领域偏移、局部-全局关系建模不足、对细微诊断线索的敏感性不够。
ai.method: 提出SSMamba混合自监督框架，包含三个领域自适应组件：MAMIM缓解领域偏移，DMS模块平衡局部-全局建模，LPR模块增强细粒度敏感性。采用两阶段流程：先在目标ROI数据集上自监督预训练，再进行监督微调。
ai.result: 在10个公开ROI数据集上超越11个SOTA病理基础模型，在6个公开WSI数据集上超越8个SOTA方法，验证了针对病理图像分析的特定任务架构设计的优越性。
ai.help_to_user: 为病理图像分类任务提供了一个不依赖大型外部数据集、能有效学习细粒度特征的混合自监督框架，特别适用于ROI级分析。

idea_spark.idea: 将Mamba架构与自监督学习结合，针对医学图像的多尺度、细粒度特性设计专用模块，可迁移到其他需要高分辨率细粒度分析的视觉任务。
idea_spark.risk: 对特定病理图像数据集的依赖可能影响泛化能力；混合架构可能增加实现和调优复杂度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将序列建模新架构（如Mamba）与自监督学习范式结合，针对领域特定挑战（如医学图像中的尺度变化、细微特征）设计轻量高效组件，减少对大规模预训练数据的依赖。
