en.tldr: SSMamba is a self-supervised hybrid state space model for pathological image classification. It addresses three core limitations of ViT-based foundation models in ROI analysis through domain-adaptive components, outperforming existing methods on multiple public datasets.
en.motivation: ViT-based ROI-level foundation models for pathological image analysis suffer from three core limitations: cross-magnification domain shift, inadequate local-global relationship modeling, and insufficient fine-grained sensitivity to subtle diagnostic cues.
en.method: Proposes SSMamba, a hybrid SSL framework with three domain-adaptive components: MAMIM for domain shift mitigation, a DMS module for balanced local-global modeling, and an LPR module for enhanced fine-grained sensitivity. Employs a two-stage pipeline: SSL pretraining on target ROI datasets followed by supervised fine-tuning.
en.result: Outperforms 11 SOTA pathological foundation models on 10 public ROI datasets and surpasses 8 SOTA methods on 6 public WSI datasets, validating the superiority of task-specific architectural designs for pathological image analysis.
en.help_to_user: Provides a hybrid self-supervised framework for pathological image classification that enables effective fine-grained feature learning without relying on large external datasets, particularly suitable for ROI-level analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Mamba architecture with self-supervised learning and designing specialized modules for multi-scale, fine-grained characteristics of medical images. Transferable to other visual tasks requiring high-resolution fine-grained analysis.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on specific pathological image datasets may affect generalization; hybrid architecture could increase implementation and tuning complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires integrating novel sequence modeling architectures (e.g., Mamba) with self-supervised learning paradigms, designing lightweight and efficient components for domain-specific challenges (e.g., scale variation, subtle features in medical images), reducing reliance on large-scale pretraining data.

papers.title: Batalin-Vilkovisky quantization with an angular twist
papers.summary: We construct cubic scalar field theory on $λ$-Minkowski space by combining the Batalin-Vilkovisky formalism with harmonic analysis, and produce two inequivalent noncommutative quantum field theories. The braided theory is based on a braided $L_\infty$-algebra whereby covariance dictates a spectral decomposition into cylindrical Bessel functions that diagonalise the angular Drinfel'd twist; in this theory we find the usual logarithmic ultraviolet divergences and confirm the absence of UV/IR mixing. The standard noncommutative theory is based on a classical $L_\infty$-algebra; in this theory we relate the spectral decompositions into plane wave and cylindrical harmonic eigenmodes of the Klein-Gordan operator, we verify the planar equivalence theorem, and we demonstrate a periodic form of UV/IR mixing in which non-planar correlators are generically ultraviolet finite but become non-analytic on an infinite lattice of exceptional momenta.

ai.tldr: 在λ-Minkowski空间上结合Batalin-Vilkovisky形式体系与调和分析，构建了两种不等价的非对易量子场论：辫子理论和标准非对易理论。
ai.motivation: 在非对易几何框架下建立一致的量子场论，特别是探索λ-Minkowski空间上的场论构造。
ai.method: 结合Batalin-Vilkovisky形式体系与调和分析，分别基于辫子L∞-代数和经典L∞-代数构建理论，使用角Drinfel'd扭曲和柱面贝塞尔函数谱分解。
ai.result: 辫子理论显示通常的对数紫外发散且无UV/IR混合；标准理论显示周期性UV/IR混合，非平面关联函数紫外有限但在例外动量格点上非解析。
ai.help_to_user: 为在扭曲非对易空间上构建量子场论提供了具体数学框架和两种实现路径。

idea_spark.idea: 利用角扭曲的谱分解性质控制量子修正行为。
idea_spark.risk: 理论构造高度依赖特定代数结构，物理可观测性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 不同代数结构（辫子vs经典）导致截然不同的UV/IR混合行为，暗示代数对称性在量子引力效应中的核心作用。

en.tldr: Constructs cubic scalar field theory on λ-Minkowski space by combining the Batalin-Vilkovisky formalism with harmonic analysis, yielding two inequivalent noncommutative quantum field theories.
en.motivation: To establish consistent quantum field theories in noncommutative geometric frameworks, specifically on λ-Minkowski space.
en.method: Combine Batalin-Vilkovisky formalism with harmonic analysis; construct theories based on braided L∞-algebra and classical L∞-algebra respectively, using angular Drinfel'd twist and spectral decompositions into cylindrical Bessel functions.
en.result: Braided theory exhibits usual logarithmic UV divergences without UV/IR mixing; standard theory shows periodic UV/IR mixing where non-planar correlators are UV-finite but non-analytic at exceptional momentum lattice.
en.help_to_user: Provides concrete mathematical frameworks and two implementation paths for constructing QFT on twisted noncommutative spaces.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize spectral decomposition properties of angular twists to control quantum correction behaviors.
idea_spark.risk: Construction heavily depends on specific algebraic structures; physical observability requires further verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Different algebraic structures (braided vs classical) lead to radically different UV/IR mixing behaviors, suggesting core role of algebraic symmetry in quantum gravity effects.

papers.title: Weak-to-Strong Knowledge Distillation Accelerates Visual Learning
papers.summary: Large-scale visual learning is increasingly limited by training cost. Existing knowledge distillation methods transfer from a stronger teacher to a weaker student for compression or final-accuracy improvement. We instead investigate distillation to accelerate the training of strong students. We propose a generalizable plug-and-play recipe that freezes a weaker teacher, applies distillation only in early training, and turns it off once the student reaches and surpasses teacher-level performance. For ImageNet and CIFAR classification, this strategy reaches target thresholds much earlier, with up to 4.8 times speedup measured by epochs.   We confirm that the method generalizes to other tasks and report 1.7 times epoch speedup for object detection on the COCO dataset, and 2.5 times earlier target-FID crossing for diffusion generation on the CIFAR-10 dataset, measured in steps. These findings validate our method as a universal speedup mechanism for visual learning.

ai.tldr: 提出一种弱到强知识蒸馏方法，通过冻结较弱教师模型并在训练早期应用蒸馏，显著加速强学生模型的视觉学习训练过程。
ai.motivation: 大规模视觉学习的训练成本日益成为限制因素，现有知识蒸馏方法主要用于模型压缩或最终精度提升，而非训练加速。
ai.method: 提出通用即插即用方案：冻结较弱教师模型，仅在训练早期应用知识蒸馏，当学生模型达到并超越教师性能后关闭蒸馏。
ai.result: 在ImageNet和CIFAR分类任务上达到目标阈值时间大幅提前，最高加速4.8倍（按周期计）；在COCO目标检测上加速1.7倍，在CIFAR-10扩散生成任务上目标FID提前2.5倍达到。
ai.help_to_user: 为视觉学习任务提供了一种通用的训练加速机制，可应用于分类、检测、生成等多种任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用现有较弱模型作为临时教师，加速更强模型的训练过程。
idea_spark.risk: 需要平衡蒸馏强度与关闭时机，过早关闭可能影响最终性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 反向思考知识蒸馏的应用方向——从“强教弱”的传统范式转向“弱助强”的加速范式。

zh.result: 在ImageNet和CIFAR分类任务上达到目标阈值时间大幅提前，最高加速4.8倍（按周期计）；在COCO目标检测上加速1.7倍，在CIFAR-10扩散生成任务上目标FID提前2.5倍达到。

idea_spark.risk: 需要平衡蒸馏强度与关闭时机，过早关闭可能影响最终性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 反向思考知识蒸馏的应用方向——从“强教弱”的传统范式转向“弱助强”的加速范式。

en.tldr: Proposes weak-to-strong knowledge distillation that accelerates visual learning training by freezing a weaker teacher and applying distillation only in early training stages.
en.motivation: Large-scale visual learning is increasingly limited by training costs, while existing knowledge distillation methods focus on compression or final accuracy rather than training acceleration.
en.method: A generalizable plug-and-play recipe: freeze a weaker teacher model, apply distillation only during early training, and turn it off once the student surpasses teacher performance.
en.result: Achieves target thresholds much earlier with up to 4.8× speedup in epochs for ImageNet/CIFAR classification, 1.7× speedup for COCO object detection, and 2.5× earlier target-FID crossing for CIFAR-10 diffusion generation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a universal speedup mechanism for visual learning tasks applicable to classification, detection, generation, and other domains.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize existing weaker models as temporary teachers to accelerate training of stronger models.
idea_spark.risk: Requires balancing distillation strength and shutdown timing; premature shutdown may affect final performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Reversing the traditional knowledge distillation paradigm—from 'strong teaching weak' to 'weak helping strong' for acceleration.

papers.title: Stein Variational Black-Box Combinatorial Optimization
papers.summary: Combinatorial black-box optimization in high-dimensional settings demands a careful trade-off between exploiting promising regions of the search space and preserving sufficient exploration to identify multiple optima. Although Estimation-of-Distribution Algorithms (EDAs) provide a powerful model-based framework, they often concentrate on a single region of interest, which may result in premature convergence when facing complex or multimodal objective landscapes. In this work, we incorporate the Stein operator to introduce a repulsive mechanism among particles in the parameter space, thereby encouraging the population to disperse and jointly explore several modes of the fitness landscape. Empirical evaluations across diverse benchmark problems show that the proposed method achieves performance competitive with, and in several cases superior to, leading state-of-the-art approaches, particularly on large-scale instances. These findings highlight the potential of Stein variational gradient descent as a promising direction for addressing large, computationally expensive, discrete black-box optimization problems.

ai.tldr: 本研究将Stein算子引入黑盒组合优化，通过粒子间的排斥机制促进种群分散，有效探索多模态目标空间，在大规模问题上表现优异。
ai.motivation: 传统分布估计算法（EDAs）在复杂多模态目标空间中容易过早收敛于单一区域，需要一种机制来平衡探索与利用。
ai.method: 在参数空间中引入Stein算子，在粒子间产生排斥力，促使种群分散并共同探索适应度景观的多个模态。
ai.result: 在多种基准问题上，该方法性能与现有先进方法相当或更优，尤其在大规模实例上表现突出。
ai.help_to_user: 为高维、计算昂贵的离散黑盒优化问题提供了一种基于Stein变分梯度下降的新框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将变分推断中的排斥项引入组合优化，维持种群多样性。
idea_spark.risk: 排斥强度的调参可能影响收敛速度；在高维离散空间的理论保证尚不完善。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将概率建模中的分散技术应用于离散优化，以应对局部最优陷阱。

zh.tldr: 本研究将Stein算子引入黑盒组合优化，通过粒子间的排斥机制促进种群分散，有效探索多模态目标空间，在大规模问题上表现优异。
zh.motivation: 传统分布估计算法（EDAs）在复杂多模态目标空间中容易过早收敛于单一区域，需要一种机制来平衡探索与利用。

zh.result: 在多种基准问题上，该方法性能与现有先进方法相当或更优，尤其在大规模实例上表现突出。
zh.help_to_user: 为高维、计算昂贵的离散黑盒优化问题提供了一种基于Stein变分梯度下降的新框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将变分推断中的排斥项引入组合优化，维持种群多样性。
idea_spark.risk: 排斥强度的调参可能影响收敛速度；在高维离散空间的理论保证尚不完善。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将概率建模中的分散技术应用于离散优化，以应对局部最优陷阱。

en.tldr: This work incorporates the Stein operator into black-box combinatorial optimization, using a repulsive mechanism among particles to promote population dispersion and effectively explore multimodal objective spaces, showing strong performance on large-scale problems.
en.motivation: Traditional Estimation-of-Distribution Algorithms (EDAs) tend to converge prematurely to a single region in complex multimodal objective landscapes, necessitating a mechanism to balance exploration and exploitation.
en.method: Introduces the Stein operator into the parameter space to create repulsive forces among particles, encouraging the population to disperse and jointly explore multiple modes of the fitness landscape.
en.result: Empirical evaluations across diverse benchmarks show the method achieves performance competitive with or superior to leading state-of-the-art approaches, particularly on large-scale instances.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel framework based on Stein variational gradient descent for addressing high-dimensional, computationally expensive discrete black-box optimization problems.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate repulsive terms from variational inference into combinatorial optimization to maintain population diversity.
idea_spark.risk: Tuning the repulsion strength may affect convergence rate; theoretical guarantees in high-dimensional discrete spaces are not fully established.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the application of dispersion techniques from probabilistic modeling to discrete optimization to avoid local optima traps.

papers.title: TableSeq: Unified Generation of Structure, Content, and Layout
papers.summary: We present TableSeq, an image-only, end-to-end framework for joint table structure recognition, content recognition, and cell localization. The model formulates these tasks as a single sequence-generation problem: one decoder produces an interleaved stream of \texttt{HTML} tags, cell text, and discretized coordinate tokens, thereby aligning logical structure, textual content, and cell geometry within a unified autoregressive sequence. This design avoids external OCR, auxiliary decoders, and complex multi-stage post-processing. TableSeq combines a lightweight high-resolution FCN-H16 encoder with a minimal structure-prior head and a single-layer transformer encoder, yielding a compact architecture that remains effective on challenging layouts. Across standard benchmarks, TableSeq achieves competitive or state-of-the-art results while preserving architectural simplicity. It reaches 95.23 TEDS / 96.83 S-TEDS on PubTabNet, 97.45 TEDS / 98.69 S-TEDS on FinTabNet, and 99.79 / 99.54 / 99.66 precision / recall / F1 on SciTSR under the CAR protocol, while remaining competitive on PubTables-1M under GriTS. Beyond TSR/TCR, the same sequence interface generalizes to index-based table querying without task-specific heads, achieving the best IRDR score and competitive ICDR/ICR performance. We also study multi-token prediction for faster blockwise decoding and show that it reduces inference latency with only limited accuracy degradation. Overall, TableSeq provides a practical and reproducible single-stream baseline for unified table recognition, and the source code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/hamdilaziz/TableSeq.

ai.tldr: TableSeq是一个仅使用图像的端到端框架，通过单一解码器生成HTML标签、单元格文本和坐标标记的统一序列，联合完成表格结构识别、内容识别和单元格定位。
ai.motivation: 现有的表格识别方法通常依赖外部OCR、多个辅助解码器或复杂的多阶段后处理流程，导致系统复杂且效率低下。需要一个统一的、简化的端到端解决方案。
ai.method: 将表格结构识别、内容识别和单元格定位任务统一建模为单一序列生成问题。使用轻量级高分辨率FCN-H16编码器、最小化结构先验头和一个单层Transformer编码器，通过解码器生成交错的HTML标签、文本和离散化坐标标记。
ai.result: 在PubTabNet上达到95.23 TEDS / 96.83 S-TEDS，在FinTabNet上达到97.45 TEDS / 98.69 S-TEDS，在SciTSR的CAR协议下达到99.79/99.54/99.66的精确率/召回率/F1分数。模型还支持基于索引的表格查询，无需特定任务头。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个实用且可复现的单流基线模型，用于统一的表格识别任务，代码已开源。

idea_spark.idea: 将逻辑结构、文本内容和几何坐标对齐到一个自回归序列中，避免任务特定的模块。
idea_spark.risk: 对复杂或非标准表格布局的泛化能力可能受限；序列生成顺序可能影响性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该统一序列范式可启发其他多模态或多任务文档分析问题，如公式识别或图表理解。

zh.tldr: TableSeq是一个仅使用图像的端到端框架，通过单一解码器生成HTML标签、单元格文本和坐标标记的统一序列，联合完成表格结构识别、内容识别和单元格定位。
zh.motivation: 现有的表格识别方法通常依赖外部OCR、多个辅助解码器或复杂的多阶段后处理流程，导致系统复杂且效率低下。需要一个统一的、简化的端到端解决方案。
zh.method: 将表格结构识别、内容识别和单元格定位任务统一建模为单一序列生成问题。使用轻量级高分辨率FCN-H16编码器、最小化结构先验头和一个单层Transformer编码器，通过解码器生成交错的HTML标签、文本和离散化坐标标记。
zh.result: 在PubTabNet上达到95.23 TEDS / 96.83 S-TEDS，在FinTabNet上达到97.45 TEDS / 98.69 S-TEDS，在SciTSR的CAR协议下达到99.79/99.54/99.66的精确率/召回率/F1分数。模型还支持基于索引的表格查询，无需特定任务头。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了一个实用且可复现的单流基线模型，用于统一的表格识别任务，代码已开源。

idea_spark.idea: 将逻辑结构、文本内容和几何坐标对齐到一个自回归序列中，避免任务特定的模块。
idea_spark.risk: 对复杂或非标准表格布局的泛化能力可能受限；序列生成顺序可能影响性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该统一序列范式可启发其他多模态或多任务文档分析问题，如公式识别或图表理解。

en.tldr: TableSeq is an image-only, end-to-end framework that jointly performs table structure recognition, content recognition, and cell localization via a single decoder generating a unified sequence of HTML tags, cell text, and coordinate tokens.
en.motivation: Existing table recognition methods often rely on external OCR, auxiliary decoders, or complex multi-stage post-processing, leading to cumbersome systems. A unified, simplified end-to-end solution is needed.
en.method: Formulates table structure recognition, content recognition, and cell localization as a single sequence-generation problem. Uses a lightweight high-resolution FCN-H16 encoder, a minimal structure-prior head, and a single-layer transformer encoder to generate an interleaved stream of HTML tags, text, and discretized coordinate tokens autoregressively.
en.result: Achieves 95.23 TEDS / 96.83 S-TEDS on PubTabNet, 97.45 TEDS / 98.69 S-TEDS on FinTabNet, and 99.79 / 99.54 / 99.66 precision / recall / F1 on SciTSR under the CAR protocol. Also generalizes to index-based table querying without task-specific heads.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical and reproducible single-stream baseline for unified table recognition, with source code publicly available.

idea_spark.idea: Align logical structure, textual content, and geometric coordinates within a unified autoregressive sequence, avoiding task-specific modules.
idea_spark.risk: Generalization to highly complex or non-standard table layouts may be limited; sequence generation order could impact performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: The unified sequence paradigm could inspire other multimodal or multi-task document analysis problems, such as formula recognition or chart understanding.

papers.title: Seeing the imagined: a latent functional alignment in visual imagery decoding from fMRI data
papers.summary: Recent progress in visual brain decoding from fMRI has been enabled by large-scale datasets such as the Natural Scenes Dataset (NSD) and powerful diffusion-based generative models. While current pipelines are primarily optimized for perception, their performance under mental-imagery remains less well understood. In this work, we study how a state-of-the-art (SOTA) perception decoder (DynaDiff) can be adapted to reconstruct imagined content from the Imagery-NSD benchmark. We propose a latent functional alignment approach that maps imagery-evoked activity into the pretrained model's conditioning space, while keeping the remaining components frozen. To mitigate the limited amount of matched imagery-perception supervision, we further introduce a retrieval-based augmentation strategy that selects semantically related NSD perception trials. Across four subjects, latent functional alignment consistently improves high-level semantic reconstruction metrics relative to the frozen pretrained baseline and a voxel-space ridge alignment baseline, and enables above-chance decoding from multiple cortical regions. These results suggest that semantic structure learned from perception can be leveraged to stabilize and improve visual imagery decoding under out-of-distribution conditions.

ai.tldr: 本研究提出一种潜在功能对齐方法，将视觉想象引发的fMRI活动映射到预训练感知解码器的条件空间，以改进想象内容的解码重建。
ai.motivation: 当前基于fMRI的视觉解码流程主要针对感知任务优化，在心理想象条件下的性能尚不明确。
ai.method: 采用潜在功能对齐方法，将想象活动映射到预训练DynaDiff模型的条件空间，并引入基于检索的增强策略以补充监督数据。
ai.result: 在四名被试中，该方法相比基线持续提升了高层语义重建指标，并实现了多个皮层区域的超随机解码。
ai.help_to_user: 展示了从感知中学习的语义结构可用于稳定和改进分布外条件下的视觉想象解码。

idea_spark.idea: 利用检索增强解决配对想象-感知数据稀缺问题，提升模型在有限监督下的泛化能力。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于预训练感知解码器的质量，且在不同被试或想象任务间的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为脑机接口、神经反馈和认知状态解码等领域提供了跨模态适应的新思路。

zh.tldr: 本研究提出一种潜在功能对齐方法，将视觉想象引发的fMRI活动映射到预训练感知解码器的条件空间，以改进想象内容的解码重建。
zh.motivation: 当前基于fMRI的视觉解码流程主要针对感知任务优化，在心理想象条件下的性能尚不明确。

zh.help_to_user: 展示了从感知中学习的语义结构可用于稳定和改进分布外条件下的视觉想象解码。

idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于预训练感知解码器的质量，且在不同被试或想象任务间的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为脑机接口、神经反馈和认知状态解码等领域提供了跨模态适应的新思路。

en.tldr: This study proposes a latent functional alignment approach that maps imagery-evoked fMRI activity into a pretrained perception decoder's conditioning space to improve reconstruction of imagined content.
en.motivation: Current fMRI-based visual decoding pipelines are primarily optimized for perception, with their performance under mental imagery remaining less understood.
en.method: A latent functional alignment method maps imagery activity into the pretrained DynaDiff model's conditioning space, coupled with a retrieval-based augmentation strategy to supplement supervision.
en.result: Across four subjects, the method consistently improved high-level semantic reconstruction metrics relative to baselines and enabled above-chance decoding from multiple cortical regions.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates that semantic structure learned from perception can be leveraged to stabilize and improve visual imagery decoding under out-of-distribution conditions.

idea_spark.idea: Using retrieval augmentation to address scarce paired imagery-perception data, enhancing generalization under limited supervision.
idea_spark.risk: Method depends on quality of pretrained perception decoder, and generalization across subjects or imagery tasks requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Provides new directions for cross-modal adaptation in brain-computer interfaces, neurofeedback, and cognitive state decoding.

papers.title: VLSF Decoding with Reliability Guarantees over Correlated Noncoherent Fading Channels
papers.summary: This paper studies reliability-guaranteed decoding for variable-length stop-feedback (VLSF) codes over correlated noncoherent fading channels. The decoding rule is based on the evolution of the information density associated with a given channel input-output realization. Due to channel memory, exact evaluation of this information density is intractable. To enable constructive decoding, computable finite-blocklength lower and upper bounds on the information density that hold uniformly over time along each input-output sequence are derived. The lower bound enables a stopping-time analysis for VLSF decoding and has an operational meaning, while the upper bound provides a reference for the relaxation gap, which is explicitly characterized. As a concrete application, the Gauss-Markov fading channel with Gaussian signaling is considered to numerically investigate the stopping-time distribution and the impact of fading correlation on decoding performance.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了相关非相干衰落信道上具有可靠性保证的可变长度停止反馈码的解码问题，推导了信息密度的可计算上下界，并应用于高斯-马尔可夫衰落信道进行数值分析。
ai.motivation: 在具有记忆的相关非相干衰落信道上，信息密度的精确计算难以处理，需要构造性解码方法。
ai.method: 推导了信息密度在有限块长下的可计算上下界，这些界在输入输出序列上随时间一致成立。下界用于停止时间分析，上界用于量化松弛间隙。
ai.result: 获得了可操作的解码规则，明确了松弛间隙，并通过高斯-马尔可夫衰落信道实例分析了停止时间分布和衰落相关性对解码性能的影响。
ai.help_to_user: 为相关衰落信道上的可变长度编码提供了具有可靠性保证的解码框架和可计算边界。

idea_spark.idea: 将一致边界与停止时间分析结合，为变长系统提供可靠性保证。
idea_spark.risk: 边界紧致性依赖于具体信道模型和信号假设。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在非平稳或相关环境下设计自适应可靠通信协议。

idea_spark.risk: 边界紧致性依赖于具体信道模型和信号假设。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在非平稳或相关环境下设计自适应可靠通信协议。

en.tldr: This paper studies reliability-guaranteed decoding for variable-length stop-feedback codes over correlated noncoherent fading channels, derives computable bounds on information density, and applies them to Gauss-Markov fading for numerical analysis.
en.motivation: Exact evaluation of information density is intractable over correlated noncoherent fading channels with memory, necessitating constructive decoding approaches.
en.method: Derived computable finite-blocklength lower and upper bounds on information density that hold uniformly over time along input-output sequences. The lower bound enables stopping-time analysis, while the upper bound quantifies the relaxation gap.
en.result: Obtained an operational decoding rule, explicitly characterized the relaxation gap, and numerically investigated the stopping-time distribution and impact of fading correlation using Gauss-Markov fading with Gaussian signaling.
en.help_to_user: Provides a decoding framework with reliability guarantees and computable bounds for variable-length coding over correlated fading channels.

idea_spark.idea: Combining uniform bounds with stopping-time analysis to provide reliability guarantees for variable-length systems.
idea_spark.risk: Tightness of bounds depends on specific channel models and signaling assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires designing adaptive reliable communication protocols in non-stationary or correlated environments.

papers.title: DenTab: A Dataset for Table Recognition and Visual QA on Real-World Dental Estimates
papers.summary: Tables condense key transactional and administrative information into compact layouts, but practical extraction requires more than text recognition: systems must also recover structure (rows, columns, merged cells, headers) and interpret roles such as line items, subtotals, and totals under common capture artifacts. Many existing resources for table structure recognition and TableVQA are built from clean digital-born sources or rendered tables, and therefore only partially reflect noisy administrative conditions.   We introduce DenTab, a dataset of 2{,}000 cropped table images from dental estimates with high-quality HTML annotations, enabling evaluation of table recognition (TR) and table visual question answering (TableVQA) on the same inputs. DenTab includes 2{,}208 questions across eleven categories spanning retrieval, aggregation, and logic/consistency checks. We benchmark 16 systems, including 14 vision--language models (VLMs) and two OCR baselines. Across models, strong structure recovery does not consistently translate into reliable performance on multi-step arithmetic and consistency questions, and these reasoning failures persist even when using ground-truth HTML table inputs.   To improve arithmetic reliability without training, we propose the Table Router Pipeline, which routes arithmetic questions to deterministic execution. The pipeline combines (i) a VLM that produces a baseline answer, a structured table representation, and a constrained table program with (ii) a rule-based executor that performs exact computation over the parsed table. The source code and dataset will be made publicly available at https://github.com/hamdilaziz/DenTab.

ai.tldr: DenTab是一个包含2000张牙科估算单表格图像的数据集，用于表格识别和视觉问答评估，并提出了结合视觉语言模型与规则执行的Table Router Pipeline以提高算术可靠性。
ai.motivation: 现有表格识别和视觉问答数据集多基于干净的数字源或渲染表格，未能充分反映真实行政文档中的噪声和复杂结构。
ai.method: 构建了包含高质量HTML标注的牙科估算单表格图像数据集DenTab，并提出了Table Router Pipeline：使用视觉语言模型生成答案、结构化表格表示和受限表格程序，再通过基于规则的执行器进行精确计算。
ai.result: 在16个系统上的基准测试表明，强大的结构恢复能力并不总能转化为多步算术和一致性问题的可靠性能；Table Router Pipeline在不训练的情况下提高了算术可靠性。
ai.help_to_user: 为真实世界行政表格的识别和问答任务提供了专门的评估数据集和基准结果，提出的管道方法可提升现有模型的算术推理可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 在视觉语言模型中添加确定性计算路由，将算术问题导向规则引擎执行。
idea_spark.risk: 管道依赖前端模型对表格结构的准确解析，解析错误会传播到后续计算。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在文档AI中设计混合系统，将神经网络的感知能力与符号系统的可靠计算相结合。

en.tldr: DenTab is a dataset of 2,000 dental estimate table images for table recognition and visual QA evaluation, with a proposed Table Router Pipeline combining VLMs and rule-based execution to improve arithmetic reliability.
en.motivation: Existing datasets for table structure recognition and TableVQA are largely built from clean digital-born sources, inadequately reflecting noisy administrative conditions.
en.method: Introduces DenTab dataset with high-quality HTML annotations, and proposes Table Router Pipeline: a VLM produces baseline answer, structured table representation, and constrained table program, followed by a rule-based executor for exact computation.
en.result: Benchmarking 16 systems shows strong structure recovery does not consistently translate to reliable performance on multi-step arithmetic and consistency questions; the pipeline improves arithmetic reliability without training.
en.help_to_user: Provides a specialized dataset and benchmarks for real-world administrative table understanding, with a pipeline method to enhance arithmetic reasoning reliability of existing models.

idea_spark.idea: Add deterministic computation routing to vision-language models, directing arithmetic questions to a rule-based engine.
idea_spark.risk: Pipeline depends on accurate table structure parsing by the front-end model; parsing errors propagate to downstream computation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires designing hybrid systems in document AI that combine neural perception with symbolic computational reliability.

papers.title: Federated Learning with Quantum Enhanced LSTM for Applications in High Energy Physics
papers.summary: Learning with large-scale datasets and information-critical applications, such as in High Energy Physics (HEP), demands highly complex, large-scale models that are both robust and accurate. To tackle this issue and cater to the learning requirements, we envision using a federated learning framework with a quantum-enhanced model. Specifically, we design a hybrid quantum-classical long-shot-term-memory model (QLSTM) for local training at distributed nodes. It combines the representative power of quantum models in understanding complex relationships within the feature space, and an LSTM-based model to learn necessary correlations across data points. Given the computing limitations and unprecedented cost of current stand-alone noisy-intermediate quantum (NISQ) devices, we propose to use a federated learning setup, where the learning load can be distributed to local servers as per design and data availability. We demonstrate the benefits of such a design on a classification task for the Supersymmetry(SUSY) dataset, having 5M rows. Our experiments indicate that the performance of this design is not only better that some of the existing work using variational quantum circuit (VQC) based quantum machine learning (QML) techniques, but is also comparable ($Δ\sim \pm 1\%$) to that of classical deep-learning benchmarks. An important observation from this study is that the designed framework has $<$300 parameters and only needs 20K data points to give a comparable performance. Which also turns out to be a 100$\times$ improvement than the compared baseline models. This shows an improved learning capability of the proposed framework with minimal data and resource requirements, due to the joint model with an LSTM based architecture and a quantum enhanced VQC.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合联邦学习与量子增强LSTM的混合框架，用于高能物理应用，在SUSY数据集上以更少参数和数据实现可比性能。
ai.motivation: 高能物理等数据密集型应用需要复杂、鲁棒且精确的大规模模型，但当前独立NISQ量子设备存在计算限制和高成本。
ai.method: 设计混合量子经典长短期记忆模型（QLSTM）用于分布式节点本地训练，结合量子模型的表征能力和LSTM的序列相关性学习，在联邦学习框架下分配计算负载。
ai.result: 在500万行的SUSY分类任务中，该框架仅需<300个参数和2万数据点即可达到与经典深度学习基准相当的性能（Δ∼±1%），相比基线模型有100倍改进。
ai.help_to_user: 为资源受限的量子-经典混合学习场景提供了一种参数高效、数据需求低的分布式解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将量子电路的复杂关系表征能力与LSTM的时序建模能力在联邦框架下协同
idea_spark.risk: NISQ设备的噪声和稳定性可能影响分布式训练的收敛
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索类似架构用于其他科学计算领域的小数据、高复杂度建模问题

zh.tldr: 提出一种结合联邦学习与量子增强LSTM的混合框架，用于高能物理应用，在SUSY数据集上以更少参数和数据实现可比性能。
zh.motivation: 高能物理等数据密集型应用需要复杂、鲁棒且精确的大规模模型，但当前独立NISQ量子设备存在计算限制和高成本。

zh.help_to_user: 为资源受限的量子-经典混合学习场景提供了一种参数高效、数据需求低的分布式解决方案。

idea_spark.risk: NISQ设备的噪声和稳定性可能影响分布式训练的收敛
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索类似架构用于其他科学计算领域的小数据、高复杂度建模问题

en.tldr: Proposes a federated learning framework with quantum-enhanced LSTM for high-energy physics applications, achieving comparable performance with fewer parameters and data on the SUSY dataset.
en.motivation: Large-scale data-intensive applications like high-energy physics demand robust and accurate models, but standalone NISQ quantum devices face computational limits and high costs.
en.method: Designs a hybrid quantum-classical LSTM (QLSTM) for local training at distributed nodes, combining quantum representation power with LSTM-based correlation learning, using federated learning to distribute computational load.
en.result: On the 5M-row SUSY classification task, the framework requires <300 parameters and only 20K data points to match classical deep-learning benchmarks (Δ∼±1%), showing a 100× improvement over baseline models.
en.help_to_user: Provides a parameter-efficient, low-data-demand distributed solution for resource-constrained quantum-classical hybrid learning scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Synergizing quantum circuit representation of complex relationships with LSTM's sequential modeling in a federated setup
idea_spark.risk: Noise and stability of NISQ devices may affect convergence in distributed training
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore similar architectures for other scientific computing domains with small-data, high-complexity modeling problems

papers.title: Disentangling Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs: A Methodological Investigation of Internal Mechanisms
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities, yet their internal mechanisms for handling reasoning-intensive tasks remain underexplored. To advance the understanding of model-internal processing mechanisms, we present an investigation of how LLMs perform arithmetic operations by examining internal mechanisms during task execution. Using early decoding, we trace how next-token predictions are constructed across layers. Our experiments reveal that while the models recognize arithmetic tasks early, correct result generation occurs only in the final layers. Notably, models proficient in arithmetic exhibit a clear division of labor between attention and MLP modules, where attention propagates input information and MLP modules aggregate it. This division is absent in less proficient models. Furthermore, successful models appear to process more challenging arithmetic tasks functionally, suggesting reasoning capabilities beyond factual recall.

ai.tldr: 本研究通过早期解码技术追踪LLM在算术任务中的内部处理机制，发现熟练模型在注意力与MLP模块间存在明确分工，而正确结果仅在最后层生成。
ai.motivation: 尽管大语言模型展现出强大能力，但其处理推理密集型任务（如算术运算）的内部机制仍不明确，需要系统性探究。
ai.method: 采用早期解码技术，逐层追踪下一个token预测的构建过程，对比分析熟练与不熟练模型在注意力模块和MLP模块中的处理模式。
ai.result: 熟练模型早期识别算术任务，但正确结果仅在最后层生成；其注意力模块负责信息传播，MLP模块负责信息聚合，这种分工在不熟练模型中缺失。
ai.help_to_user: 揭示了LLM执行数学推理时的内部模块分工机制，为理解模型推理能力提供了可解释性视角。

idea_spark.idea: 构建模块分工图谱，量化不同任务中注意力与MLP的贡献度，可能发现通用推理模式。
idea_spark.risk: 实验仅针对算术任务，结论可能不适用于更复杂的数学推理或跨领域任务。
idea_spark.inspiration: 模块分工模式可作为模型推理能力的评估指标，或用于设计更高效的模型架构。

en.tldr: This study traces internal processing mechanisms of LLMs during arithmetic tasks via early decoding, revealing a clear division of labor between attention and MLP modules in proficient models, with correct results generated only in final layers.
en.motivation: While large language models demonstrate impressive capabilities, their internal mechanisms for handling reasoning-intensive tasks like arithmetic operations remain underexplored and require systematic investigation.
en.method: Employing early decoding to trace next-token prediction construction across layers, comparing processing patterns in attention and MLP modules between proficient and less proficient models.
en.result: Proficient models recognize arithmetic tasks early but generate correct results only in final layers; their attention modules propagate input information while MLP modules aggregate it—a division absent in less proficient models.
en.help_to_user: Reveals the internal module division of labor during mathematical reasoning in LLMs, providing an interpretability perspective for understanding model reasoning capabilities.

idea_spark.idea: Construct module division maps and quantify contributions of attention vs. MLP across different tasks to potentially discover universal reasoning patterns.
idea_spark.risk: Experiments focus solely on arithmetic tasks; conclusions may not generalize to more complex mathematical reasoning or cross-domain tasks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Module division patterns could serve as evaluation metrics for model reasoning ability or inform the design of more efficient architectures.

papers.title: Discover and Prove: An Open-source Agentic Framework for Hard Mode Automated Theorem Proving in Lean 4
papers.summary: Most ATP benchmarks embed the final answer within the formal statement -- a convention we call "Easy Mode" -- a design that simplifies the task relative to what human competitors face and may lead to optimistic estimates of model capability. We call the stricter, more realistic setting "Hard Mode": the system must independently discover the answer before constructing a formal proof. To enable Hard Mode research, we make two contributions. First, we release MiniF2F-Hard and FIMO-Hard, expert-reannotated Hard Mode variants of two widely-used ATP benchmarks. Second, we introduce Discover And Prove (DAP), an agentic framework that uses LLM natural-language reasoning with explicit self-reflection to discover answers, then rewrites Hard Mode statements into Easy Mode ones for existing ATP provers. DAP sets the state of the art: on CombiBench it raises solved problems from 7 (previous SOTA, Pass@16) to 10; on PutnamBench it is the first system to formally prove 36 theorems in Hard Mode -- while simultaneously revealing that state-of-the-art LLMs exceed 80% answer accuracy on the same problems where formal provers manage under 10%, exposing a substantial gap that Hard Mode benchmarks are uniquely suited to measure.

ai.tldr: 提出DAP框架和Hard Mode基准，用于更真实的自动定理证明评估，通过LLM推理发现答案并构造形式化证明。
ai.motivation: 现有ATP基准将答案嵌入形式化陈述（Easy Mode），简化了任务，可能导致对模型能力的高估。需要更严格、更接近人类竞赛的Hard Mode设置。
ai.method: 发布MiniF2F-Hard和FIMO-Hard基准；提出DAP框架，使用LLM自然语言推理和自反思发现答案，然后将Hard Mode陈述重写为Easy Mode供现有证明器使用。
ai.result: DAP在CombiBench上将已解决问题从7个提升至10个；在PutnamBench上首次在Hard Mode下形式化证明36个定理；揭示LLM答案准确率超80%，而形式证明器成功率低于10%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了更真实的ATP评估基准和框架，帮助研究者测量模型在独立发现答案和构造证明方面的能力差距。

idea_spark.idea: 在形式化证明中引入“答案发现”作为独立阶段，并用自然语言推理驱动。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM的答案发现可能引入错误，且模式转换可能不适用于所有问题类型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可应用于其他需要“猜想-验证”结构的领域，如程序合成、数学问题求解或科学假设检验。

idea_spark.idea: 在形式化证明中引入“答案发现”作为独立阶段，并用自然语言推理驱动。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM的答案发现可能引入错误，且模式转换可能不适用于所有问题类型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可应用于其他需要“猜想-验证”结构的领域，如程序合成、数学问题求解或科学假设检验。

en.tldr: Proposes the DAP framework and Hard Mode benchmarks for more realistic automated theorem proving evaluation, using LLM reasoning to discover answers and construct formal proofs.
en.motivation: Current ATP benchmarks embed answers in formal statements (Easy Mode), simplifying the task and potentially leading to overestimated model capabilities. A stricter, more human-competitive Hard Mode setting is needed.
en.method: Releases MiniF2F-Hard and FIMO-Hard benchmarks; introduces the DAP framework that uses LLM natural-language reasoning with self-reflection to discover answers, then rewrites Hard Mode statements into Easy Mode for existing provers.
en.result: DAP raises solved problems from 7 to 10 on CombiBench; is the first to formally prove 36 theorems in Hard Mode on PutnamBench; reveals LLMs exceed 80% answer accuracy where formal provers achieve under 10%.
en.help_to_user: Provides more realistic ATP evaluation benchmarks and a framework to measure the gap between answer discovery and formal proof construction capabilities.

idea_spark.idea: Introducing 'answer discovery' as a separate stage in formal proof, driven by natural language reasoning.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on LLM for answer discovery may introduce errors, and mode transformation may not generalize to all problem types.
idea_spark.inspiration: Applicable to other domains with 'conjecture-verification' structures, such as program synthesis, mathematical problem-solving, or scientific hypothesis testing.

papers.title: Optimization Workshop Notes for Mathematical Programming with Equilibrium Constraints Algorithms: Penalty Interior-Point, Implicit-Programming, and Piecewise SQP
papers.summary: In this workshop, we discuss several algorithms for mathematical programs with equilibrium constraints (MPECs). The unifying theme is that MPECs are optimization problems whose feasible set contains a lower-level equilibrium system, often written through complementarity or variational-inequality conditions. This destroys the smooth manifold or convex structure that standard nonlinear programming methods rely on. We focus on four algorithmic viewpoints:   (i) the classical penalty interior-point algorithm (PIPA); (ii) a monotone-linear complementarity problem (LCP) variant of PIPA that explicitly controls complementarity decay; (iii) an implicit-programming descent method for variational inequality (VI)-constrained MPECs; (iv) piecewise SQP (PSQP), which applies SQP on locally selected smooth pieces.   For each method we explain the model, the search direction subproblem, the globalization mechanism, and the meaning of the convergence result. Particular emphasis is placed on what the assumptions are really doing and on the distinction between an attractive algorithmic idea and a fully valid convergence theorem.

ai.tldr: 本研讨会讨论了数学规划与均衡约束（MPEC）的几种算法，包括惩罚内点法、单调线性互补问题变体、隐式规划下降法和分段SQP方法，重点分析了各方法的模型、搜索方向、全局化机制和收敛性假设。
ai.motivation: MPEC问题的可行集包含下层均衡系统（通常用互补性或变分不等式条件描述），破坏了标准非线性规划方法依赖的光滑流形或凸结构，需要专门算法处理。
ai.method: 聚焦四种算法视角：（i）经典惩罚内点算法（PIPA）；（ii）显式控制互补性衰减的单调线性互补问题变体；（iii）变分不等式约束MPEC的隐式规划下降法；（iv）在局部选择光滑片段上应用SQP的分段SQP方法。
ai.result: 对每种方法解释了模型、搜索方向子问题、全局化机制和收敛结果的含义，特别强调假设的实际作用以及有吸引力的算法思想与完整收敛定理之间的区别。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理均衡约束的优化问题提供了系统的算法分类和理论分析框架，帮助理解不同方法的适用条件和收敛保证。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一种混合算法，结合惩罚内点法的全局收敛性和分段SQP的局部快速收敛性，自适应切换策略以提高求解效率。
idea_spark.risk: 算法假设（如单调性、约束规范性）在实际问题中可能不成立，导致收敛失败或陷入局部解。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从控制理论中的切换系统视角分析分段SQP的稳定性，或借鉴机器学习中对抗训练的思想处理均衡约束中的对抗性下层问题。

en.tldr: This workshop discusses several algorithms for mathematical programs with equilibrium constraints (MPECs), including penalty interior-point, monotone LCP variant, implicit-programming descent, and piecewise SQP methods, focusing on their models, search directions, globalization mechanisms, and convergence assumptions.
en.motivation: MPECs contain a lower-level equilibrium system (often via complementarity or variational inequality conditions) in their feasible set, destroying the smooth manifold or convex structure relied upon by standard nonlinear programming methods, necessitating specialized algorithms.
en.method: Focuses on four algorithmic viewpoints: (i) classical penalty interior-point algorithm (PIPA); (ii) a monotone LCP variant of PIPA that explicitly controls complementarity decay; (iii) an implicit-programming descent method for VI-constrained MPECs; (iv) piecewise SQP applying SQP on locally selected smooth pieces.
en.result: Explains for each method the model, search direction subproblem, globalization mechanism, and meaning of convergence results, with particular emphasis on what assumptions actually do and the distinction between attractive algorithmic ideas and fully valid convergence theorems.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic algorithmic taxonomy and theoretical analysis framework for optimization problems with equilibrium constraints, aiding understanding of applicable conditions and convergence guarantees for different methods.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a hybrid algorithm combining the global convergence of penalty interior-point methods with the local fast convergence of piecewise SQP, using adaptive switching strategies to improve solution efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Algorithmic assumptions (e.g., monotonicity, constraint qualifications) may not hold in practical problems, leading to convergence failure or entrapment in local solutions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Analyze the stability of piecewise SQP from the perspective of switched systems in control theory, or borrow ideas from adversarial training in machine learning to handle adversarial lower-level problems in equilibrium constraints.

papers.title: Bilevel Optimization of Agent Skills via Monte Carlo Tree Search
papers.summary: Agent \texttt{skills} are structured collections of instructions, tools, and supporting resources that help large language model (LLM) agents perform particular classes of tasks. Empirical evidence shows that the design of \texttt{skills} can materially affect agent task performance, yet systematically optimizing \texttt{skills} remains challenging. Since a \texttt{skill} comprises instructions, tools, and supporting resources in a structured way, optimizing it requires jointly determining both the structure of these components and the content each component contains. This gives rise to a complex decision space with strong interdependence across structure and components. We therefore represent these two coupled decisions as \texttt{skill} structure and component content, and formulate \texttt{skill} optimization as a bilevel optimization problem. We propose a bilevel optimization framework in which an outer loop employs Monte Carlo Tree Search to determine the \texttt{skill} structure, while an inner loop refines the component content within the structure selected by the outer loop. In both loops, we employ LLMs to assist the optimization procedure. We evaluate the proposed framework on an open-source Operations Research Question Answering dataset, and the experimental results suggest that the bilevel optimization framework improves the performance of the agents with the optimized \texttt{skill}.

ai.tldr: 本文提出一种双层优化框架，利用蒙特卡洛树搜索优化LLM智能体的技能结构，内层循环细化组件内容，在运筹学问答数据集上提升了智能体性能。
ai.motivation: 智能体技能（指令、工具、资源的结构化集合）的设计显著影响任务性能，但系统化优化技能面临结构-内容强耦合的复杂决策空间挑战。
ai.method: 将技能优化建模为双层优化问题：外层用蒙特卡洛树搜索确定技能结构，内层在选定结构内细化组件内容，两阶段均利用LLM辅助优化。
ai.result: 在开源运筹学问答数据集上的实验表明，该双层优化框架提升了配备优化技能后智能体的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 为优化LLM智能体技能提供了一种结构化联合优化方法，解决了技能结构与内容相互依赖的优化难题。

idea_spark.idea: 将蒙特卡洛树搜索与LLM评估结合，实现结构化决策空间的探索与内容优化的解耦协同。
idea_spark.risk: 优化过程计算成本较高，且技能性能可能对特定任务数据集过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将经典搜索算法与LLM生成能力结合，处理传统优化方法难以直接建模的复杂结构化决策问题。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a bilevel optimization framework that uses Monte Carlo Tree Search to optimize the structure of LLM agent skills, with an inner loop refining component content, improving agent performance on an Operations Research QA dataset.
en.motivation: The design of agent skills (structured collections of instructions, tools, and resources) materially affects task performance, but systematic optimization is challenging due to the strongly interdependent decision space of structure and content.
en.method: Formulates skill optimization as a bilevel problem: outer loop employs Monte Carlo Tree Search to determine skill structure; inner loop refines component content within the selected structure, using LLMs to assist both loops.
en.result: Experimental evaluation on an open-source Operations Research Question Answering dataset shows the bilevel optimization framework improves the performance of agents equipped with the optimized skill.
en.help_to_user: Provides a structured joint optimization method for LLM agent skills, addressing the challenge of optimizing interdependent structure and content.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Monte Carlo Tree Search with LLM evaluation enables decoupled yet synergistic exploration of structured decision spaces and content refinement.
idea_spark.risk: The optimization process is computationally expensive, and skill performance may overfit to specific task datasets.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the integration of classical search algorithms with LLM generative capabilities to tackle complex structured decision problems difficult to model with traditional optimization methods.

papers.title: Continual Hand-Eye Calibration for Open-world Robotic Manipulation
papers.summary: Hand-eye calibration through visual localization is a critical capability for robotic manipulation in open-world environments. However, most deep learning-based calibration models suffer from catastrophic forgetting when adapting into unseen data amongst open-world scene changes, while simple rehearsal-based continual learning strategy cannot well mitigate this issue. To overcome this challenge, we propose a continual hand-eye calibration framework, enabling robots to adapt to sequentially encountered open-world manipulation scenes through spatially replay strategy and structure-preserving distillation. Specifically, a Spatial-Aware Replay Strategy (SARS) constructs a geometrically uniform replay buffer that ensures comprehensive coverage of each scene pose space, replacing redundant adjacent frames with maximally informative viewpoints. Meanwhile, a Structure-Preserving Dual Distillation (SPDD) is proposed to decompose localization knowledge into coarse scene layout and fine pose precision, and distills them separately to alleviate both types of forgetting during continual adaptation. As a new manipulation scene arrives, SARS provides geometrically representative replay samples from all prior scenes, and SPDD applies structured distillation on these samples to retain previously learned knowledge. After training on the new scene, SARS incorporates selected samples from the new scene into the replay buffer for future rehearsal, allowing the model to continuously accumulate multi-scene calibration capability. Experiments on multiple public datasets show significant anti scene forgetting performance, maintaining accuracy on past scenes while preserving adaptation to new scenes, confirming the effectiveness of the framework.

ai.tldr: 提出一个持续手眼标定框架，通过空间感知回放策略和结构保持双重蒸馏，使机器人能够在开放世界场景变化中持续适应，有效缓解灾难性遗忘。
ai.motivation: 基于深度学习的手眼标定模型在适应开放世界未见数据时，容易发生灾难性遗忘，而简单的基于回放的持续学习策略无法很好缓解此问题。
ai.method: 1. 空间感知回放策略（SARS）：构建几何均匀的回放缓冲区，确保对每个场景位姿空间的全面覆盖，用信息量最大的视角替换冗余相邻帧。2. 结构保持双重蒸馏（SPDD）：将定位知识分解为粗略场景布局和精细位姿精度，并分别进行蒸馏，以缓解持续适应过程中的两类遗忘。
ai.result: 在多个公共数据集上的实验表明，该方法具有显著的抗场景遗忘性能，在保持对过去场景精度的同时，保留了对新场景的适应能力。
ai.help_to_user: 为开放世界机器人操作中的持续手眼标定提供了一个有效的解决方案框架，并开源了代码和模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将定位知识结构化分解（布局 vs. 精度）并进行针对性蒸馏，结合几何驱动的代表性样本选择。
idea_spark.risk: 回放缓冲区的存储和计算开销可能随场景数量增加而增长，需权衡性能与效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将任务知识进行更细粒度的解耦，并为不同部分设计差异化的持续学习策略。

zh.tldr: 提出一个持续手眼标定框架，通过空间感知回放策略和结构保持双重蒸馏，使机器人能够在开放世界场景变化中持续适应，有效缓解灾难性遗忘。
zh.motivation: 基于深度学习的手眼标定模型在适应开放世界未见数据时，容易发生灾难性遗忘，而简单的基于回放的持续学习策略无法很好缓解此问题。
zh.method: 1. 空间感知回放策略（SARS）：构建几何均匀的回放缓冲区，确保对每个场景位姿空间的全面覆盖，用信息量最大的视角替换冗余相邻帧。2. 结构保持双重蒸馏（SPDD）：将定位知识分解为粗略场景布局和精细位姿精度，并分别进行蒸馏，以缓解持续适应过程中的两类遗忘。
zh.result: 在多个公共数据集上的实验表明，该方法具有显著的抗场景遗忘性能，在保持对过去场景精度的同时，保留了对新场景的适应能力。
zh.help_to_user: 为开放世界机器人操作中的持续手眼标定提供了一个有效的解决方案框架，并开源了代码和模型。

en.tldr: Proposes a continual hand-eye calibration framework that enables robots to adapt to open-world scene changes through a spatial-aware replay strategy and structure-preserving dual distillation, effectively mitigating catastrophic forgetting.
en.motivation: Deep learning-based hand-eye calibration models suffer from catastrophic forgetting when adapting to unseen data in open-world environments, and simple rehearsal-based continual learning strategies cannot well mitigate this issue.
en.method: 1. Spatial-Aware Replay Strategy (SARS): constructs a geometrically uniform replay buffer ensuring comprehensive coverage of each scene's pose space, replacing redundant adjacent frames with maximally informative viewpoints. 2. Structure-Preserving Dual Distillation (SPDD): decomposes localization knowledge into coarse scene layout and fine pose precision, distilling them separately to alleviate both types of forgetting during continual adaptation.
en.result: Experiments on multiple public datasets show significant anti-forgetting performance, maintaining accuracy on past scenes while preserving adaptation to new scenes.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective solution framework for continual hand-eye calibration in open-world robotic manipulation, with code and models made available.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose localization knowledge structurally (layout vs. precision) and perform targeted distillation, combined with geometry-driven representative sample selection.
idea_spark.risk: Storage and computational overhead of the replay buffer may grow with the number of scenes, requiring a trade-off between performance and efficiency.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking about how to decouple task knowledge more granularly and design differentiated continual learning strategies for different components.

papers.title: MemEvoBench: Benchmarking Memory MisEvolution in LLM Agents
papers.summary: Equipping Large Language Models (LLMs) with persistent memory enhances interaction continuity and personalization but introduces new safety risks. Specifically, contaminated or biased memory accumulation can trigger abnormal agent behaviors. Existing evaluation methods have not yet established a standardized framework for measuring memory misevolution. This phenomenon refers to the gradual behavioral drift resulting from repeated exposure to misleading information. To address this gap, we introduce MemEvoBench, the first benchmark evaluating long-horizon memory safety in LLM agents against adversarial memory injection, noisy tool outputs, and biased feedback. The framework consists of QA-style tasks across 7 domains and 36 risk types, complemented by workflow-style tasks adapted from 20 Agent-SafetyBench environments with noisy tool returns. Both settings employ mixed benign and misleading memory pools within multi-round interactions to simulate memory evolution. Experiments on representative models reveal substantial safety degradation under biased memory updates. Our analysis suggests that memory evolution is a significant contributor to these failures. Furthermore, static prompt-based defenses prove insufficient, underscoring the urgency of securing memory evolution in LLM agents.

ai.tldr: MemEvoBench是首个评估LLM智能体长期记忆安全性的基准，针对对抗性记忆注入、噪声工具输出和偏见反馈，揭示记忆污染导致的行为漂移风险。
ai.motivation: 为LLM配备持久记忆虽提升交互连续性，但可能积累污染或偏见记忆，引发异常行为。现有方法缺乏标准化框架来衡量这种记忆错误演化。
ai.method: 构建包含7个领域、36种风险类型的问答式任务，并改编20个Agent-SafetyBench环境为工作流式任务，在混合良性/误导性记忆池中进行多轮交互模拟记忆演化。
ai.result: 实验表明代表性模型在偏见记忆更新下安全性显著下降，静态提示防御不足，突显保障记忆演化安全的紧迫性。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究者和开发者提供标准化工具，系统评估LLM智能体记忆系统的安全漏洞与演化风险。

idea_spark.idea: 设计动态记忆净化机制，实时检测并过滤污染记忆条目。
idea_spark.risk: 过度过滤可能损害记忆连续性，需平衡安全性与个性化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物记忆可塑性启发，可探索具有遗忘/强化机制的适应性记忆架构。

en.tldr: MemEvoBench is the first benchmark evaluating long-horizon memory safety in LLM agents against adversarial memory injection, noisy tool outputs, and biased feedback, revealing behavioral drift risks from memory contamination.
en.motivation: Equipping LLMs with persistent memory enhances interaction but risks accumulating contaminated or biased memory, triggering abnormal behaviors. Existing methods lack standardized frameworks to measure this memory misevolution.
en.method: Constructs QA-style tasks across 7 domains and 36 risk types, and adapts 20 Agent-SafetyBench environments into workflow-style tasks, simulating memory evolution through multi-round interactions with mixed benign/misleading memory pools.
en.result: Experiments show significant safety degradation in representative models under biased memory updates. Static prompt-based defenses prove insufficient, highlighting the urgency of securing memory evolution.
en.help_to_user: Provides researchers and developers with a standardized tool to systematically evaluate safety vulnerabilities and evolution risks in LLM agent memory systems.

idea_spark.idea: Design dynamic memory purification mechanisms to detect and filter contaminated memory entries in real-time.
idea_spark.risk: Over-filtering may compromise memory continuity, requiring a balance between safety and personalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological memory plasticity, explore adaptive memory architectures with forgetting/reinforcement mechanisms.

papers.title: From Zero to Detail: A Progressive Spectral Decoupling Paradigm for UHD Image Restoration with New Benchmark
papers.summary: Ultra-high-definition (UHD) image restoration poses unique challenges due to the high spatial resolution, diverse content, and fine-grained structures present in UHD images. To address these issues, we introduce a progressive spectral decomposition for the restoration process, decomposing it into three stages: zero-frequency \textbf{enhancement}, low-frequency \textbf{restoration}, and high-frequency \textbf{refinement}. Based on this formulation, we propose a novel framework, \textbf{ERR}, which integrates three cooperative sub-networks: the zero-frequency enhancer (ZFE), the low-frequency restorer (LFR), and the high-frequency refiner (HFR). The ZFE incorporates global priors to learn holistic mappings, the LFR reconstructs the main content by focusing on coarse-scale information, and the HFR adopts our proposed frequency-windowed Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (FW-KAN) to recover fine textures and intricate details for high-fidelity restoration. To further advance research in UHD image restoration, we also construct a large-scale, high-quality benchmark dataset, \textbf{LSUHDIR}, comprising 82{,}126 UHD images with diverse scenes and rich content. Our proposed methods demonstrate superior performance across a range of UHD image restoration tasks, and extensive ablation studies confirm the contribution and necessity of each module. Project page: https://github.com/NJU-PCALab/ERR.

ai.tldr: 提出一种渐进式频谱解耦范式ERR，通过零频增强、低频恢复、高频细化三阶段处理UHD图像修复，并构建大规模基准数据集LSUHDIR。
ai.motivation: UHD图像修复面临高分辨率、内容多样和结构精细的独特挑战，现有方法难以同时处理全局映射和细节恢复。
ai.method: 将修复过程分解为零频增强（ZFE）、低频恢复（LFR）和高频细化（HFR）三阶段；ZFE学习全局先验，LFR重建主体内容，HFR采用提出的FW-KAN网络恢复精细纹理。
ai.result: 在多种UHD图像修复任务中表现优异，消融实验验证了各模块的必要性；构建了包含82,126张图像的大规模高质量数据集LSUHDIR。
ai.help_to_user: 为UHD图像修复提供了系统的渐进式频谱解耦框架和高质量基准数据，有助于提升高分辨率图像的细节恢复能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将复杂修复任务按频率域分解为不同难度的子任务，通过专用模块分阶段处理，降低整体学习难度。
idea_spark.risk: 三阶段框架可能增加计算复杂度和训练难度；频率划分的边界定义需要仔细设计以避免信息丢失。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受信号处理中多尺度分析的启发，将图像修复视为从全局到局部的渐进重建过程，类似“从零到细节”的生成逻辑。

en.tldr: Proposes a progressive spectral decoupling paradigm ERR for UHD image restoration through three stages: zero-frequency enhancement, low-frequency restoration, and high-frequency refinement, along with a large-scale benchmark dataset LSUHDIR.
en.motivation: UHD image restoration faces unique challenges due to high spatial resolution, diverse content, and fine-grained structures, where existing methods struggle to handle both global mapping and detail recovery simultaneously.
en.method: Decomposes restoration into three stages: zero-frequency enhancement (ZFE), low-frequency restoration (LFR), and high-frequency refinement (HFR); ZFE learns global priors, LFR reconstructs main content, and HFR uses the proposed FW-KAN network to recover fine textures.
en.result: Demonstrates superior performance across various UHD image restoration tasks; ablation studies confirm each module's contribution; constructs a large-scale high-quality dataset LSUHDIR with 82,126 images.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic progressive spectral decoupling framework and high-quality benchmark data for UHD image restoration, enhancing detail recovery capabilities for high-resolution images.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose complex restoration tasks into subtasks of varying difficulty in the frequency domain, processed by dedicated modules in stages to reduce overall learning difficulty.
idea_spark.risk: The three-stage framework may increase computational complexity and training difficulty; frequency partition boundaries require careful design to avoid information loss.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-scale analysis in signal processing, treating image restoration as a progressive reconstruction from global to local, akin to a 'from zero to detail' generation logic.

papers.title: How Hypocritical Is Your LLM judge? Listener-Speaker Asymmetries in the Pragmatic Competence of Large Language Models
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly studied as repositories of linguistic knowledge. In this line of work, models are commonly evaluated both as generators of language and as judges of linguistic output, yet these two roles are rarely examined in direct relation to one another. As a result, it remains unclear whether success in one role aligns with success in the other. In this paper, we address this question for pragmatic competence by comparing LLMs' performance as pragmatic listeners, judging the appropriateness of linguistic outputs, and as pragmatic speakers, generating pragmatically appropriate language. We evaluate multiple open-weight and proprietary LLMs across three pragmatic settings. We find a robust asymmetry between pragmatic evaluation and pragmatic generation: many models perform substantially better as listeners than as speakers. Our results suggest that pragmatic judging and pragmatic generation are only weakly aligned in current LLMs, calling for more integrated evaluation practices.

ai.tldr: 研究发现大型语言模型在作为语用听者（判断语言适当性）和语用说者（生成适当语言）时存在显著不对称性，多数模型听者表现远优于说者表现。
ai.motivation: 当前研究通常将LLMs分别作为语言生成器和语言判断器进行评估，但很少考察这两种角色之间的直接关系，不清楚一种角色的成功是否与另一种角色的成功一致。
ai.method: 在三种语用场景中评估多个开源和专有LLMs，比较它们作为语用听者（判断语言适当性）和语用说者（生成语用适当语言）的表现。
ai.result: 发现语用判断和语用生成之间存在稳健的不对称性：许多模型作为听者的表现明显优于作为说者。
ai.help_to_user: 揭示了当前LLMs中语用能力评估的局限性，表明仅评估单一角色可能无法全面反映模型的真实语用能力。

idea_spark.idea: 开发同时优化生成与判断能力的联合训练目标
idea_spark.risk: 仅依赖听者评估可能导致高估模型实际对话能力
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发设计更综合的评估协议，要求模型在交互中切换听者/说者角色

en.tldr: The study reveals a robust asymmetry in LLMs' performance as pragmatic listeners (judging appropriateness) versus pragmatic speakers (generating appropriate language), with listener performance substantially better for many models.
en.motivation: LLMs are commonly evaluated separately as language generators and judges, yet the relationship between these two roles remains unclear—whether success in one aligns with success in the other.
en.method: Evaluate multiple open-weight and proprietary LLMs across three pragmatic settings, comparing their performance as pragmatic listeners (judging linguistic appropriateness) and pragmatic speakers (generating pragmatically appropriate language).
en.result: Find a robust asymmetry between pragmatic evaluation and pragmatic generation: many models perform substantially better as listeners than as speakers.
en.help_to_user: Highlights limitations in current pragmatic competence evaluations of LLMs, suggesting that assessing only one role may not fully capture a model's true pragmatic capabilities.

idea_spark.idea: Develop joint training objectives that simultaneously optimize generation and judgment capabilities
idea_spark.risk: Relying solely on listener evaluation may overestimate a model's actual conversational ability
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires designing more integrated evaluation protocols requiring models to switch between listener/speaker roles in interaction

papers.title: PolarMAE: Efficient Fetal Ultrasound Pre-training via Semantic Screening and Polar-Guided Masking
papers.summary: Intelligent fetal ultrasound (US) interpretation is crucial for prenatal diagnosis, but high annotation costs and operator-induced variance make unsupervised pre-training a highly promising paradigm. However, existing pre-training methods largely ignore US-specific characteristics -- severe data redundancy, fan-shaped locality, and polar coordinate beamforming -- limiting their effectiveness in downstream tasks. To address this, we propose PolarMAE, a novel and efficient pre-training framework tailored for US images. Specifically, to mitigate continuous scanning redundancy, we introduce a Progressive Visual-Semantic Screening (PVSS) that adaptively extracts high-value samples, significantly boosting pre-training efficiency. Furthermore, we design an Acoustic-Bounded Region Constraint (ABRC) to accommodate US locality, forcing the model to focus strictly on valid acoustic regions rather than invalid dark backgrounds. Finally, leveraging the beamforming prior and local details, we propose a Polar-Texture Collaborative Masking (PTCM), enabling the model to capture underlying radial imaging patterns and critical tissue structures. Extensive experiments across diverse datasets and downstream interpretation tasks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance with strong pre-training scalability and efficiency.

ai.tldr: PolarMAE是一种针对胎儿超声图像的高效预训练框架，通过语义筛选和极坐标引导掩码，解决数据冗余和超声特异性问题。
ai.motivation: 胎儿超声智能解读对产前诊断至关重要，但高标注成本和操作者差异使得无监督预训练成为有前景的范式。现有方法忽略了超声特有的数据冗余、扇形局部性和极坐标波束形成特性。
ai.method: 提出渐进视觉语义筛选(PVSS)提取高价值样本；设计声学边界区域约束(ABRC)聚焦有效声学区域；提出极坐标-纹理协同掩码(PTCM)捕获径向成像模式和关键组织结构。
ai.result: 在多个数据集和下游解读任务上的实验表明，该方法实现了最先进的性能，并具有强大的预训练可扩展性和效率。
ai.help_to_user: 为医学超声图像的无监督预训练提供了专门优化的框架，显著提升下游任务性能。

idea_spark.idea: 利用成像物理先验（如极坐标波束形成）指导掩码策略，增强模型对领域特定模式的学习。
idea_spark.risk: 方法对超声成像参数和探头几何可能敏感，需在不同设备上验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将成像物理约束与自监督学习结合，为领域自适应预训练开辟新路径。

idea_spark.idea: 利用成像物理先验（如极坐标波束形成）指导掩码策略，增强模型对领域特定模式的学习。
idea_spark.risk: 方法对超声成像参数和探头几何可能敏感，需在不同设备上验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将成像物理约束与自监督学习结合，为领域自适应预训练开辟新路径。

en.tldr: PolarMAE is an efficient pre-training framework for fetal ultrasound images, addressing data redundancy and ultrasound-specific characteristics via semantic screening and polar-guided masking.
en.motivation: Intelligent fetal ultrasound interpretation is crucial for prenatal diagnosis, but high annotation costs and operator variance make unsupervised pre-training promising. Existing methods ignore US-specific traits: severe redundancy, fan-shaped locality, and polar coordinate beamforming.
en.method: Progressive Visual-Semantic Screening (PVSS) extracts high-value samples; Acoustic-Bounded Region Constraint (ABRC) focuses on valid acoustic regions; Polar-Texture Collaborative Masking (PTCM) captures radial imaging patterns and critical tissue structures.
en.result: Extensive experiments across diverse datasets and downstream tasks demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with strong pre-training scalability and efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Provides a specialized framework for unsupervised pre-training of medical ultrasound images, significantly improving downstream task performance.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage imaging physics priors (e.g., polar beamforming) to guide masking strategies, enhancing model learning of domain-specific patterns.
idea_spark.risk: Method may be sensitive to ultrasound imaging parameters and probe geometry, requiring validation across devices for generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining imaging physics constraints with self-supervised learning opens new paths for domain-adaptive pre-training.

papers.title: Risk-Sensitive Investment Management via Free Energy-Entropy Duality
papers.summary: We study a benchmarked risk-sensitive portfolio problem in a factor-based setting to bring together three strands of the literature: benchmarked risk-sensitive investment management, the Kuroda-Nagai change-of-measure method, and the free energy-entropy duality of Dai Pra et al. (1996). We show that the duality yields a direct solution of the benchmarked problem by reformulating it as a linear-quadratic-Gaussian stochastic differential game under a suitable equivalent probability measure, with an entropic regularization. The resulting value function is quadratic, the optimal controls are explicit affine feedback maps, and the optimal allocation admits two complementary interpretations: as a fractional Kelly strategy and as a Kelly portfolio adjusted via the entropic regularization. This formulation, therefore, contributes both a direct analytical route to the solution and a clearer interpretation of risk sensitivity, thereby embedding the classical Kuroda-Nagai change-of-measure approach within a more general framework. An added benefit of this formulation is that it is suitable for implementation via an RL algorithm. A simple implementation on U.S. equity data illustrates the tractability of the framework and numerically confirms the equivalence of the two approaches.

ai.tldr: 本研究通过自由能-熵对偶性，将基准化风险敏感投资组合问题重构为线性二次高斯随机微分博弈，得到显式最优控制策略，并统一了经典方法框架。
ai.motivation: 整合基准化风险敏感投资管理、Kuroda-Nagai测度变换方法和自由能-熵对偶性这三个文献分支，为风险敏感投资问题提供更直接的解析解和更清晰的解释框架。
ai.method: 利用自由能-熵对偶性，在等价概率测度下将基准化风险敏感组合问题转化为带有熵正则化的线性二次高斯随机微分博弈。
ai.result: 得到二次型值函数、显式仿射反馈最优控制，最优配置可解释为分数凯利策略或经熵正则化调整的凯利组合，并通过美股数据验证了框架的可处理性。
ai.help_to_user: 为风险敏感投资管理提供了统一的解析框架和可直接实现的显式策略，同时支持强化学习算法实现。

idea_spark.idea: 将其他金融优化问题（如动态资产配置、风险管理）通过类似对偶性转化为可解的博弈或控制问题。
idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖于特定的因子设定和线性二次结构，市场结构突变时策略可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 物理/信息论中的对偶概念（如自由能原理）可为金融随机控制问题提供新的建模视角和解法框架。

en.tldr: This study reformulates a benchmarked risk-sensitive portfolio problem as a linear-quadratic-Gaussian stochastic differential game via free energy-entropy duality, yielding explicit optimal controls and unifying classical approaches.
en.motivation: To integrate three strands of literature: benchmarked risk-sensitive investment management, the Kuroda-Nagai change-of-measure method, and the free energy-entropy duality, providing a more direct analytical solution and clearer interpretive framework for risk-sensitive investment problems.
en.method: Utilizes free energy-entropy duality to transform the benchmarked risk-sensitive portfolio problem into a linear-quadratic-Gaussian stochastic differential game with entropic regularization under an equivalent probability measure.
en.result: Obtains a quadratic value function, explicit affine feedback optimal controls, with optimal allocation interpretable as either a fractional Kelly strategy or a Kelly portfolio adjusted via entropic regularization, validated on U.S. equity data.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified analytical framework and directly implementable explicit strategies for risk-sensitive investment management, with support for reinforcement learning implementation.

idea_spark.idea: Apply similar duality transformations to other financial optimization problems (e.g., dynamic asset allocation, risk management) into solvable games or control problems.
idea_spark.risk: Model relies on specific factor settings and linear-quadratic structure; strategies may fail under sudden market regime changes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Duality concepts from physics/information theory (e.g., free energy principle) can offer new modeling perspectives and solution frameworks for financial stochastic control problems.

papers.title: The Metacognitive Monitoring Battery: A Cross-Domain Benchmark for LLM Self-Monitoring
papers.summary: We introduce a cross-domain behavioural assay of monitoring-control coupling in LLMs, grounded in the Nelson and Narens (1990) metacognitive framework and applying human psychometric methodology to LLM evaluation. The battery comprises 524 items across six cognitive domains (learning, metacognitive calibration, social cognition, attention, executive function, prospective regulation), each grounded in an established experimental paradigm. Tasks T1-T5 were pre-registered on OSF prior to data collection; T6 was added as an exploratory extension. After every forced-choice response, dual probes adapted from Koriat and Goldsmith (1996) ask the model to KEEP or WITHDRAW its answer and to BET or decline. The critical metric is the withdraw delta: the difference in withdrawal rate between incorrect and correct items. Applied to 20 frontier LLMs (10,480 evaluations), the battery discriminates three profiles consistent with the Nelson-Narens architecture: blanket confidence, blanket withdrawal, and selective sensitivity. Accuracy rank and metacognitive sensitivity rank are largely inverted. Retrospective monitoring and prospective regulation appear dissociable (r = .17, 95% CI wide given n=20; exemplar-based evidence is the primary support). Scaling on metacognitive calibration is architecture-dependent: monotonically decreasing (Qwen), monotonically increasing (GPT-5.4), or flat (Gemma). Behavioural findings converge structurally with an independent Type-2 SDT approach, providing preliminary cross-method construct validity. All items, data, and code: https://github.com/synthiumjp/metacognitive-monitoring-battery.

ai.tldr: 我们提出了一个跨领域的行为测试电池，用于评估大语言模型中的监控-控制耦合，基于Nelson-Narens元认知框架，并在20个前沿LLM上进行了验证。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM评估缺乏对人类元认知监控能力的系统性、跨领域测量。需要一种基于成熟心理学范式的标准化测试来量化LLM的自我监控行为。
ai.method: 构建了包含524个项目的测试电池，覆盖六个认知领域。每个项目采用强制选择后接双探针（保留/撤回、下注/放弃）的设计。核心指标是撤回差异（错误与正确项目的撤回率之差）。
ai.result: 测试区分出三种行为模式：盲目自信、盲目撤回和选择性敏感。模型准确率排名与元认知敏感性排名基本倒置。元认知校准的缩放趋势因模型架构而异。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个开源、标准化的行为测试框架，可用于系统评估和比较不同LLM的自我监控能力，支持模型诊断和架构改进。

idea_spark.idea: 将撤回差异等行为指标作为模型置信度校准和不确定性量化的代理测量。
idea_spark.risk: 行为模式可能高度依赖提示工程和任务格式，泛化到真实应用场景需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可扩展至更多认知领域（如推理、规划），或开发动态的、基于交互的实时监控评估任务。

en.tldr: We introduce a cross-domain behavioral assay for evaluating monitoring-control coupling in LLMs, grounded in the Nelson-Narens metacognitive framework, and validate it on 20 frontier LLMs.
en.motivation: Existing LLM evaluations lack systematic, cross-domain measurement of human-like metacognitive monitoring. A standardized test based on established psychological paradigms is needed to quantify LLM self-monitoring behavior.
en.method: Constructed a battery of 524 items across six cognitive domains. Each item uses a forced-choice response followed by dual probes (KEEP/WITHDRAW, BET/decline). The critical metric is the withdraw delta (difference in withdrawal rates between incorrect and correct items).
en.result: The battery discriminates three behavioral profiles: blanket confidence, blanket withdrawal, and selective sensitivity. Accuracy rank and metacognitive sensitivity rank are largely inverted. Scaling on metacognitive calibration is architecture-dependent.
en.help_to_user: Provides an open-source, standardized behavioral testing framework for systematically assessing and comparing self-monitoring capabilities across LLMs, supporting model diagnosis and architectural improvement.

idea_spark.idea: Using behavioral metrics like withdraw delta as proxy measures for model confidence calibration and uncertainty quantification.
idea_spark.risk: Behavioral profiles may be highly sensitive to prompt engineering and task formatting; generalization to real-world applications requires caution.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could be extended to more cognitive domains (e.g., reasoning, planning) or developed into dynamic, interaction-based real-time monitoring assessment tasks.

papers.title: Dual-Modal Lung Cancer AI: Interpretable Radiology and Microscopy with Clinical Risk Integration
papers.summary: Lung cancer remains one of the leading causes of cancer-related mortality worldwide. Conventional computed tomography (CT) imaging, while essential for detection and staging, has limitations in distinguishing benign from malignant lesions and providing interpretable diagnostic insights. To address this challenge, this study proposes a dual-modal artificial intelligence framework that integrates CT radiology with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) histopathology for lung cancer diagnosis and subtype classification. The system employs convolutional neural networks to extract radiologic and histopathologic features and incorporates clinical metadata to improve robustness. Predictions from both modalities are fused using a weighted decision-level integration mechanism to classify adenocarcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, large cell carcinoma, small cell lung cancer, and normal tissue. Explainable AI techniques including Grad-CAM, Grad-CAM++, Integrated Gradients, Occlusion, Saliency Maps, and SmoothGrad are applied to provide visual interpretability. Experimental results show strong performance with accuracy up to 0.87, AUROC above 0.97, and macro F1-score of 0.88. Grad-CAM++ achieved the highest faithfulness and localization accuracy, demonstrating strong correspondence with expert-annotated tumor regions. These results indicate that multimodal fusion of radiology and histopathology can improve diagnostic performance while maintaining model transparency, suggesting potential for future clinical decision support systems in precision oncology.

ai.tldr: 本研究提出了一种融合CT影像与H&E病理切片的双模态AI框架，用于肺癌诊断与亚型分类，并通过可解释性技术提升模型透明度。
ai.motivation: 传统CT影像在区分良恶性病变和提供可解释诊断方面存在局限，需要更精准、透明的诊断方法。
ai.method: 使用卷积神经网络提取影像与病理特征，结合临床元数据，通过加权决策级融合机制对肺癌亚型进行分类，并应用Grad-CAM++等多种可解释性技术提供可视化解释。
ai.result: 模型准确率达0.87，AUROC超过0.97，宏观F1分数为0.88；Grad-CAM++在忠实度和定位准确性上表现最佳，与专家标注区域高度一致。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种融合放射学与病理学的多模态诊断框架，可提升肺癌诊断性能并保持模型可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 将放射影像与病理切片在决策层融合，并系统比较多种可解释性方法的效果。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于高质量的配对影像-病理数据，临床元数据的获取与标准化可能存在挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在精准肿瘤学中构建透明、多模态的临床决策支持系统。

en.tldr: This study proposes a dual-modal AI framework integrating CT radiology and H&E histopathology for lung cancer diagnosis and subtype classification, with enhanced transparency via explainable AI techniques.
en.motivation: Conventional CT imaging has limitations in distinguishing benign from malignant lesions and providing interpretable diagnostic insights, necessitating more accurate and transparent diagnostic approaches.
en.method: Convolutional neural networks extract radiologic and histopathologic features, clinical metadata is incorporated, a weighted decision-level fusion mechanism classifies lung cancer subtypes, and multiple explainable AI techniques (e.g., Grad-CAM++) provide visual interpretability.
en.result: Model accuracy reached 0.87, AUROC exceeded 0.97, macro F1-score was 0.88; Grad-CAM++ achieved the highest faithfulness and localization accuracy, strongly correlating with expert-annotated tumor regions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a multimodal diagnostic framework fusing radiology and histopathology to improve lung cancer diagnostic performance while maintaining model interpretability.

idea_spark.idea: Fuse radiology and histopathology at the decision level and systematically compare the effectiveness of multiple explainability methods.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality paired imaging-pathology data; acquisition and standardization of clinical metadata may pose challenges.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the development of transparent, multimodal clinical decision support systems in precision oncology.

papers.title: Stochasticity in Tokenisation Improves Robustness
papers.summary: The widespread adoption of large language models (LLMs) has increased concerns about their robustness. Vulnerabilities in perturbations of tokenisation of the input indicate that models trained with a deterministic canonical tokenisation can be brittle to adversarial attacks. Recent studies suggest that stochastic tokenisation can deliver internal representations that are less sensitive to perturbations. In this paper, we analyse how stochastic tokenisations affect robustness to adversarial attacks and random perturbations. We systematically study this over a range of learning regimes (pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, and in-context learning), data sets, and model architectures. We show that pre-training and fine-tuning with uniformly sampled stochastic tokenisations improve robustness to random and adversarial perturbations. Evaluating on uniformly sampled non-canonical tokenisations reduces the accuracy of a canonically trained Llama-1b model by 29.8%. We find that training with stochastic tokenisation preserves accuracy without increasing inference cost.

ai.tldr: 研究表明，在预训练和微调阶段使用随机分词策略，能有效提升大语言模型对随机扰动和对抗攻击的鲁棒性，且不增加推理成本。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型广泛采用确定性标准分词，使其在面对输入分词扰动时表现脆弱，易受对抗攻击影响。
ai.method: 系统研究了随机分词在不同学习范式（预训练、监督微调、上下文学习）、多种数据集和模型架构中对鲁棒性的影响。
ai.result: 在预训练和微调中使用均匀采样的随机分词，提升了模型对随机和对抗扰动的鲁棒性。评估显示，标准训练的Llama-1b模型在非标准分词下准确率下降29.8%，而随机分词训练能保持准确率。
ai.help_to_user: 本文为提升LLM鲁棒性提供了一种无需增加推理成本的训练策略。

idea_spark.idea: 在模型训练的数据预处理阶段引入可控的随机性（如分词），作为一种数据增强或正则化手段。
idea_spark.risk: 随机性的引入可能影响训练稳定性或在某些任务上降低性能，需仔细控制随机范围和强度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索在其他模态（如图像patch划分、语音帧分割）或模型组件中引入类似的结构性随机性，以提升对抗鲁棒性或分布外泛化能力。

idea_spark.risk: 随机性的引入可能影响训练稳定性或在某些任务上降低性能，需仔细控制随机范围和强度。
