en.tldr: Training with stochastic tokenisation during pre-training and fine-tuning improves LLM robustness to random and adversarial perturbations without increasing inference cost.
en.motivation: LLMs trained with deterministic canonical tokenisation are brittle to adversarial attacks via input tokenisation perturbations.
en.method: Systematically analysed the effect of stochastic tokenisation on robustness across learning regimes (pre-training, fine-tuning, in-context learning), datasets, and model architectures.
en.result: Pre-training and fine-tuning with uniformly sampled stochastic tokenisations improve robustness. Evaluating on non-canonical tokenisations reduces accuracy of a canonically trained Llama-1b model by 29.8%, while stochastic training preserves accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Provides a training strategy to enhance LLM robustness without extra inference overhead.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate controlled randomness (e.g., in tokenisation) during data pre-processing as a form of data augmentation or regularisation.
idea_spark.risk: Introducing randomness may affect training stability or degrade performance on some tasks, requiring careful control of its scope and intensity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explore similar structural randomness in other modalities (e.g., image patching, audio framing) or model components to enhance adversarial robustness or out-of-distribution generalisation.

papers.title: Polyglot: Multilingual Style Preserving Speech-Driven Facial Animation
papers.summary: Speech-Driven Facial Animation (SDFA) has gained significant attention due to its applications in movies, video games, and virtual reality. However, most existing models are trained on single-language data, limiting their effectiveness in real-world multilingual scenarios. In this work, we address multilingual SDFA, which is essential for realistic generation since language influences phonetics, rhythm, intonation, and facial expressions. Speaking style is also shaped by individual differences, not only by language. Existing methods typically rely on either language-specific or speaker-specific conditioning, but not both, limiting their ability to model their interaction. We introduce Polyglot, a unified diffusion-based architecture for personalized multilingual SDFA. Our method uses transcript embeddings to encode language information and style embeddings extracted from reference facial sequences to capture individual speaking characteristics. Polyglot does not require predefined language or speaker labels, enabling generalization across languages and speakers through self-supervised learning. By jointly conditioning on language and style, it captures expressive traits such as rhythm, articulation, and habitual facial movements, producing temporally coherent and realistic animations. Experiments show improved performance in both monolingual and multilingual settings, providing a unified framework for modeling language and personal style in SDFA.

ai.tldr: Polyglot是一个基于扩散模型的统一架构，用于实现个性化、多语言的语音驱动面部动画，能同时建模语言信息和个体说话风格。
ai.motivation: 现有语音驱动面部动画模型大多基于单语言数据训练，难以处理现实世界的多语言场景，且通常只依赖语言特定或说话人特定的条件，无法同时建模两者的交互。
ai.method: 使用转录本嵌入编码语言信息，从参考面部序列中提取风格嵌入捕捉个体说话特征。无需预定义语言或说话人标签，通过自监督学习实现跨语言和说话人的泛化。
ai.result: 在单语和多语设置下均表现出改进的性能，能生成时间连贯、逼真的动画，有效捕捉节奏、发音和习惯性面部运动等表达特征。
ai.help_to_user: 为电影、游戏、虚拟现实等应用提供了一个能处理多语言并保持个人说话风格的统一面部动画生成框架。

idea_spark.risk: 模型性能可能受限于训练数据的语言多样性和风格覆盖范围；在极低资源语言上可能表现不佳。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将扩散模型与多条件控制结合，用于需要同时建模全局属性（如语言）和局部属性（如个人风格）的生成任务。

en.tldr: Polyglot is a unified diffusion-based architecture for personalized multilingual speech-driven facial animation, jointly modeling language information and individual speaking style.
en.motivation: Most existing speech-driven facial animation models are trained on single-language data, limiting real-world multilingual applicability. They typically rely on either language-specific or speaker-specific conditioning, failing to model their interaction.
en.method: Uses transcript embeddings to encode language information and style embeddings extracted from reference facial sequences to capture individual speaking characteristics. Requires no predefined language or speaker labels, enabling generalization through self-supervised learning.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage self-supervised learning from raw data to automatically extract language and style representations, avoiding reliance on expensive labeled data.
idea_spark.risk: Performance may be limited by the linguistic diversity and style coverage of the training data; may underperform on very low-resource languages.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking about how to combine diffusion models with multi-condition control for generation tasks requiring simultaneous modeling of global attributes (e.g., language) and local attributes (e.g., personal style).

papers.title: On the Rejection Criterion for Proxy-based Test-time Alignment
papers.summary: Recent works proposed test-time alignment methods that rely on a small aligned model as a proxy that guides the generation of a larger base (unaligned) model. The implicit reward approach skews the large model distribution, whereas the nudging approach defers the generation of the next token to the small aligned model when the large base one is unconfident about its outcome. In this work, we first show that both approaches can be reduced to sampling from similar graphical models, where they differ only in the definition of a rejection criterion (or distribution). Moreover, we argue that the confidence criterion is ill-motivated due to linguistic phenomena like ambiguous phrasing. We propose a novel rejection criterion based on a conservative confidence bet. Experimentally, our novel approach outperforms previous work on several datasets.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于保守置信赌注的新拒绝准则，用于代理测试时对齐方法，在多个数据集上优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有基于代理的测试时对齐方法依赖于大型基础模型的不确定性作为拒绝准则，但语言歧义等现象使其动机不足。
ai.method: 将现有方法统一为从相似图模型采样，仅拒绝准则不同；提出基于保守置信赌注的新拒绝准则。
ai.result: 新方法在多个数据集上优于先前工作。
ai.help_to_user: 为使用代理模型进行测试时对齐的研究者提供了更稳健的拒绝准则设计思路。

idea_spark.idea: 将语言生成中的歧义问题建模为置信赌注，而非简单的不确定性阈值。
idea_spark.risk: 新准则的计算开销可能增加，需在实际部署中权衡效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们重新思考语言模型中“不确定性”的语义定义，超越概率分布的方差度量。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a novel rejection criterion based on a conservative confidence bet for proxy-based test-time alignment, outperforming previous methods on several datasets.
en.motivation: Existing proxy-based test-time alignment methods rely on the large base model's uncertainty as a rejection criterion, but linguistic ambiguities make this motivation insufficient.
en.method: Unify existing approaches as sampling from similar graphical models differing only in rejection criteria; propose a new criterion based on a conservative confidence bet.
en.result: The novel approach outperforms previous work on several datasets.
en.help_to_user: Provides researchers using proxy models for test-time alignment with a more robust rejection criterion design.

idea_spark.idea: Model linguistic ambiguity in generation as a confidence bet rather than a simple uncertainty threshold.
idea_spark.risk: Computational overhead of the new criterion may increase, requiring efficiency trade-offs in deployment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires rethinking the semantic definition of 'uncertainty' in language models beyond variance of probability distributions.

papers.title: ChemGraph-XANES: An Agentic Framework for XANES Simulation and Analysis
papers.summary: Computational X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) is widely used to probe local coordination environments, oxidation states, and electronic structure in chemically complex systems. However, the use of computational XANES at scale is constrained more by workflow complexity than by the underlying simulation method itself. To address this challenge, we present ChemGraph-XANES, an agentic framework for automated XANES simulation and analysis that unifies natural-language task specification, structure acquisition, FDMNES input generation, task-parallel execution, spectral normalization, and provenance-aware data curation. Built on ASE, FDMNES, Parsl, and a LangGraph/LangChain-based tool interface, the framework exposes XANES workflow operations as typed Python tools that can be orchestrated by large language model (LLM) agents. In multi-agent mode, a retrieval-augmented expert agent consults the FDMNES manual to ground parameter selection, while executor agents translate user requests into structured tool calls. We demonstrate documentation-grounded parameter retrieval and show that the same workflow supports both explicit structure-file inputs and chemistry-level natural-language requests. Because independent XANES calculations are naturally task-parallel, the framework is well suited for high-throughput deployment on high-performance computing (HPC) systems, enabling scalable XANES database generation for downstream analysis and machine-learning applications. ChemGraph-XANES thus provides a reproducible and extensible workflow layer for physics-based XANES simulation, spectral curation, and agent-compatible computational spectroscopy.

ai.tldr: ChemGraph-XANES是一个基于智能体的自动化框架，用于X射线吸收近边结构（XANES）的模拟与分析，通过自然语言接口和任务并行执行简化复杂工作流。
ai.motivation: 计算XANES在化学复杂系统中应用广泛，但其规模化使用受限于工作流复杂性而非模拟方法本身。
ai.method: 框架基于ASE、FDMNES、Parsl和LangGraph/LangChain构建，将XANES工作流操作封装为类型化Python工具，由大语言模型（LLM）智能体编排。在多智能体模式下，检索增强的专家智能体参考FDMNES手册进行参数选择，执行智能体将用户请求转换为结构化工具调用。
ai.result: 框架支持基于文档的参数检索，并能同时处理显式结构文件输入和化学级自然语言请求。其任务并行特性使其适合在高性能计算系统上进行高通量部署。
ai.help_to_user: 为基于物理的XANES模拟、光谱管理和智能体兼容的计算光谱学提供了一个可重复、可扩展的工作流层。

idea_spark.idea: 为其他计算光谱学或第一性原理计算领域开发类似的智能体驱动自动化框架。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于特定模拟软件（如FDMNES）和LLM的可靠性，可能需要对不同计算代码进行适配。
idea_spark.inspiration: 展示了如何将传统科学计算工作流与现代AI智能体技术结合，实现人机协同的自动化科学研究。

en.tldr: ChemGraph-XANES is an agentic framework for automated X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) simulation and analysis, simplifying complex workflows through natural-language interfaces and task-parallel execution.
en.motivation: Computational XANES is widely used in chemically complex systems, but its use at scale is constrained more by workflow complexity than by the underlying simulation method itself.
en.method: Built on ASE, FDMNES, Parsl, and LangGraph/LangChain, the framework exposes XANES workflow operations as typed Python tools orchestrated by LLM agents. idea_spark.idea: Develop similar agent-driven automation frameworks for other computational spectroscopy or first-principles calculation domains.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on specific simulation software (e.g., FDMNES) and LLM reliability may require adaptation to different computational codes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Demonstrates how to integrate traditional scientific computing workflows with modern AI agent technology to enable human-AI collaborative automated scientific research.

papers.title: Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents
papers.summary: As LLM agents scale to long-horizon, multi-session deployments, efficiently managing accumulated experience becomes a critical bottleneck. Agent memory systems and agent skill discovery both address this challenge -- extracting reusable knowledge from interaction traces -- yet a citation analysis of 1,136 references across 22 primary papers reveals a cross-community citation rate below 1%. We propose the \emph{Experience Compression Spectrum}, a unifying framework that positions memory, skills, and rules as points along a single axis of increasing compression (5--20$\times$ for episodic memory, 50--500$\times$ for procedural skills, 1,000$\times$+ for declarative rules), directly reducing context consumption, retrieval latency, and compute overhead. Mapping 20+ systems onto this spectrum reveals that every system operates at a fixed, predetermined compression level -- none supports adaptive cross-level compression, a gap we term the \emph{missing diagonal}. We further show that specialization alone is insufficient -- both communities independently solve shared sub-problems without exchanging solutions -- that evaluation methods are tightly coupled to compression levels, that transferability increases with compression at the cost of specificity, and that knowledge lifecycle management remains largely neglected. We articulate open problems and design principles for scalable, full-spectrum agent learning systems.

ai.tldr: 本文提出经验压缩谱框架，将LLM智能体的记忆、技能和规则统一为单一压缩轴上的不同点，揭示了当前系统缺乏跨级别自适应压缩的空白，并分析了压缩级别与评估方法、可迁移性之间的关联。
ai.motivation: 随着LLM智能体向长周期、多会话部署扩展，高效管理累积经验成为关键瓶颈。记忆系统和技能发现都旨在从交互轨迹中提取可重用知识，但跨社区引用率极低，缺乏统一视角。
ai.method: 通过对22篇主要论文的1136篇参考文献进行引文分析，提出经验压缩谱框架，将20多个系统映射到压缩轴上，分析压缩级别（5-20倍用于情景记忆，50-500倍用于程序性技能，1000倍以上用于声明性规则）与系统特性、评估方法的关系。
ai.result: 发现所有现有系统都在固定的预定压缩级别上运行，缺乏自适应跨级别压缩（缺失对角线）；压缩增加可提高可迁移性但牺牲特异性；评估方法与压缩级别紧密耦合；知识生命周期管理被忽视。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个统一框架来理解LLM智能体的经验管理方法，揭示了当前系统的局限性，并为设计可扩展的全谱智能体学习系统提出了开放问题和设计原则。

idea_spark.idea: 开发支持动态跨压缩级别切换的智能体架构，根据任务需求自适应选择记忆、技能或规则表示。
idea_spark.risk: 高压缩可能导致知识过度泛化，在特定场景下失效；跨级别转换需要高效的编码-解码机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数据压缩理论中借鉴率失真概念，优化智能体在有限上下文窗口下的经验表示效率。

en.tldr: This paper proposes the Experience Compression Spectrum framework, unifying LLM agent memory, skills, and rules as points along a single compression axis, revealing the gap of missing adaptive cross-level compression in current systems, and analyzing relationships between compression levels, evaluation methods, and transferability.
en.motivation: As LLM agents scale to long-horizon, multi-session deployments, efficiently managing accumulated experience becomes critical. Both memory systems and skill discovery aim to extract reusable knowledge from interaction traces, yet cross-community citation rates are extremely low, lacking a unified perspective.
en.method: Through citation analysis of 1,136 references across 22 primary papers, proposes the Experience Compression Spectrum framework, maps 20+ systems onto the compression axis, and analyzes relationships between compression levels (5–20× for episodic memory, 50–500× for procedural skills, 1,000×+ for declarative rules) and system characteristics/evaluation methods.
en.result: Finds all existing systems operate at fixed, predetermined compression levels with no adaptive cross-level compression (the missing diagonal); increased compression improves transferability at the cost of specificity; evaluation methods are tightly coupled to compression levels; knowledge lifecycle management remains largely neglected.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unifying framework for understanding experience management approaches in LLM agents, reveals limitations of current systems, and articulates open problems and design principles for scalable, full-spectrum agent learning systems.

idea_spark.idea: Develop agent architectures supporting dynamic switching across compression levels, adaptively selecting memory, skill, or rule representations based on task demands.
idea_spark.risk: High compression may cause over-generalization of knowledge, failing in specific scenarios; cross-level transitions require efficient encoding-decoding mechanisms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow rate-distortion concepts from data compression theory to optimize experience representation efficiency within limited context windows.

papers.title: Fed3D: Federated 3D Object Detection
papers.summary: 3D object detection models trained in one server plays an important role in autonomous driving, robotics manipulation, and augmented reality scenarios. However, most existing methods face severe privacy concern when deployed on a multi-robot perception network to explore large-scale 3D scene. Meanwhile, it is highly challenging to employ conventional federated learning methods on 3D object detection scenes, due to the 3D data heterogeneity and limited communication bandwidth. In this paper, we take the first attempt to propose a novel Federated 3D object detection framework (i.e., Fed3D), to enable distributed learning for 3D object detection with privacy preservation. Specifically, considering the irregular input 3D object in local robot and various category distribution between robots could cause local heterogeneity and global heterogeneity, respectively. We then propose a local-global class-aware loss for the 3D data heterogeneity issue, which could balance gradient back-propagation rate of different 3D categories from local and global aspects. To reduce communication cost on each round, we develop a federated 3D prompt module, which could only learn and communicate the prompts with few learnable parameters. To the end, several extensive experiments on federated 3D object detection show that our Fed3D model significantly outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms with lower communication cost when providing the limited local training data.

ai.tldr: Fed3D是一个用于3D目标检测的联邦学习框架，通过局部-全局类别感知损失和联邦3D提示模块，解决数据异构性和通信成本问题。
ai.motivation: 现有3D目标检测方法在多机器人感知网络中部署时面临严重的隐私问题，且传统联邦学习方法难以应对3D数据异构性和有限通信带宽的挑战。
ai.method: 提出局部-全局类别感知损失平衡不同3D类别的梯度回传速率；设计联邦3D提示模块，仅学习和通信少量可学习参数以降低通信成本。
ai.result: 在联邦3D目标检测实验中，Fed3D在有限本地训练数据下，以更低通信成本显著优于现有先进算法。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要在分布式、隐私敏感场景下进行3D感知的研究者提供了一个高效的联邦学习解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将类别感知机制与参数高效的提示学习结合，应对联邦场景下的数据异构与通信约束。
idea_spark.risk: 在极端非独立同分布或极低带宽场景下的性能稳定性需进一步验证。

zh.tldr: Fed3D是一个用于3D目标检测的联邦学习框架，通过局部-全局类别感知损失和联邦3D提示模块，解决数据异构性和通信成本问题。
zh.motivation: 现有3D目标检测方法在多机器人感知网络中部署时面临严重的隐私问题，且传统联邦学习方法难以应对3D数据异构性和有限通信带宽的挑战。
zh.method: 提出局部-全局类别感知损失平衡不同3D类别的梯度回传速率；设计联邦3D提示模块，仅学习和通信少量可学习参数以降低通信成本。
zh.result: 在联邦3D目标检测实验中，Fed3D在有限本地训练数据下，以更低通信成本显著优于现有先进算法。
zh.help_to_user: 为需要在分布式、隐私敏感场景下进行3D感知的研究者提供了一个高效的联邦学习解决方案。

en.tldr: Fed3D is a federated learning framework for 3D object detection, addressing data heterogeneity and communication cost via a local-global class-aware loss and a federated 3D prompt module.
en.motivation: Existing 3D object detection methods face severe privacy concerns when deployed on multi-robot perception networks, and conventional federated learning struggles with 3D data heterogeneity and limited communication bandwidth.
en.method: Proposes a local-global class-aware loss to balance gradient back-propagation rates across 3D categories; develops a federated 3D prompt module that learns and communicates only few parameters to reduce communication cost.
en.result: Extensive experiments on federated 3D object detection show Fed3D significantly outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms with lower communication cost given limited local training data.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient federated learning solution for researchers working on distributed, privacy-sensitive 3D perception scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Combine category-aware mechanisms with parameter-efficient prompt learning to address data heterogeneity and communication constraints in federated settings.
idea_spark.risk: Performance stability under extreme non-IID data or very low bandwidth scenarios requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires integrating parameter-efficient methods like prompt learning with federated learning to balance performance, privacy, and communication overhead.

papers.title: Causal Bootstrapped Alignment for Unsupervised Video-Based Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification
papers.summary: VVI-ReID is a critical technique for all-day surveillance, where temporal information provides additional cues beyond static images. However, existing approaches rely heavily on fully supervised learning with expensive cross-modality annotations, limiting scalability. To address this issue, we investigate Unsupervised Learning for VVI-ReID (USL-VVI-ReID), which learns identity-discriminative representations directly from unlabeled video tracklets. Directly extending image-based USL-VI-ReID methods to this setting with generic pretrained encoders leads to suboptimal performance. Such encoders suffer from weak identity discrimination and strong modality bias, resulting in severe intra-modality identity confusion and pronounced clustering granularity imbalance between visible and infrared modalities. These issues jointly degrade pseudo-label reliability and hinder effective cross-modality alignment. To address these challenges, we propose a Causal Bootstrapped Alignment (CBA) framework that explicitly exploits inherent video priors. First, we introduce Causal Intervention Warm-up (CIW), which performs sequence-level causal interventions by leveraging temporal identity consistency and cross-modality identity consistency to suppress modality- and motion-induced spurious correlations while preserving identity-relevant semantics, yielding cleaner representations for unsupervised clustering. Second, we propose Prototype-Guided Uncertainty Refinement (PGUR), which employs a coarse-to-fine alignment strategy to resolve cross-modality granularity mismatch, reorganizing under-clustered infrared representations under the guidance of reliable visible prototypes with uncertainty-aware supervision. Extensive experiments on the HITSZ-VCM and BUPTCampus benchmarks demonstrate that CBA significantly outperforms existing USL-VI-ReID methods when extended to the USL-VVI-ReID setting.

ai.tldr: 提出因果引导对齐框架，用于无监督视频跨模态行人重识别，利用时序先验解决模态偏差和粒度失衡问题。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖昂贵的跨模态标注，且图像无监督方法直接扩展到视频时性能不佳，存在身份混淆和模态粒度失衡。
ai.method: 1. 因果干预预热：利用时序和跨模态身份一致性进行序列级干预，抑制虚假相关。2. 原型引导不确定性细化：以可靠可见原型引导重组欠聚类红外表征，进行不确定性感知的粗到细对齐。
ai.result: 在HITSZ-VCM和BUPTCampus基准上显著优于现有无监督跨模态方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为无标注视频跨模态重识别提供了无需人工标注的解决方案，提升了模型在模态偏差和聚类失衡下的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用视频固有的时序一致性作为自然监督信号，替代人工标注。
idea_spark.risk: 对视频质量及跨模态时序对齐要求较高，在低帧率或严重遮挡场景可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将因果推理与无监督聚类结合，为缓解模态偏差提供了新视角。

en.tldr: Proposes a Causal Bootstrapped Alignment framework for unsupervised video-based visible-infrared person re-identification, leveraging temporal priors to address modality bias and granularity imbalance.
en.motivation: Existing methods rely on expensive cross-modality annotations, and directly extending image-based unsupervised methods to video leads to suboptimal performance due to identity confusion and cross-modality clustering granularity imbalance.
en.method: 1. Causal Intervention Warm-up: performs sequence-level causal intervention using temporal and cross-modality identity consistency to suppress spurious correlations. 2. Prototype-Guided Uncertainty Refinement: employs a coarse-to-fine alignment strategy to reorganize under-clustered infrared representations guided by reliable visible prototypes with uncertainty-aware supervision.
en.result: Significantly outperforms existing unsupervised cross-modality methods on the HITSZ-VCM and BUPTCampus benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Provides an annotation-free solution for video-based cross-modality re-identification, improving robustness against modality bias and clustering imbalance.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage inherent temporal consistency in videos as natural supervision to replace manual annotations.
idea_spark.risk: Requires high video quality and cross-modality temporal alignment; may fail in low-frame-rate or heavily occluded scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining causal inference with unsupervised clustering offers a novel perspective for mitigating modality bias.

papers.title: SWNet: A Cross-Spectral Network for Camouflaged Weed Detection
papers.summary: This paper presents SWNet, a bimodal end-to-end cross-spectral network specifically engineered for the detection of camouflaged weeds in dense agricultural environments. Plant camouflage, characterized by homochromatic blending where invasive species mimic the phenotypic traits of primary crops, poses a significant challenge for traditional computer vision systems. To overcome these limitations, SWNet utilizes a Pyramid Vision Transformer v2 backbone to capture long-range dependencies and a Bimodal Gated Fusion Module to dynamically integrate Visible and Near-Infrared information. By leveraging the physiological differences in chlorophyll reflectance captured in the NIR spectrum, the proposed architecture effectively discriminates targets that are otherwise indistinguishable in the visible range. Furthermore, an Edge-Aware Refinement module is employed to produce sharper object boundaries and reduce structural ambiguity. Experimental results on the Weeds-Banana dataset indicate that SWNet outperforms ten state-of-the-art methods. The study demonstrates that the integration of cross-spectral data and boundary-guided refinement is essential for high segmentation accuracy in complex crop canopies. The code is available on GitHub: https://cod-espol.github.io/SWNet/

ai.tldr: SWNet是一种双模态端到端跨光谱网络，专门用于在密集农业环境中检测伪装杂草。它通过融合可见光和近红外信息，利用叶绿素反射差异来区分目标，并采用边缘感知细化模块提升边界精度。
ai.motivation: 植物伪装（入侵物种模仿主要作物的表型特征）给传统计算机视觉系统带来了重大挑战，需要新的方法来在复杂作物冠层中实现高精度分割。
ai.method: 使用Pyramid Vision Transformer v2主干捕获长程依赖，通过双模态门控融合模块动态整合可见光和近红外信息，并采用边缘感知细化模块生成更清晰的对象边界。
ai.result: 在Weeds-Banana数据集上的实验表明，SWNet优于十种最先进的方法，证明了跨光谱数据和边界引导细化对于复杂作物冠层中高分割精度的必要性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种有效的农业杂草检测解决方案，特别适用于处理伪装和密集环境。代码已在GitHub上开源。

idea_spark.idea: 利用不同光谱波段（如近红外）捕获的生理差异（如叶绿素反射）来增强在可见光波段难以区分的目标的检测能力。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于特定光谱数据（近红外）的采集设备，可能增加部署成本和复杂性。在光照条件变化或不同作物品种下性能可能波动。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将Transformer的长程依赖建模能力与特定领域的先验知识（如光谱差异、边界信息）相结合，可以提升特定视觉任务的性能。

It fuses Visible and Near-Infrared information to leverage chlorophyll reflectance differences for target discrimination and employs an Edge-Aware Refinement module to improve boundary accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage physiological differences (e.g., chlorophyll reflectance) captured in different spectral bands (e.g., NIR) to enhance the detection of targets that are indistinguishable in the visible spectrum.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on specific spectral data (NIR) acquisition equipment may increase deployment cost and complexity. Performance may vary under changing lighting conditions or with different crop varieties.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining the long-range dependency modeling capability of Transformers with domain-specific prior knowledge (e.g., spectral differences, boundary information) can improve performance on specific vision tasks.

papers.title: LP$^{2}$DH: A Locality-Preserving Pixel-Difference Hashing Framework for Dynamic Texture Recognition
papers.summary: Spatiotemporal Local Binary Pattern (STLBP) is a widely used dynamic texture descriptor, but it suffers from extremely high dimensionality. To tackle this, STLBP features are often extracted on three orthogonal planes, which sacrifice inter-plane correlation. In this work, we propose a Locality-Preserving Pixel-Difference Hashing (LP$^{2}$DH) framework that jointly encodes pixel differences in the full spatiotemporal neighbourhood. LP$^{2}$DH transforms Pixel-Difference Vectors (PDVs) into compact binary codes with maximal discriminative power. Furthermore, we incorporate a locality-preserving embedding to maintain the PDVs' local structure before and after hashing. Then, a curvilinear search strategy is utilized to jointly optimize the hashing matrix and binary codes via gradient descent on the Stiefel manifold. After hashing, dictionary learning is applied to encode the binary vectors into codewords, and the resulting histogram is utilized as the final feature representation. The proposed LP$^{2}$DH achieves state-of-the-art performance on three major dynamic texture recognition benchmarks: 99.80% against DT-GoogleNet's 98.93% on UCLA, 98.52% against HoGF$^{3D}$'s 97.63% on DynTex++, and 96.19% compared to STS's 95.00% on YUPENN. The source code is available at: https://github.com/drx770/LP2DH.

ai.tldr: 提出LP²DH框架，通过局部保持的像素差哈希将动态纹理的高维特征编码为紧凑二进制码，在三个基准数据集上达到SOTA性能。
ai.motivation: STLBP动态纹理描述符维度极高，现有方法在三个正交平面上提取特征牺牲了平面间相关性。
ai.method: 联合编码全时空邻域的像素差，通过局部保持嵌入保持哈希前后结构，在Stiefel流形上联合优化哈希矩阵与二进制码，最后用字典学习生成直方图特征。
ai.result: 在UCLA、DynTex++、YUPENN数据集上分别达到99.80%、98.52%、96.19%的准确率，超越现有最佳方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为动态纹理识别提供了一种高效的特征哈希框架，显著降低维度同时保持判别力。

idea_spark.idea: 将像素差向量映射到二进制空间时显式保持局部几何结构。
idea_spark.risk: 方法计算复杂度较高，需优化实现效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合流形学习与哈希学习，为特征压缩问题提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes LP²DH framework that encodes dynamic texture features into compact binary codes via locality-preserving pixel-difference hashing, achieving SOTA performance on three benchmarks.
en.motivation: STLBP dynamic texture descriptor suffers from extremely high dimensionality, and existing three-orthogonal-plane extraction sacrifices inter-plane correlation.
en.method: Jointly encodes pixel differences in full spatiotemporal neighborhood, preserves local structure via locality-preserving embedding, jointly optimizes hashing matrix and binary codes on Stiefel manifold, and applies dictionary learning for final histogram representation.
en.result: Achieves 99.80% on UCLA (vs DT-GoogleNet 98.93%), 98.52% on DynTex++ (vs HoGF³D 97.63%), and 96.19% on YUPENN (vs STS 95.00%).
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient feature hashing framework for dynamic texture recognition that significantly reduces dimensionality while maintaining discriminative power.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly preserve local geometric structure when mapping pixel-difference vectors to binary space.
idea_spark.risk: Method has relatively high computational complexity requiring implementation optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining manifold learning with hashing learning offers new perspectives for feature compression problems.

papers.title: Dark solitons in nonlinear Su-Schrieffer-Heeger lattices
papers.summary: The introduction of nonlinearities into lattices with topological band structures has led to the discovery of various types of solitons. The Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) lattice, as the most fundamental topological model, has been extended into the nonlinear regime. In particular, nonlinear edge states and bulk solitons exhibiting intensity humps against a zero background have been extensively studied in nonlinear SSH lattices. In this paper, we systematically investigate dark solitons in nonlinear SSH lattices. These dark solitons maintain a nonzero and constant background, featuring intensity dips either in the bulk of the lattice or at its edges, and residing spectrally in the semi-infinite gap or the middle finite gap. Regardless of the specific type of dark soliton, the intensity dip remains wellpreserved and is not affected by the band structure of the original linear lattice. Although the dark solitons we have identified are generally dynamically unstable across a broad range of parameters, several types exhibit linear stability when the intracell coupling is much larger than the intercell coupling. Our findings may provide valuable insights for the exploration of novel types of solitons in nonlinear topological lattices.

ai.tldr: 本文系统研究了非线性SSH晶格中的暗孤子，这些孤子在非零恒定背景下呈现强度凹陷，位于体区或边缘，光谱位于半无限带隙或中间有限带隙。
ai.motivation: 非线性拓扑晶格中已广泛研究亮孤子和非线性边缘态，但对暗孤子的系统研究尚不充分。
ai.method: 在非线性Su-Schrieffer-Heeger晶格模型中，理论分析并数值研究具有强度凹陷的暗孤子解及其稳定性。
ai.result: 发现暗孤子在多数参数下动态不稳定，但当胞内耦合远大于胞间耦合时，部分类型呈现线性稳定性；强度凹陷不受原始线性晶格能带结构影响。
ai.help_to_user: 为非线性拓扑晶格中新型孤子的探索提供了理论依据和分类框架。

idea_spark.idea: 在其他拓扑晶格模型（如Chern绝缘体）中引入非线性并探索暗孤子变体。
idea_spark.risk: 实验实现需要精确控制非线性强度和耦合参数，动态不稳定性可能限制实际应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将暗孤子作为拓扑保护的信息载体或传感探针，利用其强度凹陷对局部扰动的敏感性。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将暗孤子作为拓扑保护的信息载体或传感探针，利用其强度凹陷对局部扰动的敏感性。

en.tldr: This paper systematically investigates dark solitons in nonlinear SSH lattices, which feature intensity dips against a nonzero constant background, reside in the bulk or at edges, and spectrally occupy the semi-infinite gap or middle finite gap.
en.motivation: While bright solitons and nonlinear edge states have been extensively studied in nonlinear topological lattices, systematic investigation of dark solitons remains limited.
en.method: Theoretical analysis and numerical study of dark soliton solutions with intensity dips in the nonlinear Su-Schrieffer-Heeger lattice model, including stability analysis.
en.result: Dark solitons are generally dynamically unstable across broad parameters but exhibit linear stability when intracell coupling significantly exceeds intercell coupling; intensity dips are preserved independently of the original linear lattice's band structure.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretical foundation and classification framework for exploring novel soliton types in nonlinear topological lattices.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce nonlinearity into other topological lattice models (e.g., Chern insulators) and explore dark soliton variants.
idea_spark.risk: Experimental realization requires precise control of nonlinear strength and coupling parameters; dynamical instability may limit practical applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Utilize dark solitons as topologically protected information carriers or sensing probes, leveraging their intensity dip sensitivity to local perturbations.

papers.title: Using Large Language Models and Knowledge Graphs to Improve the Interpretability of Machine Learning Models in Manufacturing
papers.summary: Explaining Machine Learning (ML) results in a transparent and user-friendly manner remains a challenging task of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). In this paper, we present a method to enhance the interpretability of ML models by using a Knowledge Graph (KG). We store domain-specific data along with ML results and their corresponding explanations, establishing a structured connection between domain knowledge and ML insights. To make these insights accessible to users, we designed a selective retrieval method in which relevant triplets are extracted from the KG and processed by a Large Language Model (LLM) to generate user-friendly explanations of ML results. We evaluated our method in a manufacturing environment using the XAI Question Bank. Beyond standard questions, we introduce more complex, tailored questions that highlight the strengths of our approach. We evaluated 33 questions, analyzing responses using quantitative metrics such as accuracy and consistency, as well as qualitative ones such as clarity and usefulness. Our contribution is both theoretical and practical: from a theoretical perspective, we present a novel approach for effectively enabling LLMs to dynamically access a KG in order to improve the explainability of ML results. From a practical perspective, we provide empirical evidence showing that such explanations can be successfully applied in real-world manufacturing environments, supporting better decision-making in manufacturing processes.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合知识图谱与大语言模型的方法，提升制造业中机器学习模型的可解释性，通过结构化存储领域知识与ML结果，并利用LLM生成用户友好的解释。
ai.motivation: 在制造业中，以透明且用户友好的方式解释机器学习结果仍是可解释人工智能（XAI）的挑战。现有方法往往缺乏与领域知识的深度结合，导致解释不够直观或实用。
ai.method: 构建知识图谱存储领域特定数据、ML结果及其解释；设计选择性检索方法从KG中提取相关三元组；利用大语言模型处理这些三元组，生成用户友好的ML结果解释。
ai.result: 在制造业环境中使用XAI问题库进行评估，涵盖33个问题。定量指标（准确性、一致性）和定性指标（清晰度、有用性）均显示该方法能有效生成高质量解释，支持实际决策。
ai.help_to_user: 为制造业从业者提供更直观、可理解的ML模型解释，帮助其理解模型预测依据，从而做出更明智的工艺或生产决策。

idea_spark.idea: 将静态知识图谱与动态大语言模型生成能力结合，实现领域知识增强的解释生成框架。
idea_spark.risk: 知识图谱的构建质量与覆盖范围直接影响解释的准确性；LLM可能产生与KG事实不一致的“幻觉”解释。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将符号知识（KG）与神经生成（LLM）协同用于可解释性任务，平衡结构化严谨性与自然语言灵活性。

idea_spark.risk: 知识图谱的构建质量与覆盖范围直接影响解释的准确性；LLM可能产生与KG事实不一致的“幻觉”解释。

en.tldr: Proposes a method combining knowledge graphs and large language models to improve the interpretability of machine learning models in manufacturing, by structuring domain knowledge with ML results and using LLMs to generate user-friendly explanations.
en.motivation: Explaining machine learning results in a transparent and user-friendly manner remains a challenge in Explainable AI (XAI) for manufacturing. Existing approaches often lack deep integration with domain knowledge, leading to explanations that are not intuitive or practical enough.
en.method: Construct a knowledge graph to store domain-specific data, ML results, and their explanations; design a selective retrieval method to extract relevant triplets from the KG; use a large language model to process these triplets and generate user-friendly explanations of ML results.
en.result: Evaluated in a manufacturing environment using the XAI Question Bank, covering 33 questions. Both quantitative metrics (accuracy, consistency) and qualitative metrics (clarity, usefulness) show the method effectively generates high-quality explanations, supporting real-world decision-making.
en.help_to_user: Provides manufacturing practitioners with more intuitive and understandable explanations of ML model predictions, helping them comprehend the rationale behind model outputs for better process or production decisions.

idea_spark.idea: Combine static knowledge graphs with the dynamic generative capabilities of large language models to create a domain-knowledge-enhanced explanation generation framework.
idea_spark.risk: The quality and coverage of the knowledge graph directly affect explanation accuracy; LLMs may generate 'hallucinated' explanations inconsistent with KG facts.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the synergy of symbolic knowledge (KG) and neural generation (LLM) for explainability tasks, balancing structured rigor with natural language flexibility.

papers.title: SCHK-HTC: Sibling Contrastive Learning with Hierarchical Knowledge-Aware Prompt Tuning for Hierarchical Text Classification
papers.summary: Few-shot Hierarchical Text Classification (few-shot HTC) is a challenging task that involves mapping texts to a predefined tree-structured label hierarchy under data-scarce conditions. While current approaches utilize structural constraints from the label hierarchy to maintain parent-child prediction consistency, they face a critical bottleneck, the difficulty in distinguishing semantically similar sibling classes due to insufficient domain knowledge. We introduce an innovative method named Sibling Contrastive Learning with Hierarchical Knowledge-aware Prompt Tuning for few-shot HTC tasks (SCHK-HTC). Our work enhances the model's perception of subtle differences between sibling classes at deeper levels, rather than just enforcing hierarchical rules. Specifically, we propose a novel framework featuring two core components: a hierarchical knowledge extraction module and a sibling contrastive learning mechanism. This design guides model to encode discriminative features at each hierarchy level, thus improving the separability of confusable classes. Our approach achieves superior performance across three benchmark datasets, surpassing existing state-of-the-art methods in most cases. Our code is available at https://github.com/happywinder/SCHK-HTC.

ai.tldr: 提出SCHK-HTC方法，通过层级知识感知提示调优和兄弟类对比学习，解决少样本层级文本分类中语义相似兄弟类难以区分的问题。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖标签层级结构约束保证父子预测一致性，但难以区分语义相似的兄弟类，缺乏领域知识感知。
ai.method: 包含两个核心组件：层级知识提取模块和兄弟对比学习机制，引导模型编码每层级的判别性特征。
ai.result: 在三个基准数据集上取得优越性能，在多数情况下超越现有最先进方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为数据稀缺条件下的层级文本分类任务提供了更精细的兄弟类区分能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将标签层级结构知识显式编码到提示中，并结合对比学习增强兄弟类区分。
idea_spark.risk: 在层级过深或兄弟类数量极多时，计算和表示复杂度可能增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将结构知识注入少样本学习，通过对比学习解决语义相似类混淆问题。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将结构知识注入少样本学习，通过对比学习解决语义相似类混淆问题。

en.tldr: Proposes SCHK-HTC, using hierarchical knowledge-aware prompt tuning and sibling contrastive learning to address the difficulty in distinguishing semantically similar sibling classes in few-shot hierarchical text classification.
en.motivation: Existing methods rely on label hierarchy constraints for parent-child consistency but struggle to distinguish semantically similar sibling classes due to insufficient domain knowledge.
en.method: Features two core components: a hierarchical knowledge extraction module and a sibling contrastive learning mechanism, guiding the model to encode discriminative features at each hierarchy level.
en.result: Achieves superior performance across three benchmark datasets, surpassing existing state-of-the-art methods in most cases.
en.help_to_user: Provides finer-grained sibling class discrimination for hierarchical text classification under data-scarce conditions.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly encode label hierarchy knowledge into prompts and combine with contrastive learning to enhance sibling class distinction.
idea_spark.risk: Computational and representational complexity may increase with very deep hierarchies or extremely large numbers of sibling classes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires injecting structural knowledge into few-shot learning and using contrastive learning to address semantic similarity confusion.

papers.title: Hero-Mamba: Mamba-based Dual Domain Learning for Underwater Image Enhancement
papers.summary: Underwater images often suffer from severe degradation, such as color distortion, low contrast, and blurred details, due to light absorption and scattering in water. While learning-based methods like CNNs and Transformers have shown promise, they face critical limitations: CNNs struggle to model the long-range dependencies needed for non-uniform degradation, and Transformers incur quadratic computational complexity, making them inefficient for high-resolution images. To address these challenges, we propose Hero-Mamba, a novel Mamba-based network that achieves efficient dual-domain learning for underwater image enhancement. Our approach uniquely processes information from both the spatial domain (RGB image) and the spectral domain (FFT components) in parallel. This dual-domain input allows the network to decouple degradation factors, separating color/brightness information from texture/noise. The core of our network utilizes Mamba-based SS2D blocks to capture global receptive fields and long-range dependencies with linear complexity, overcoming the limitations of both CNNs and Transformers. Furthermore, we introduce a ColorFusion block, guided by a background light prior, to restore color information with high fidelity. Extensive experiments on the LSUI and UIEB benchmark datasets demonstrate that Hero-Mamba outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Notably, our model achieves a PSNR of 25.802 and an SSIM of 0.913 on LSUI, validating its superior performance and generalization capabilities.

ai.tldr: Hero-Mamba是一种基于Mamba架构的双域学习网络，用于高效的水下图像增强，通过并行处理空间域和频域信息来解耦退化因素。
ai.motivation: 水下图像因光线吸收和散射而存在严重的颜色失真、低对比度和细节模糊。现有CNN方法难以建模长距离依赖，Transformer则计算复杂度高，不适用于高分辨率图像。
ai.method: 提出Hero-Mamba网络，并行处理RGB空间域和FFT频域信息，利用Mamba-based SS2D块以线性复杂度捕获全局感受野，并引入由背景光先验引导的ColorFusion块来恢复颜色。
ai.result: 在LSUI和UIEB基准数据集上超越现有方法，在LSUI上达到PSNR 25.802和SSIM 0.913，验证了其优越性能和泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种计算高效的水下图像增强方案，适用于需要处理高分辨率水下图像的视觉应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将空间颜色信息与频域纹理/噪声信息分离处理，结合先验引导的颜色恢复模块。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于水下图像特定的背景光先验，可能在其他退化场景中需要调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将序列建模的高效架构（Mamba）与多域表示学习结合，用于计算敏感的视觉任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将空间颜色信息与频域纹理/噪声信息分离处理，结合先验引导的颜色恢复模块。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于水下图像特定的背景光先验，可能在其他退化场景中需要调整。

en.tldr: Hero-Mamba is a Mamba-based dual-domain learning network for efficient underwater image enhancement, decoupling degradation factors by parallel processing of spatial and spectral domain information.
en.motivation: Underwater images suffer from severe degradation like color distortion, low contrast, and blurred details due to light absorption and scattering. Existing CNN methods struggle with long-range dependencies, while Transformers have quadratic complexity, making them inefficient for high-resolution images.
en.method: Proposes the Hero-Mamba network that processes RGB spatial domain and FFT spectral domain information in parallel. It uses Mamba-based SS2D blocks to capture global receptive fields with linear complexity and introduces a ColorFusion block guided by a background light prior for color restoration.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on LSUI and UIEB benchmark datasets, achieving PSNR 25.802 and SSIM 0.913 on LSUI, demonstrating superior performance and generalization.
en.help_to_user: Provides a computationally efficient solution for underwater image enhancement, suitable for vision applications requiring high-resolution underwater image processing.

idea_spark.idea: Separate spatial color information from spectral texture/noise information, combined with a prior-guided color restoration module.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on underwater-specific background light prior; may require adaptation for other degradation scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining efficient sequence modeling architectures (Mamba) with multi-domain representation learning for computationally sensitive vision tasks.

papers.title: Robust Transmission Design for RIS-Assisted High-Speed Train Communication Coverage Enhancement With Imperfect Cascaded Channels
papers.summary: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) has recently been gained attention as an effective technique improving the coverage and performance of communication systems by creating additional communication links. Deployment of RIS is crucial for overcoming signal coverage limitations, especially in high-speed train (HST) scenarios. Considerable research has been performed assuming perfect channel state information (CSI). However, due to the rapidly time-varying fading channels and feedback delays, achieving perfect CSI at the base station (BS) is not feasible in the HST scenarios. To tackle this problem, this paper investigates a robust design strategy for RIS-aided HST communication coverage enhancement, particularly focusing on cascaded BS-RIS-user channels at BS (CBRUB). The study explores the optimization problem under two types distinct of models: centered on minimizing transmit power subject to worst-case rate constraints within the bounded CSI error (BCSIE) model, and the other focusing on outage probability (OP) constraints under the statistical CSI error (SCSIE) model. We use the S-procedure to approximate the non-convex (NC) constraints, converting the worst-case rate constraints into linear matrix inequalities. Additionally, the Bernstein-type inequality is applied to transform the OP constraints into second-order cone constraints and linear inequalities. The simulation analysis results show that CBRUB errors have a significant effect on system performance compared to direct CSI errors.

ai.tldr: 针对高速列车场景中不完美的级联信道，研究RIS辅助通信覆盖增强的鲁棒传输设计，提出基于有界CSI误差和统计CSI误差两种模型的优化方法。
ai.motivation: 高速列车场景中信道快速时变且存在反馈延迟，基站难以获得完美信道状态信息，现有基于完美CSI的RIS研究不适用，需设计鲁棒传输方案。
ai.method: 采用两种信道误差模型：有界CSI误差模型下以最坏情况速率约束最小化发射功率；统计CSI误差模型下以中断概率为约束。使用S-过程处理非凸约束，伯恩斯坦型不等式转换中断概率约束。
ai.result: 仿真表明级联BS-RIS-用户信道误差对系统性能影响显著大于直接信道误差，所提鲁棒设计能有效应对信道不确定性。
ai.help_to_user: 为高速移动场景中RIS部署提供了实用的鲁棒优化框架，特别关注级联信道不完美性的影响。

idea_spark.idea: 将级联信道误差与直接信道误差分离建模，针对性设计鲁棒方案。
idea_spark.risk: 实际高速场景中信道误差统计特性可能偏离假设模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索机器学习方法在线估计误差边界，或设计自适应鲁棒度调整机制。

idea_spark.idea: 将级联信道误差与直接信道误差分离建模，针对性设计鲁棒方案。
idea_spark.risk: 实际高速场景中信道误差统计特性可能偏离假设模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索机器学习方法在线估计误差边界，或设计自适应鲁棒度调整机制。

en.tldr: Investigates robust transmission design for RIS-assisted high-speed train communication coverage enhancement with imperfect cascaded channels, proposing optimization methods under bounded and statistical CSI error models.
en.motivation: In high-speed train scenarios, rapidly time-varying channels and feedback delays make perfect CSI infeasible at the base station, necessitating robust designs beyond perfect-CSI assumptions.
en.method: Two CSI error models: bounded error model with worst-case rate constraints for transmit power minimization, and statistical error model with outage probability constraints. Uses S-procedure to handle non-convex constraints and Bernstein-type inequality to transform outage constraints.
en.result: Simulations show cascaded BS-RIS-user channel errors significantly impact system performance more than direct channel errors, and the proposed robust design effectively handles channel uncertainties.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical robust optimization framework for RIS deployment in high-mobility scenarios, with specific focus on cascaded channel imperfections.

idea_spark.idea: Separately model cascaded vs. direct channel errors for targeted robust design.
idea_spark.risk: Actual channel error statistics in high-speed scenarios may deviate from assumed models.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore machine learning for online error bound estimation or adaptive robustness adjustment mechanisms.

papers.title: Target-Oriented Pretraining Data Selection via Neuron-Activated Graph
papers.summary: Everyday tasks come with a target, and pretraining models around this target is what turns them into experts. In this paper, we study target-oriented language model (LM) pretraining by introducing Neuron-Activated Graph Ranking (NAG-based Ranking), a training-free and interpretable framework for target pretraining data selection. Rather than using black-box representations, our approach directly characterizes each target input by a sparse set of high-impact neurons in any off-the-shelf LLMs. Concretely, we quantify neuron impact and select the most influential neurons across layers into a compact Neuron-Activated Graph (NAG), and rank candidate data by NAG similarity to target examples. We conduct experiments across six benchmarks, where our NAG-based Ranking improves target-oriented pretraining by 4.9% on average over random sampling, and also outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by 5.3% accuracy on HellaSwag. It also remains effective under a more applicable multi-target setting, where our best setup surpasses two baselines by 1.1% and 4.1%, respectively. Furthermore, we provide a comprehensive analysis on why and how our NAG works, e.g., deactivating NAG-selected neurons (only 0.12% of all) causes a 23.5% performance collapse, and restricting NAG to the final layer incurs a 4.1% average drop, indicating that NAG captures a sparse "functional backbone" for learning target features. We release the code at https://github.com/asillycat/NAG.

ai.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经元激活图（NAG）的训练免费、可解释的目标导向预训练数据选择框架，通过识别目标输入在现成大语言模型中的关键神经元来筛选数据，在多个基准上显著提升目标任务的性能。
ai.motivation: 日常任务通常有特定目标，围绕目标进行预训练可使模型成为专家。现有方法多依赖黑盒表征，缺乏可解释性。
ai.method: 提出神经元激活图排序（NAG-based Ranking）框架：量化神经元对目标输入的影响，选取各层最具影响力的神经元构建紧凑的神经元激活图（NAG），通过计算候选数据与目标示例的NAG相似度进行排序。
ai.result: 在六个基准测试中，NAG排序相比随机采样平均提升4.9%，在HellaSwag上比最先进基线高5.3%准确率；在多目标场景下仍有效，最佳设置分别超越两个基线1.1%和4.1%。分析表明，仅停用NAG选中的神经元（占总数0.12%）会导致性能下降23.5%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种无需训练、可解释的数据选择方法，帮助用户针对特定目标高效筛选预训练数据，提升模型专业化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用现成模型的内部神经元激活模式作为数据筛选的代理信号，避免重新训练或复杂表征计算。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于特定预训练模型的神经元响应，可能受模型架构或训练数据偏差影响；稀疏神经元选择可能忽略分布式表征中的重要信息。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从模型内部机制（如神经元激活、注意力模式）出发设计可解释的数据处理流程，而非仅依赖输出层表征。

en.tldr: This paper introduces Neuron-Activated Graph Ranking (NAG-based Ranking), a training-free and interpretable framework for target-oriented pretraining data selection. It identifies key neurons in off-the-shelf LLMs for target inputs, constructing a Neuron-Activated Graph (NAG) to rank candidate data, significantly improving performance on target tasks across multiple benchmarks.
en.motivation: Everyday tasks come with a target, and pretraining models around this target turns them into experts. Existing methods often rely on black-box representations and lack interpretability.
en.method: Proposes the NAG-based Ranking framework: quantify neuron impact on target inputs, select the most influential neurons across layers into a compact Neuron-Activated Graph (NAG), and rank candidate data by NAG similarity to target examples.
en.result: On six benchmarks, NAG-based Ranking improves target-oriented pretraining by 4.9% on average over random sampling, and outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by 5.3% accuracy on HellaSwag. It remains effective under multi-target settings, with the best setup surpassing two baselines by 1.1% and 4.1%. Analysis shows that deactivating NAG-selected neurons (only 0.12% of all) causes a 23.5% performance drop.
en.help_to_user: Provides a training-free, interpretable data selection method to help users efficiently filter pretraining data for specific targets, enhancing model specialization.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage internal neuron activation patterns of off-the-shelf models as proxy signals for data selection, avoiding retraining or complex representation computations.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on neuron responses of specific pretrained models, which may be affected by model architecture or training data bias; sparse neuron selection might overlook important information in distributed representations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires designing interpretable data processing pipelines from internal model mechanisms (e.g., neuron activation, attention patterns) rather than relying solely on output-layer representations.

papers.title: LACE: Lattice Attention for Cross-thread Exploration
papers.summary: Current large language models reason in isolation. Although it is common to sample multiple reasoning paths in parallel, these trajectories do not interact, and often fail in the same redundant ways. We introduce LACE, a framework that transforms reasoning from a collection of independent trials into a coordinated, parallel process. By repurposing the model architecture to enable cross-thread attention, LACE allows concurrent reasoning paths to share intermediate insights and correct one another during inference. A central challenge is the absence of natural training data that exhibits such collaborative behavior. We address this gap with a synthetic data pipeline that explicitly teaches models to communicate and error-correct across threads. Experiments show that this unified exploration substantially outperforms standard parallel search, improving reasoning accuracy by over 7 points. Our results suggest that large language models can be more effective when parallel reasoning paths are allowed to interact.

ai.tldr: LACE框架通过跨线程注意力机制，让并行推理路径在推理过程中交互共享中间见解并相互纠错，显著提升大语言模型的推理准确率。
ai.motivation: 当前大语言模型的并行推理路径相互独立、缺乏交互，容易以相同冗余方式失败，限制了推理效率。
ai.method: 重新利用模型架构实现跨线程注意力，使并发推理线程能够相互关注；通过合成数据管道显式训练模型进行跨线程通信和纠错。
ai.result: 统一探索框架显著优于标准并行搜索，推理准确率提升超过7个百分点。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要提升大模型并行推理可靠性的研究者提供了可交互的架构思路与数据生成方法。

idea_spark.idea: 在模型推理时构建轻量级通信层，让并行实例实时交换局部状态信息。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据与真实场景的分布差异可能导致泛化不足；跨线程通信引入额外计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将多智能体协作思想引入单模型内部并行化，用架构修改替代单纯增加采样数。

en.tldr: The LACE framework enables parallel reasoning paths to interact via cross-thread attention, sharing intermediate insights and correcting each other during inference, significantly improving LLM reasoning accuracy.
en.motivation: Current parallel reasoning paths in large language models operate in isolation, often failing in redundant ways, limiting reasoning efficiency.
en.method: Repurpose model architecture to enable cross-thread attention for concurrent reasoning threads; use a synthetic data pipeline to explicitly train models for inter-thread communication and error correction.
en.result: Unified exploration substantially outperforms standard parallel search, improving reasoning accuracy by over 7 percentage points.
en.help_to_user: Provides an interactive architecture approach and data generation method for researchers needing to improve the reliability of parallel reasoning in large models.

idea_spark.idea: Construct a lightweight communication layer during model inference to allow parallel instances to exchange local state information in real time.
idea_spark.risk: Distribution mismatch between synthetic and real-world data may limit generalization; cross-thread communication introduces additional computational overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires introducing multi-agent collaboration concepts into intra-model parallelism, using architectural modifications rather than merely increasing sample count.

papers.title: Saturation-Aware Space-Variant Blind Image Deblurring
papers.summary: This paper presents a novel saturation aware space variant blind image deblurring framework designed to address challenges posed by saturated pixels in deblurring under high dynamic range and low light conditions. The proposed approach effectively segments the image based on blur intensity and proximity to saturation, leveraging a pre estimated Light Spread Function to mitigate stray light effects. By accurately estimating the true radiance of saturated regions using the dark channel prior, our method enhances the deblurring process without introducing artifacts like ringing. Experimental evaluations on both synthetic and real world datasets demonstrate that the framework improves deblurring outcomes across various scenarios showcasing superior performance compared to state of the art saturation-aware and general purpose methods. This adaptability highlights the framework potential integration with existing and emerging blind image deblurring techniques.

ai.tldr: 提出一种饱和度感知的空间变化盲图像去模糊框架，专门处理高动态范围和低光条件下饱和像素带来的挑战。
ai.motivation: 在高动态范围和低光条件下，饱和像素会严重影响去模糊效果，现有方法对此处理不足。
ai.method: 基于模糊强度和接近饱和程度分割图像，利用预估计的光扩散函数减少杂散光，并使用暗通道先验准确估计饱和区域的真实辐射度。
ai.result: 在合成和真实数据集上验证，相比现有饱和度感知和通用方法，去模糊效果更优且不引入振铃等伪影。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理含饱和区域的模糊图像提供了专用框架，可提升高动态范围场景的恢复质量。

idea_spark.idea: 将图像按模糊强度和饱和程度分区处理，针对性估计真实辐射度。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖预估计的光扩散函数准确性，在复杂光照条件下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将物理光照模型与先验知识结合，解决特定退化模式的问题。

en.tldr: Proposes a saturation-aware space-variant blind image deblurring framework to address challenges from saturated pixels in high dynamic range and low-light conditions.
en.motivation: Saturated pixels severely degrade deblurring performance in high dynamic range and low-light scenarios, which existing methods handle inadequately.
en.method: Segments images based on blur intensity and proximity to saturation, uses a pre-estimated Light Spread Function to mitigate stray light, and estimates true radiance of saturated regions via dark channel prior.
en.result: Demonstrates improved deblurring on synthetic and real-world datasets, outperforming state-of-the-art saturation-aware and general-purpose methods without introducing ringing artifacts.
en.help_to_user: Provides a dedicated framework for deblurring images with saturated regions, enhancing restoration quality in high dynamic range scenes.

idea_spark.idea: Partition images by blur intensity and saturation level for targeted radiance estimation.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accuracy of pre-estimated Light Spread Function, which may fail under complex lighting.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires integrating physical lighting models with prior knowledge to address specific degradation patterns.

papers.title: Safety Filtering with an Infinite Number of Constraints
papers.summary: Control barrier functions (CBFs) provide a rigorous framework for designing controllers enforcing safety constraints. While CBF theory is well-developed for a finite number of safety constraints, certain applications, e.g., backup CBFs, require an infinite number of constraints. Despite the practical success of CBFs, several fundamental questions remain unanswered when safe sets are defined with an infinite numbers of constraints, including: necessary and sufficient conditions for forward set invariance, the actual definition of CBFs associated with these sets, the regularity properties of the resulting controllers, and the ability to reduce a collection of infinite constraints to a finite number. This paper addresses these questions by extending CBF theory to the infinite constraint setting. We identify regularity conditions under which Nagumo's Theorem reduces to barrier-like inequalities and when the associated CBF controllers are at least continuous. We further connect these results to optimal-decay CBFs, bridging theoretical conditions for invariance and practical instantiations of the resulting controller. Finally, we illustrate how the developed theory addresses limitations of backup CBFs.

ai.tldr: 本文扩展了控制屏障函数理论，以处理无限个安全约束的情况，建立了保证集合前向不变性的条件，并分析了所得控制器的正则性。
ai.motivation: 现有CBF理论主要针对有限个安全约束，而某些应用（如备份CBF）需要处理无限个约束，相关基本问题尚未解决。
ai.method: 通过扩展CBF理论至无限约束场景，识别使Nagumo定理简化为类屏障不等式的正则条件，并连接最优衰减CBF。
ai.result: 建立了无限约束下集合前向不变性的充要条件，证明了相关CBF控制器至少连续，并展示了如何将无限约束集简化为有限个。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理无限安全约束的控制系统提供了理论框架，解决了备份CBF等应用中的局限性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用正则条件将无限约束问题转化为可处理的屏障不等式
idea_spark.risk: 理论条件在实际系统中的验证可能具有挑战性
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索将类似框架应用于其他具有连续参数依赖约束的控制问题

en.tldr: This paper extends control barrier function theory to handle an infinite number of safety constraints, establishing conditions for forward set invariance and analyzing regularity of resulting controllers.
en.motivation: Existing CBF theory is well-developed for finite constraints, but applications like backup CBFs require infinite constraints, with fundamental questions unanswered.
en.method: Extends CBF theory to infinite constraints, identifies regularity conditions under which Nagumo's Theorem reduces to barrier-like inequalities, and connects to optimal-decay CBFs.
en.result: Provides necessary and sufficient conditions for forward invariance with infinite constraints, shows associated CBF controllers are at least continuous, and demonstrates reduction of infinite constraints to finite sets.
en.help_to_user: Offers a theoretical framework for control systems with infinite safety constraints, addressing limitations in applications like backup CBFs.

idea_spark.idea: Using regularity conditions to transform infinite constraint problems into tractable barrier inequalities
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical conditions may be challenging to verify in practical systems
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore applying similar frameworks to other control problems with continuously parameterized constraints

papers.title: Decoding Algorithms for Tensor Codes
papers.summary: Tensor codes are a generalisation of matrix codes. Such codes are defined as subspaces of order-r tensors for which the ambient space is endowed with the tensor-rank as a metric. A class of these codes was introduced by Roth, who also outlined a decoding algorithm for low tensor-rank errors that can be generalised to an algorithm with exponential complexity in the decoding radius. They may be viewed as a generalisation of the well-known Delsarte-Gabidulin-Roth maximum rank distance codes. We study a generalised class of these codes. We investigate their properties and outline decoding techniques for different metrics that leverage their tensor structure. We first consider a fibre-wise decoding approach, as each fibre of a codeword corresponds to a Gabidulin codeword. We then give a generalisation of Loidreau-Overbeck's decoding method that corrects errors with properties constrained by the dimensions of the slice spaces and fibre spaces. The metrics we consider are bounded from above by the tensor-rank metric, and therefore these algorithms also decode tensor-rank weight errors.

ai.tldr: 本文研究张量码的解码算法，提出基于纤维结构和切片/纤维空间维度的两种解码方法，适用于张量秩度量及相关的度量。
ai.motivation: 张量码是矩阵码的推广，Roth引入了一类张量码并给出了指数复杂度的解码算法。本文旨在研究更广泛的张量码类，探索利用其张量结构的解码技术。
ai.method: 首先采用纤维级解码方法，利用每个纤维对应Gabidulin码的特点；其次推广Loidreau-Overbeck解码方法，校正受切片空间和纤维空间维度约束的误差。
ai.result: 提出的算法能够解码张量秩权重误差，所考虑的度量由张量秩度量上界约束，因此算法也适用于张量秩度量下的解码。
ai.help_to_user: 为张量码的解码提供了两种结构化方法，扩展了现有解码技术的适用范围。

idea_spark.idea: 将高维张量码分解为低维分量（纤维）进行逐部分解码，再整合全局结构约束。
idea_spark.risk: 算法复杂度可能随张量阶数增长，实际应用需考虑计算可行性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将经典矩阵码解码技术（如Gabidulin码、Loidreau-Overbeck方法）推广至高维张量场景。

zh.tldr: 本文研究张量码的解码算法，提出基于纤维结构和切片/纤维空间维度的两种解码方法，适用于张量秩度量及相关的度量。
zh.motivation: 张量码是矩阵码的推广，Roth引入了一类张量码并给出了指数复杂度的解码算法。本文旨在研究更广泛的张量码类，探索利用其张量结构的解码技术。

idea_spark.risk: 算法复杂度可能随张量阶数增长，实际应用需考虑计算可行性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将经典矩阵码解码技术（如Gabidulin码、Loidreau-Overbeck方法）推广至高维张量场景。

en.tldr: This paper studies decoding algorithms for tensor codes, proposing two methods based on fibre structure and slice/fibre space dimensions, applicable to the tensor-rank metric and related metrics.
en.motivation: Tensor codes generalize matrix codes. Roth introduced a class of such codes with an exponential-complexity decoding algorithm. This work aims to study a broader class of tensor codes and explore decoding techniques leveraging their tensor structure.
en.method: First, a fibre-wise decoding approach is used, exploiting that each fibre corresponds to a Gabidulin codeword. Second, a generalization of the Loidreau-Overbeck decoding method corrects errors constrained by slice space and fibre space dimensions.
en.result: The proposed algorithms decode tensor-rank weight errors. The considered metrics are bounded above by the tensor-rank metric, so the algorithms also decode under the tensor-rank metric.
en.help_to_user: Provides two structured decoding approaches for tensor codes, extending the applicability of existing decoding techniques.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose high-dimensional tensor codes into lower-dimensional components (fibres) for partial decoding, then integrate global structural constraints.
idea_spark.risk: Algorithm complexity may grow with tensor order; computational feasibility must be considered for practical applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires generalization of classical matrix code decoding techniques (e.g., Gabidulin codes, Loidreau-Overbeck method) to higher-dimensional tensor settings.

papers.title: Accessible Quantum Correlations Under Complexity Constraints
papers.summary: Quantum systems may contain underlying correlations which are inaccessible to computationally bounded observers. We capture this distinction through a framework that analyses bipartite states only using efficiently implementable quantum channels. This leads to a complexity-constrained max-divergence and a corresponding computational min-entropy. The latter quantity recovers the standard operational meaning of the conditional min-entropy: in the fully quantum case, it quantifies the largest overlap with a maximally entangled state attainable via efficient operations on the conditional subsystem. For classical-quantum states, it further reduces to the optimal guessing probability of a computationally bounded observer with access to side information. Lastly, in the absence of side information, the computational min-entropy simplifies to a computational notion of the operator norm. We then establish strong separations between the information-theoretic and complexity-constrained notions of min-entropy. For pure states, there exist highly entangled families of states with extremal min-entropy whose efficiently accessible entanglement in terms of computational min-entropy is exponentially suppressed. For mixed states, the separation is even sharper: the information-theoretic conditional min-entropy can be highly negative while the complexity-constrained quantity remains nearly maximal. Overall, our results demonstrate that computational constraints can fundamentally limit the quantum correlations that are observable in practice.

ai.tldr: 本文提出一个框架，分析在计算能力受限下可访问的量子关联，定义了计算最小熵，并证明计算约束能从根本上限制实践中可观测的量子关联。
ai.motivation: 量子系统可能包含底层关联，但这些关联对于计算能力有限的观察者是不可访问的。需要一种框架来区分信息论意义上的关联与计算约束下实际可访问的关联。
ai.method: 建立一个仅使用高效可实现的量子信道来分析二分态的理论框架，由此导出复杂度约束的最大散度和相应的计算最小熵。
ai.result: 计算最小熵恢复了条件最小熵的标准操作意义：在纯量子情况下，它量化了通过对条件子系统进行高效操作所能达到的与最大纠缠态的最大重叠；对于经典-量子态，它简化为计算受限观察者在有侧信息时的最优猜测概率。研究证明了信息论最小熵与复杂度约束最小熵之间存在强分离。
ai.help_to_user: 该工作为理解计算限制如何影响量子信息处理提供了理论基础，特别是在量子密码学和资源理论中，区分理论上存在与实际可提取的关联至关重要。

idea_spark.idea: 在量子资源理论或密码学协议的安全分析中，引入‘计算可访问性’作为关键指标，而不仅仅是信息论存在性。
idea_spark.risk: 框架依赖于计算复杂性假设（如P≠NP或量子复杂性假设），实际应用需考虑具体困难问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在量子机器学习或噪声中等尺度量子（NISQ）设备中，评估哪些量子优势在计算约束下仍然实际可用。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a framework for analyzing accessible quantum correlations under computational constraints, defines a computational min-entropy, and demonstrates that computational constraints can fundamentally limit observable quantum correlations in practice.
en.motivation: Quantum systems may contain underlying correlations that are inaccessible to computationally bounded observers. A framework is needed to distinguish between information-theoretic correlations and those practically accessible under computational constraints.
en.method: A framework is established that analyzes bipartite states using only efficiently implementable quantum channels, leading to a complexity-constrained max-divergence and a corresponding computational min-entropy.
en.result: The computational min-entropy recovers the standard operational meaning of the conditional min-entropy. Strong separations are established between information-theoretic and complexity-constrained notions of min-entropy for both pure and mixed states.
en.help_to_user: This work provides a theoretical foundation for understanding how computational limits impact quantum information processing, particularly in quantum cryptography and resource theories, where distinguishing between theoretically existing and practically extractable correlations is crucial.

idea_spark.idea: In quantum resource theories or security analyses of cryptographic protocols, introduce 'computational accessibility' as a key metric, beyond mere information-theoretic existence.
idea_spark.risk: The framework relies on computational complexity assumptions (e.g., P≠NP or quantum complexity assumptions); practical applications must consider specific hard problems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires evaluation of which quantum advantages remain practically usable under computational constraints in areas like quantum machine learning or noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices.

papers.title: Hierarchical Codec Diffusion for Video-to-Speech Generation
papers.summary: Video-to-Speech (VTS) generation aims to synthesize speech from a silent video without auditory signals. However, existing VTS methods disregard the hierarchical nature of speech, which spans coarse speaker-aware semantics to fine-grained prosodic details. This oversight hinders direct alignment between visual and speech features at specific hierarchical levels during property matching. In this paper, leveraging the hierarchical structure of Residual Vector Quantization (RVQ)-based codec, we propose HiCoDiT, a novel Hierarchical Codec Diffusion Transformer that exploits the inherent hierarchy of discrete speech tokens to achieve strong audio-visual alignment. Specifically, since lower-level tokens encode coarse speaker-aware semantics and higher-level tokens capture fine-grained prosody, HiCoDiT employs low-level and high-level blocks to generate tokens at different levels. The low-level blocks condition on lip-synchronized motion and facial identity to capture speaker-aware content, while the high-level blocks use facial expression to modulate prosodic dynamics. Finally, to enable more effective coarse-to-fine conditioning, we propose a dual-scale adaptive instance layer normalization that jointly captures global vocal style through channel-wise normalization and local prosody dynamics through temporal-wise normalization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that HiCoDiT outperforms baselines in fidelity and expressiveness, highlighting the potential of discrete modelling for VTS. The code and speech demo are both available at https://github.com/Jiaxin-Ye/HiCoDiT.

ai.tldr: HiCoDiT是一种利用离散语音令牌层次结构的分层编解码扩散Transformer，通过低层块捕捉说话人语义、高层块调制韵律，实现视频到语音的高质量生成。
ai.motivation: 现有视频到语音生成方法忽视了语音的层次性（从粗粒度说话人语义到细粒度韵律细节），导致视觉与语音特征在特定层次上难以直接对齐。
ai.method: 基于残差向量量化的层次结构，设计分层编解码扩散Transformer：低层块以唇部同步运动和面部身份为条件生成说话人感知内容；高层块利用面部表情调制韵律动态；提出双尺度自适应实例层归一化，联合捕获全局发声风格和局部韵律动态。
ai.result: 实验表明HiCoDiT在保真度和表现力上优于基线，证明了离散建模在视频到语音生成中的潜力。
ai.help_to_user: 为从无声视频生成高质量、与视觉内容对齐的语音提供了层次化建模框架，可应用于配音、辅助通信等领域。

idea_spark.idea: 将语音的层次属性（语义/韵律）映射到离散令牌的不同层级，并分别用对应的视觉特征进行条件生成。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖准确的视觉特征提取（如唇部、表情），在低质量或遮挡视频中性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可将层次化条件生成思想扩展到其他跨模态任务（如文本到语音、动作到声音），或探索更细粒度的视觉-语音对应关系。

en.tldr: HiCoDiT is a hierarchical codec diffusion transformer that exploits the inherent hierarchy of discrete speech tokens, using low-level blocks for speaker-aware semantics and high-level blocks for prosodic modulation to achieve high-quality video-to-speech generation.
en.motivation: Existing video-to-speech methods disregard the hierarchical nature of speech (from coarse speaker-aware semantics to fine-grained prosodic details), hindering direct alignment between visual and speech features at specific hierarchical levels.
en.method: Leveraging the hierarchical structure of Residual Vector Quantization-based codec, the proposed HiCoDiT employs low-level blocks conditioned on lip-synchronized motion and facial identity for speaker-aware content, and high-level blocks using facial expression to modulate prosodic dynamics. A dual-scale adaptive instance layer normalization is introduced to jointly capture global vocal style and local prosody dynamics.
en.result: Extensive experiments demonstrate that HiCoDiT outperforms baselines in fidelity and expressiveness, highlighting the potential of discrete modeling for video-to-speech generation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a hierarchical modeling framework for generating high-quality, visually-aligned speech from silent videos, applicable to dubbing, assistive communication, and related fields.

idea_spark.idea: Map hierarchical speech attributes (semantics/prosody) to different levels of discrete tokens and condition their generation on corresponding visual features.
idea_spark.risk: Performance may degrade with low-quality or occluded videos due to reliance on accurate visual feature extraction (e.g., lips, expressions).
idea_spark.inspiration: The hierarchical conditional generation idea could be extended to other cross-modal tasks (e.g., text-to-speech, motion-to-sound) or finer-grained visual-speech correspondence exploration.

papers.title: LLM Reasoning Is Latent, Not the Chain of Thought
papers.summary: This position paper argues that large language model (LLM) reasoning should be studied as latent-state trajectory formation rather than as faithful surface chain-of-thought (CoT). This matters because claims about faithfulness, interpretability, reasoning benchmarks, and inference-time intervention all depend on what the field takes the primary object of reasoning to be. We ask what that object should be once three often-confounded factors are separated and formalize three competing hypotheses: H1, reasoning is primarily mediated by latent-state trajectories; H2, reasoning is primarily mediated by explicit surface CoT; and H0, most apparent reasoning gains are better explained by generic serial compute than by any privileged representational object. Reorganizing recent empirical, mechanistic, and survey work under this framework, and adding compute-audited worked exemplars that factorize surface traces, latent interventions, and matched budget expansions, we find that current evidence most strongly supports H1 as a default working hypothesis rather than as a task-independent verdict. We therefore make two recommendations: the field should treat latent-state dynamics as the default object of study for LLM reasoning, and it should evaluate reasoning with designs that explicitly disentangle surface traces, latent states, and serial compute.

ai.tldr: 本文主张将大语言模型的推理视为潜在状态轨迹的形成，而非表面思维链的忠实反映，并建议将潜在状态动态作为默认研究对象。
ai.motivation: 当前关于思维链忠实性、可解释性、推理基准和推理时干预的论断，都依赖于对推理本质的理解。需要澄清推理的主要对象是什么。
ai.method: 分离并形式化了三个竞争性假设（H1：潜在状态轨迹主导；H2：表面思维链主导；H0：串行计算主导），通过重组现有实证、机制和综述研究，并添加计算审计的示例进行分析。
ai.result: 现有证据最有力地支持H1（潜在状态轨迹主导）作为默认的工作假设，而非任务无关的最终结论。
ai.help_to_user: 本文提供了一个分析LLM推理本质的清晰框架，帮助你理解思维链与潜在状态的关系，并指导如何设计能区分表面痕迹、潜在状态和串行计算的评估方法。

idea_spark.idea: 开发新的模型评估或干预技术，专门针对潜在状态轨迹而非表面文本来诊断或增强模型的推理能力。
idea_spark.risk: 过度关注潜在状态可能使研究脱离可观察、可验证的表面行为，增加解释的任意性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对“模型真正在计算什么”的深入探究，推动超越文本生成的、更本质的认知建模研究。

en.tldr: This paper argues that LLM reasoning should be studied as latent-state trajectory formation rather than faithful surface chain-of-thought, and recommends treating latent-state dynamics as the default object of study.
en.motivation: Claims about CoT faithfulness, interpretability, reasoning benchmarks, and inference-time intervention all depend on what is taken as the primary object of reasoning. The field needs clarity on this fundamental issue.
en.method: Separates and formalizes three competing hypotheses (H1: latent-state trajectory mediated; H2: surface CoT mediated; H0: generic serial compute). Reorganizes recent empirical, mechanistic, and survey work under this framework, supplemented with compute-audited worked exemplars.
en.result: Current evidence most strongly supports H1 (latent-state trajectory mediation) as a default working hypothesis, rather than as a task-independent verdict.
en.help_to_user: Provides a clear framework for analyzing the nature of LLM reasoning, helping you understand the relationship between CoT and latent states, and guiding the design of evaluations that disentangle surface traces, latent states, and serial compute.

idea_spark.idea: Develop new model evaluation or intervention techniques specifically targeting latent-state trajectories rather than surface text to diagnose or enhance model reasoning.
idea_spark.risk: Over-focusing on latent states may detach research from observable, verifiable surface behavior, increasing interpretive arbitrariness.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires deeper inquiry into 'what the model is actually computing,' pushing research towards more fundamental cognitive modeling beyond text generation.

papers.title: DALM: A Domain-Algebraic Language Model via Three-Phase Structured Generation
papers.summary: Large language models compress heterogeneous knowledge into a single parameter space, allowing facts from different domains to interfere during generation. We propose DALM, a Domain-Algebraic Language Model that replaces unconstrained token generation with structured denoising over a domain lattice. DALM follows a three-phase generation path: it first resolves domain uncertainty, then relation uncertainty, and finally concept uncertainty, so each stage operates under explicit algebraic constraints. The framework requires only three ingredients: a lattice of domains with computable meet, join, and implication; a typing function over relations that controls inheritance across domains; and a fiber partition that localizes knowledge to domain-specific subsets. Given these ingredients, DALM yields a three-phase encoder-decoder architecture in which generation is confined to a domain fiber, cross-domain contamination is structurally prevented in closed-vocabulary mode and auditably bounded in open-vocabulary mode, and a single query can produce a domain-indexed multi-perspective answer space. We instantiate the framework with the CDC knowledge representation system and outline training and evaluation on validated domain-annotated crystal libraries. DALM reframes language generation as algebraically constrained structured denoising rather than unconstrained decoding over a flat token space.

ai.tldr: DALM是一种基于领域代数结构的语言模型，通过三阶段结构化生成，将无约束的标记生成替换为在领域格上的结构化去噪，以解决不同领域知识在生成过程中的干扰问题。
ai.motivation: 大型语言模型将异构知识压缩到单一参数空间，导致不同领域的事实信息在生成过程中相互干扰。
ai.method: 提出DALM框架，采用三阶段生成路径：先解决领域不确定性，再解决关系不确定性，最后解决概念不确定性。该框架需要三个组件：具有可计算交、并、蕴含运算的领域格；控制跨领域继承的关系类型函数；将知识局部化到领域特定子集的纤维划分。
ai.result: DALM产生了一个三阶段编码器-解码器架构，生成过程被限制在领域纤维内，在封闭词汇模式下从结构上防止跨领域污染，在开放词汇模式下可审计地限制污染，单个查询可产生领域索引的多视角答案空间。
ai.help_to_user: 该模型通过代数约束的结构化去噪，为需要精确领域控制、防止知识污染和多视角答案生成的语言生成任务提供了新框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将语言生成重新定义为在代数约束下的结构化去噪过程，而非在平坦标记空间上的无约束解码。
idea_spark.risk: 框架的实用性高度依赖于领域格的构建质量、类型函数的定义以及纤维划分的合理性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为其他需要结构化控制、领域隔离或可解释性生成的AI任务（如代码生成、多模态推理）提供了模块化约束的设计思路。
