zh.help_to_user: 为需要处理多候选响应偏好数据的研究者提供高效训练方案，降低内存需求，提升模型对齐效果。

idea_spark.idea: 将组级对比损失与NLL损失结合，平衡排序目标与生成质量。
idea_spark.risk: 组规模过大可能导致对比信号稀释，需平衡计算效率与监督信息密度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发设计分层或动态组构建策略，根据响应质量差异自适应调整对比强度。

en.tldr: Proposes a memory-efficient group-wise direct preference optimization algorithm that reduces memory overhead by decoupling sample backpropagation, leveraging multiple candidate responses to improve alignment performance.
en.motivation: Existing preference optimization methods typically use only one positive-negative pair per prompt, discarding additional supervision from multiple candidate responses available in preference datasets.
en.method: Designs a memory-efficient group-wise preference optimization algorithm that preserves gradients while decoupling samples during backpropagation, substantially reducing peak memory usage and enabling training with larger group sizes.
en.result: Across both offline and online alignment settings, leveraging multiple responses consistently outperforms single-pair training; incorporating a negative log-likelihood term on positive responses is critical for performance gains and training stability.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient training solution for researchers handling preference data with multiple candidate responses, reducing memory requirements and improving model alignment effectiveness.

idea_spark.idea: Combine group-wise contrastive loss with NLL loss to balance ranking objectives with generation quality.
idea_spark.risk: Excessively large group sizes may dilute contrastive signals, requiring a balance between computational efficiency and supervision information density.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the design of hierarchical or dynamic group construction strategies that adaptively adjust contrastive intensity based on response quality differences.

papers.title: Modeling of ASD/TD Children's Behaviors in Interaction with a Virtual Social Robot During a Music Education Program Using Deep Neural Networks
papers.summary: This research aimed to develop an intelligent system to evaluate performance and extract behavioral models for children with ASD and neurotypical (TD) children by interacting with a virtual social robot in a music education program using deep neural networks. The system has two main features: 1) it distinguishes between neurotypical children and those with ASD based on their behavior, and 2) generates behaviors resembling those of neurotypical or ASD children in similar situations using deep learning. Intelligent systems that identify complex patterns and simulate behavior can aid in diagnosis, therapist training, and understanding the disorder. Using data from a previous study at the Social and Cognitive Robotics Laboratory of Sharif University of Technology (including the usable data of 9 ASD and 21 TD participants), the system achieved an accuracy of 81% and sensitivity of 96% in distinguishing neurotypical children from those with ASD using both impact data and motion signals. A transformer-based network was designed to reproduce children's behaviors. Experts in the field struggled to differentiate real behaviors from reproduced ones, with an accuracy of 53.5% and agreement of 68%, indicating the model's success in simulating realistic behaviors.

ai.tldr: 本研究开发了一个基于深度神经网络的智能系统，通过虚拟社交机器人在音乐教育程序中的交互，对自闭症谱系障碍（ASD）儿童和神经典型（TD）儿童的行为进行建模与区分。
ai.motivation: 开发能够识别复杂行为模式并模拟行为的智能系统，有助于自闭症的诊断、治疗师培训及对该障碍的理解。
ai.method: 利用先前研究的数据（9名ASD和21名TD参与者），结合冲击数据和运动信号，使用基于Transformer的网络来区分和再现儿童行为。
ai.result: 系统区分ASD与TD儿童的准确率达81%，灵敏度96%；生成的模拟行为使领域专家难以区分（准确率53.5%，一致性68%）。
ai.help_to_user: 该系统可作为辅助诊断工具和行为模拟平台，为研究人员和治疗师提供量化分析手段。

idea_spark.risk: 模型性能受限于小样本数据；模拟行为的伦理影响需谨慎评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发利用虚拟智能体在可控环境中进行行为分析与干预的新范式。

en.tldr: This study developed an intelligent system using deep neural networks to model and distinguish behaviors of children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and typically developing (TD) children through interaction with a virtual social robot in a music education program.
en.motivation: Intelligent systems that identify complex behavioral patterns and simulate behavior can aid in diagnosis, therapist training, and understanding of the disorder.
en.method: Using data from a previous study (9 ASD and 21 TD participants), the system employs impact data and motion signals with a transformer-based network to distinguish and reproduce children's behaviors.
en.result: The system achieved 81% accuracy and 96% sensitivity in distinguishing ASD from TD children; generated behaviors were difficult for experts to differentiate from real ones (53.5% accuracy, 68% agreement).
en.help_to_user: The system serves as an auxiliary diagnostic tool and behavior simulation platform, providing quantitative analysis means for researchers and therapists.

idea_spark.idea: Combine multimodal behavioral data (motion, impact) with deep generative models to create high-fidelity behavior simulators.
idea_spark.risk: Model performance is limited by small sample size; ethical implications of simulated behavior require careful assessment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires new paradigms for using virtual agents in controlled environments for behavioral analysis and intervention.

papers.title: Enhancing Hazy Wildlife Imagery: AnimalHaze3k and IncepDehazeGan
papers.summary: Atmospheric haze significantly degrades wildlife imagery, impeding computer vision applications critical for conservation, such as animal detection, tracking, and behavior analysis. To address this challenge, we introduce AnimalHaze3k a synthetic dataset comprising of 3,477 hazy images generated from 1,159 clear wildlife photographs through a physics-based pipeline. Our novel IncepDehazeGan architecture combines inception blocks with residual skip connections in a GAN framework, achieving state-of-the-art performance (SSIM: 0.8914, PSNR: 20.54, and LPIPS: 0.1104), delivering 6.27% higher SSIM and 10.2% better PSNR than competing approaches. When applied to downstream detection tasks, dehazed images improved YOLOv11 detection mAP by 112% and IoU by 67%. These advances can provide ecologists with reliable tools for population monitoring and surveillance in challenging environmental conditions, demonstrating significant potential for enhancing wildlife conservation efforts through robust visual analytics.

ai.tldr: 本文提出AnimalHaze3k合成雾霾野生动物数据集和IncepDehazeGan去雾架构，显著提升雾霾图像质量及下游检测性能。
ai.motivation: 大气雾霾严重降低野生动物图像质量，阻碍用于动物检测、追踪和行为分析的计算机视觉应用，影响保护工作。
ai.method: 通过物理模拟流程从1159张清晰野生动物照片生成3477张雾霾图像，构建AnimalHaze3k数据集；提出结合Inception模块和残差跳跃连接的IncepDehazeGan生成对抗网络架构。
ai.result: IncepDehazeGan在SSIM(0.8914)、PSNR(20.54)和LPIPS(0.1104)指标上达到最优，SSIM和PSNR分别比现有方法高6.27%和10.2%；去雾图像使YOLOv11检测mAP提升112%，IoU提升67%。
ai.help_to_user: 为生态学家提供在恶劣环境条件下进行种群监测的可靠工具，通过鲁棒的视觉分析增强野生动物保护工作。

idea_spark.idea: 将合成雾霾数据集与专用去雾网络结合，形成端到端的野生动物图像增强解决方案。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据与真实雾霾条件可能存在域差距；模型在极端雾霾浓度下性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将图像去雾作为预处理步骤，系统性提升野外监控计算机视觉任务的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 将合成雾霾数据集与专用去雾网络结合，形成端到端的野生动物图像增强解决方案。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据与真实雾霾条件可能存在域差距；模型在极端雾霾浓度下性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将图像去雾作为预处理步骤，系统性提升野外监控计算机视觉任务的鲁棒性。

en.tldr: This paper introduces the AnimalHaze3k synthetic hazy wildlife dataset and the IncepDehazeGan dehazing architecture, significantly improving hazy image quality and downstream detection performance.
en.motivation: Atmospheric haze severely degrades wildlife imagery, impeding computer vision applications for animal detection, tracking, and behavior analysis, hindering conservation efforts.
en.method: Constructed the AnimalHaze3k dataset by generating 3,477 hazy images from 1,159 clear wildlife photographs through a physics-based pipeline; proposed the IncepDehazeGan GAN architecture combining inception blocks with residual skip connections.
en.result: IncepDehazeGan achieved state-of-the-art performance with SSIM: 0.8914, PSNR: 20.54, and LPIPS: 0.1104, delivering 6.27% higher SSIM and 10.2% better PSNR than competing approaches; dehazed images improved YOLOv11 detection mAP by 112% and IoU by 67%.
en.help_to_user: Provides ecologists with reliable tools for population monitoring in challenging environmental conditions, demonstrating significant potential for enhancing wildlife conservation through robust visual analytics.

idea_spark.idea: Combining synthetic haze datasets with specialized dehazing networks to form an end-to-end wildlife image enhancement solution.
idea_spark.risk: Domain gap may exist between synthetic data and real haze conditions; model performance may degrade under extreme haze concentrations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires treating image dehazing as a preprocessing step to systematically improve the robustness of computer vision tasks in wild monitoring.

papers.title: SENSE: Stereo OpEN Vocabulary SEmantic Segmentation
papers.summary: Open-vocabulary semantic segmentation enables models to segment objects or image regions beyond fixed class sets, offering flexibility in dynamic environments. However, existing methods often rely on single-view images and struggle with spatial precision, especially under occlusions and near object boundaries. We propose SENSE, the first work on Stereo OpEN Vocabulary SEmantic Segmentation, which leverages stereo vision and vision-language models to enhance open-vocabulary semantic segmentation. By incorporating stereo image pairs, we introduce geometric cues that improve spatial reasoning and segmentation accuracy. Trained on the PhraseStereo dataset, our approach achieves strong performance in phrase-grounded tasks and demonstrates generalization in zero-shot settings. On PhraseStereo, we show a +2.9% improvement in Average Precision over the baseline method and +0.76% over the best competing method. SENSE also provides a relative improvement of +3.5% mIoU on Cityscapes and +18% on KITTI compared to the baseline work. By jointly reasoning over semantics and geometry, SENSE supports accurate scene understanding from natural language, essential for autonomous robots and Intelligent Transportation Systems.

ai.tldr: SENSE是首个利用立体视觉和视觉语言模型进行开放词汇语义分割的方法，通过几何线索提升空间推理和分割精度。
ai.motivation: 现有开放词汇语义分割方法通常依赖单视图图像，在遮挡和物体边界附近的空间精度不足。
ai.method: 引入立体图像对提供几何线索，结合视觉语言模型，在PhraseStereo数据集上训练，实现语义与几何的联合推理。
ai.result: 在PhraseStereo上平均精度比基线提升2.9%；在Cityscapes和KITTI上mIoU分别相对提升3.5%和18%。
ai.help_to_user: 为动态环境中的精确场景理解提供新方案，适用于自动驾驶和智能交通系统。

idea_spark.idea: 利用立体视觉的深度信息约束语言驱动的分割，减少歧义并提升边界精度。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖立体图像对数据，在单目或弱纹理场景中可能失效；计算开销可能增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发多模态融合研究：如何将几何、外观与语义更高效地结合，以支持更细粒度的开放世界理解。

zh.motivation: 现有开放词汇语义分割方法通常依赖单视图图像，在遮挡和物体边界附近的空间精度不足。
zh.method: 引入立体图像对提供几何线索，结合视觉语言模型，在PhraseStereo数据集上训练，实现语义与几何的联合推理。
zh.result: 在PhraseStereo上平均精度比基线提升2.9%；在Cityscapes和KITTI上mIoU分别相对提升3.5%和18%。
zh.help_to_user: 为动态环境中的精确场景理解提供新方案，适用于自动驾驶和智能交通系统。

idea_spark.idea: 利用立体视觉的深度信息约束语言驱动的分割，减少歧义并提升边界精度。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖立体图像对数据，在单目或弱纹理场景中可能失效；计算开销可能增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发多模态融合研究：如何将几何、外观与语义更高效地结合，以支持更细粒度的开放世界理解。

en.tldr: SENSE is the first stereo open-vocabulary semantic segmentation method that leverages stereo vision and vision-language models to improve spatial reasoning and segmentation accuracy via geometric cues.
en.motivation: Existing open-vocabulary semantic segmentation methods often rely on single-view images and struggle with spatial precision, especially under occlusions and near object boundaries.
en.method: Incorporates stereo image pairs to provide geometric cues, combines with vision-language models, and is trained on the PhraseStereo dataset for joint semantic-geometric reasoning.
en.result: Achieves +2.9% Average Precision improvement over baseline on PhraseStereo; +3.5% mIoU on Cityscapes and +18% on KITTI relative to baseline.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new solution for accurate scene understanding in dynamic environments, applicable to autonomous robots and Intelligent Transportation Systems.

idea_spark.idea: Use depth information from stereo vision to constrain language-driven segmentation, reducing ambiguity and improving boundary accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on stereo image pairs; may fail in monocular or low-texture scenarios; computational cost may increase.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires multimodal fusion research: how to more efficiently integrate geometry, appearance, and semantics for finer-grained open-world understanding.

papers.title: MambaBack: Bridging Local Features and Global Contexts in Whole Slide Image Analysis
papers.summary: Whole Slide Image (WSI) analysis is pivotal in computational pathology, enabling cancer diagnosis by integrating morphological and architectural cues across magnifications. Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) serves as the standard framework for WSI analysis. Recently, Mamba has become a promising backbone for MIL, overtaking Transformers due to its efficiency and global context modeling capabilities originating from Natural Language Processing (NLP). However, existing Mamba-based MIL approaches face three critical challenges: (1) disruption of 2D spatial locality during 1D sequence flattening; (2) sub-optimal modeling of fine-grained local cellular structures; and (3) high memory peaks during inference on resource-constrained edge devices. Studies like MambaOut reveal that Mamba's SSM component is redundant for local feature extraction, where Gated CNNs suffice. Recognizing that WSI analysis demands both fine-grained local feature extraction akin to natural images, and global context modeling akin to NLP, we propose MambaBack, a novel hybrid architecture that harmonizes the strengths of Mamba and MambaOut. First, we propose the Hilbert sampling strategy to preserve the 2D spatial locality of tiles within 1D sequences, enhancing the model's spatial perception. Second, we design a hierarchical structure comprising a 1D Gated CNN block based on MambaOut to capture local cellular features, and a BiMamba2 block to aggregate global context, jointly enhancing multi-scale representation. Finally, we implement an asymmetric chunking design, allowing parallel processing during training and chunking-streaming accumulation during inference, minimizing peak memory usage for deployment. Experimental results on five datasets demonstrate that MambaBack outperforms seven state-of-the-art methods. Source code and datasets are publicly available.

ai.tldr: MambaBack是一种用于全切片图像分析的混合架构，结合了门控CNN的局部特征提取能力和Mamba的全局上下文建模能力，通过Hilbert采样保持空间局部性，并采用非对称分块设计降低内存峰值。
ai.motivation: 现有基于Mamba的多示例学习方法在WSI分析中存在三个关键挑战：1D序列展平破坏2D空间局部性；对细粒度局部细胞结构建模不足；在资源受限的边缘设备上推理时内存峰值过高。
ai.method: 提出Hilbert采样策略保持瓦片在1D序列中的2D空间局部性；设计分层结构，包含基于MambaOut的1D门控CNN块提取局部细胞特征，以及BiMamba2块聚合全局上下文；实现非对称分块设计，训练时并行处理，推理时分块流式累积以最小化内存峰值。
ai.result: 在五个数据集上的实验结果表明，MambaBack优于七种最先进的方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为计算病理学中的全切片图像分析提供了一种高效、内存友好的混合架构，平衡了局部特征提取和全局上下文建模的需求。

idea_spark.idea: 将擅长局部特征提取的CNN与擅长长序列建模的Mamba相结合，构建互补的混合架构。
idea_spark.risk: 混合架构可能增加模型复杂性和调参难度；Hilbert采样对特定空间模式的适用性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发于MambaOut的发现——Mamba的SSM组件对局部特征提取冗余，而门控CNN已足够。这促使将两者优势分离并组合。

zh.motivation: 现有基于Mamba的多示例学习方法在WSI分析中存在三个关键挑战：1D序列展平破坏2D空间局部性；对细粒度局部细胞结构建模不足；在资源受限的边缘设备上推理时内存峰值过高。
zh.method: 提出Hilbert采样策略保持瓦片在1D序列中的2D空间局部性；设计分层结构，包含基于MambaOut的1D门控CNN块提取局部细胞特征，以及BiMamba2块聚合全局上下文；实现非对称分块设计，训练时并行处理，推理时分块流式累积以最小化内存峰值。
zh.result: 在五个数据集上的实验结果表明，MambaBack优于七种最先进的方法。
zh.help_to_user: 为计算病理学中的全切片图像分析提供了一种高效、内存友好的混合架构，平衡了局部特征提取和全局上下文建模的需求。

idea_spark.idea: 将擅长局部特征提取的CNN与擅长长序列建模的Mamba相结合，构建互补的混合架构。
idea_spark.risk: 混合架构可能增加模型复杂性和调参难度；Hilbert采样对特定空间模式的适用性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发于MambaOut的发现——Mamba的SSM组件对局部特征提取冗余，而门控CNN已足够。这促使将两者优势分离并组合。

en.tldr: MambaBack is a hybrid architecture for Whole Slide Image analysis that combines the local feature extraction capability of Gated CNNs with the global context modeling of Mamba. It preserves spatial locality via Hilbert sampling and reduces memory peaks with an asymmetric chunking design.
en.motivation: Existing Mamba-based MIL approaches for WSI analysis face three critical challenges: disruption of 2D spatial locality during 1D sequence flattening; sub-optimal modeling of fine-grained local cellular structures; and high memory peaks during inference on resource-constrained edge devices.
en.method: Propose Hilbert sampling to preserve 2D spatial locality of tiles within 1D sequences; design a hierarchical structure with a 1D Gated CNN block (based on MambaOut) for local cellular features and a BiMamba2 block for global context aggregation; implement asymmetric chunking for parallel training and chunking-streaming accumulation during inference to minimize peak memory.
en.result: Experimental results on five datasets demonstrate that MambaBack outperforms seven state-of-the-art methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient, memory-friendly hybrid architecture for Whole Slide Image analysis in computational pathology, balancing the need for local feature extraction and global context modeling.

idea_spark.idea: Combine CNNs (excelling at local feature extraction) with Mamba (excelling at long-sequence modeling) into a complementary hybrid architecture.
idea_spark.risk: Hybrid architecture may increase model complexity and tuning difficulty; applicability of Hilbert sampling to specific spatial patterns needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the finding from MambaOut that Mamba's SSM component is redundant for local feature extraction, where Gated CNNs suffice. This motivates separating and combining their strengths.

papers.title: Polarization by Default: Auditing Recommendation Bias in LLM-Based Content Curation
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed to curate and rank human-created content, yet the nature and structure of their biases in these tasks remains poorly understood: which biases are robust across providers and platforms, and which can be mitigated through prompt design. We present a controlled simulation study mapping content selection biases across three major LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) on real social media datasets from Twitter/X, Bluesky, and Reddit, using six prompting strategies (\textit{general}, \textit{popular}, \textit{engaging}, \textit{informative}, \textit{controversial}, \textit{neutral}). Through 540,000 simulated top-10 selections from pools of 100 posts across 54 experimental conditions, we find that biases differ substantially in how structural and how prompt-sensitive they are. Polarization is amplified across all configurations, toxicity handling shows a strong inversion between engagement- and information-focused prompts, and sentiment biases are predominantly negative. Provider comparisons reveal distinct trade-offs: GPT-4o Mini shows the most consistent behavior across prompts; Claude and Gemini exhibit high adaptivity in toxicity handling; Gemini shows the strongest negative sentiment preference. On Twitter/X, where author demographics can be inferred from profile bios, political leaning bias is the clearest demographic signal: left-leaning authors are systematically over-represented despite right-leaning authors forming the pool plurality in the dataset, and this pattern largely persists across prompts.

ai.tldr: 本研究通过模拟实验，审计了三大LLM提供商在社交媒体内容推荐中的偏见，发现极化被普遍放大，毒性处理因提示词而异，且存在明显的负面情感和政治倾向偏见。
ai.motivation: LLM越来越多地被用于策划和排序人类创作的内容，但其在这些任务中的偏见性质和结构仍不清楚，尤其是哪些偏见在不同提供商和平台间具有鲁棒性，以及哪些可以通过提示设计来缓解。
ai.method: 对三大LLM提供商（OpenAI、Anthropic、Google）在来自Twitter/X、Bluesky和Reddit的真实社交媒体数据集上进行受控模拟研究，使用六种提示策略（通用、流行、吸引人、信息性、争议性、中立），共进行了540,000次模拟的Top-10选择。
ai.result: 偏见在结构性和对提示的敏感性上差异很大。极化在所有配置中被放大；毒性处理在关注参与度和关注信息的提示间表现出强烈的反转；情感偏见主要为负面。提供商比较揭示了不同的权衡：GPT-4o Mini在提示间行为最一致；Claude和Gemini在毒性处理上表现出高适应性；Gemini显示出最强的负面情感偏好。在Twitter/X上，政治倾向偏见是最清晰的人口统计信号：尽管数据集中右倾作者占多数，但左倾作者被系统性过度代表，且该模式在不同提示下基本持续。
ai.help_to_user: 本研究揭示了LLM内容策划中存在的系统性偏见，特别是极化放大和政治倾向偏见，为开发者和政策制定者设计更公平的推荐系统提供了实证依据。

idea_spark.idea: 将偏见审计框架扩展到更多内容类型（如新闻、视频）和更细粒度的人口统计推断；研究偏见对长期用户信念形成的影响；探索结合人类反馈的偏见缓解策略。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟环境可能与真实部署场景存在差异；人口统计推断（如政治倾向）可能存在误差；研究结果可能随时间因模型更新而改变。
idea_spark.inspiration: 偏见并非单一或固定，而是具有结构性和提示敏感性，这为通过工程和设计进行干预提供了可能性。不同提供商在偏见权衡上表现出不同‘个性’，这暗示了市场多样性的价值。

zh.tldr: 本研究通过模拟实验，审计了三大LLM提供商在社交媒体内容推荐中的偏见，发现极化被普遍放大，毒性处理因提示词而异，且存在明显的负面情感和政治倾向偏见。
zh.motivation: LLM越来越多地被用于策划和排序人类创作的内容，但其在这些任务中的偏见性质和结构仍不清楚，尤其是哪些偏见在不同提供商和平台间具有鲁棒性，以及哪些可以通过提示设计来缓解。
zh.method: 对三大LLM提供商（OpenAI、Anthropic、Google）在来自Twitter/X、Bluesky和Reddit的真实社交媒体数据集上进行受控模拟研究，使用六种提示策略（通用、流行、吸引人、信息性、争议性、中立），共进行了540,000次模拟的Top-10选择。
zh.result: 偏见在结构性和对提示的敏感性上差异很大。极化在所有配置中被放大；毒性处理在关注参与度和关注信息的提示间表现出强烈的反转；情感偏见主要为负面。提供商比较揭示了不同的权衡：GPT-4o Mini在提示间行为最一致；Claude和Gemini在毒性处理上表现出高适应性；Gemini显示出最强的负面情感偏好。在Twitter/X上，政治倾向偏见是最清晰的人口统计信号：尽管数据集中右倾作者占多数，但左倾作者被系统性过度代表，且该模式在不同提示下基本持续。
zh.help_to_user: 本研究揭示了LLM内容策划中存在的系统性偏见，特别是极化放大和政治倾向偏见，为开发者和政策制定者设计更公平的推荐系统提供了实证依据。

idea_spark.idea: 将偏见审计框架扩展到更多内容类型（如新闻、视频）和更细粒度的人口统计推断；研究偏见对长期用户信念形成的影响；探索结合人类反馈的偏见缓解策略。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟环境可能与真实部署场景存在差异；人口统计推断（如政治倾向）可能存在误差；研究结果可能随时间因模型更新而改变。
idea_spark.inspiration: 偏见并非单一或固定，而是具有结构性和提示敏感性，这为通过工程和设计进行干预提供了可能性。不同提供商在偏见权衡上表现出不同‘个性’，这暗示了市场多样性的价值。

en.tldr: This study audits recommendation biases of three major LLM providers in social media content curation via simulation, finding polarization is universally amplified, toxicity handling varies by prompt, and clear negative sentiment and political leaning biases exist.
en.motivation: LLMs are increasingly deployed to curate and rank human-created content, yet the nature and structure of their biases in these tasks remains poorly understood, particularly which biases are robust across providers and platforms, and which can be mitigated through prompt design.
en.method: A controlled simulation study mapping content selection biases across three major LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) on real social media datasets from Twitter/X, Bluesky, and Reddit, using six prompting strategies (general, popular, engaging, informative, controversial, neutral), involving 540,000 simulated top-10 selections.
en.result: Biases differ substantially in how structural and how prompt-sensitive they are. Polarization is amplified across all configurations; toxicity handling shows a strong inversion between engagement- and information-focused prompts; sentiment biases are predominantly negative. Provider comparisons reveal distinct trade-offs: GPT-4o Mini shows the most consistent behavior across prompts; Claude and Gemini exhibit high adaptivity in toxicity handling; Gemini shows the strongest negative sentiment preference. On Twitter/X, political leaning bias is the clearest demographic signal: left-leaning authors are systematically over-represented despite right-leaning authors forming the pool plurality, and this pattern largely persists across prompts.
en.help_to_user: This study reveals systematic biases in LLM-based content curation, particularly polarization amplification and political leaning bias, providing empirical evidence for developers and policymakers to design fairer recommendation systems.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the bias audit framework to more content types (e.g., news, video) and finer-grained demographic inference; study the impact of biases on long-term user belief formation; explore bias mitigation strategies incorporating human feedback.
idea_spark.risk: Simulation environment may differ from real deployment scenarios; demographic inference (e.g., political leaning) may contain errors; findings may change over time due to model updates.
idea_spark.inspiration: Biases are not monolithic or fixed but are structural and prompt-sensitive, opening possibilities for intervention through engineering and design. Different providers exhibit distinct 'personalities' in bias trade-offs, suggesting the value of market diversity.

papers.title: Efficient Video Diffusion Models: Advancements and Challenges
papers.summary: Video diffusion models have rapidly become the dominant paradigm for high-fidelity generative video synthesis, but their practical deployment remains constrained by severe inference costs. Compared with image generation, video synthesis compounds computation across spatial-temporal token growth and iterative denoising, making attention and memory traffic major bottlenecks in real-world settings. This survey provides a systematic and deployment-oriented review of efficient video diffusion models. We propose a unified categorization that organizes existing methods into four classes of main paradigms, including step distillation, efficient attention, model compression, and cache/trajectory optimization. Building on this categorization, we respectively analyze algorithmic trends of these four paradigms and examine how different design choices target two core objectives: reducing the number of function evaluations and minimizing per-step overhead. Finally, we discuss open challenges and future directions, including quality preservation under composite acceleration, hardware-software co-design, robust real-time long-horizon generation, and open infrastructure for standardized evaluation. To the best of our knowledge, our work is the first comprehensive survey on efficient video diffusion models, offering researchers and engineers a structured overview of the field and its emerging research directions.

ai.tldr: 本文是关于高效视频扩散模型的系统性综述，首次全面梳理了该领域在降低推理成本方面的四大技术范式及其发展趋势。
ai.motivation: 视频扩散模型已成为高保真视频生成的主流方法，但其巨大的推理计算成本严重限制了实际部署，需要系统性地总结现有加速技术。
ai.method: 提出统一分类框架，将现有方法归纳为四类主要范式：步骤蒸馏、高效注意力、模型压缩、缓存/轨迹优化，并分析各类方法如何减少函数评估次数和降低单步开销。
ai.result: 系统梳理了高效视频扩散模型的技术脉络，明确了各范式的算法趋势和设计选择，为研究者和工程师提供了结构化概览。
ai.help_to_user: 为从事视频生成模型效率优化的研究人员提供清晰的领域地图和技术分类，帮助快速定位研究方向。

idea_spark.idea: 探索复合加速策略下的质量保持机制，或设计硬件感知的软硬件协同优化方案。
idea_spark.risk: 过度压缩或加速可能导致视频时序连贯性下降、细节丢失等质量问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可考虑将高效注意力机制与轨迹优化结合，或开发面向长序列生成的增量式缓存策略。

en.tldr: This is the first comprehensive survey on efficient video diffusion models, systematically organizing the field into four main technical paradigms for reducing inference costs.
en.motivation: Video diffusion models dominate high-fidelity generative video synthesis but face severe inference cost constraints, necessitating a systematic review of acceleration techniques.
en.method: Proposes a unified categorization of existing methods into four classes: step distillation, efficient attention, model compression, and cache/trajectory optimization, analyzing how each reduces function evaluations and per-step overhead.
en.result: Provides a structured overview of algorithmic trends and design choices across paradigms, offering researchers and engineers a clear mapping of the field.
en.help_to_user: Offers a technical taxonomy and research roadmap for those working on efficiency optimization for video generation models.

idea_spark.idea: Explore quality-preserving mechanisms under composite acceleration or design hardware-aware co-design solutions.
idea_spark.risk: Over-aggressive compression or acceleration may degrade temporal coherence and visual details.
idea_spark.inspiration: Consider combining efficient attention with trajectory optimization, or developing incremental caching strategies for long-horizon generation.

papers.title: Find, Fix, Reason: Context Repair for Video Reasoning
papers.summary: Reinforcement learning has advanced video reasoning in large multi-modal models, yet dominant pipelines either rely on on-policy self-exploration, which plateaus at the model's knowledge boundary, or hybrid replay that mixes policies and demands careful regularization. Dynamic context methods zoom into focused evidence but often require curated pretraining and two-stage tuning, and their context remains bounded by a small model's capability. In contrast, larger models excel at instruction following and multi-modal understanding, can supply richer context to smaller models, and rapidly zoom in on target regions via simple tools. Building on this capability, we introduce an observation-level intervention: a frozen, tool-integrated teacher identifies the missing spatiotemporal dependency and provides a minimal evidence patch (e.g., timestamps, regions etc.) from the original video while the question remains unchanged. The student answers again with the added context, and training updates with a chosen-rollout scheme integrated into Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). We further propose a Robust Improvement Reward (RIR) that aligns optimization with two goals: outcome validity through correct answers and dependency alignment through rationales that reflect the cited evidence. Advantages are group-normalized across the batch, preserving on-policy exploration while directing it along causally meaningful directions with minimal changes to the training stack. Experiments on various related benchmarks show consistent accuracy gains and strong generalization. Web page and source code will be available at https://github.com/JethroJames/FFR.git.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于冻结教师模型识别缺失时空依赖并提供证据补丁的视频推理上下文修复方法，通过GRPO和RIR奖励实现准确率提升。
ai.motivation: 现有视频推理方法依赖策略自探索或混合回放，受限于模型知识边界或需要精心正则化；动态上下文方法需要定制预训练且能力受限。
ai.method: 使用冻结的工具集成教师识别缺失时空依赖，从原视频提取最小证据补丁；学生基于增强上下文重新回答；采用集成到GRPO的chosen-rollout方案训练，并提出对齐答案正确性和证据依赖的RIR奖励。
ai.result: 在多个相关基准测试中实现一致的准确率提升和强泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种通过外部教师模型修复视频推理上下文的高效方法，减少对模型自身知识边界的依赖。

idea_spark.idea: 利用大模型的指令跟随能力为小模型提供精准上下文补丁。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖教师模型的准确性和工具集成效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过最小干预实现最大上下文修复的思路可用于知识蒸馏或数据增强。

en.tldr: Proposes a context repair method for video reasoning where a frozen teacher identifies missing spatiotemporal dependencies and provides evidence patches, achieving accuracy gains through GRPO and RIR rewards.
en.motivation: Current video reasoning approaches rely on on-policy self-exploration (plateaus at knowledge boundaries) or hybrid replay (requires careful regularization); dynamic context methods need curated pretraining and have limited capacity.
en.method: A frozen tool-integrated teacher identifies missing spatiotemporal dependencies and extracts minimal evidence patches from the original video; the student re-answers with enhanced context; training uses a chosen-rollout scheme integrated into GRPO with a Robust Improvement Reward aligning answer correctness and evidence dependency.
en.result: Consistent accuracy improvements and strong generalization across various related benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient context repair approach for video reasoning that reduces reliance on the model's own knowledge boundaries through external teacher guidance.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage large models' instruction-following capability to provide precise context patches for smaller models.
idea_spark.risk: Depends on teacher model accuracy and tool integration effectiveness.
idea_spark.inspiration: The minimal-intervention-for-maximal-context-repair concept could inspire knowledge distillation or data augmentation approaches.

papers.title: A Systematic Study of Training-Free Methods for Trustworthy Large Language Models
papers.summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) receive increasing attention and are being deployed across various domains, their potential risks, including generating harmful or biased content, producing unsupported claims, and exhibiting vulnerabilities to adversarial attacks, have drawn significant attention. To enable quick and low-cost adaptation, training-free methods have recently emerged as cost-effective alternatives to post-training alignment techniques. Despite their promising results, these methods are evaluated inconsistently across the literature, cover limited dimensions of trustworthiness, and can introduce undesirable side effects, such as utility degradation and increased brittleness. To fully assess the impacts of these training-free methods, we take a step back and systematically re-evaluate the effectiveness of existing training-free methods against various trustworthy settings and their influence on utility, robustness, and computational overhead. We also categorize these methods into three levels (input, internal, and output) based on where they intervene in the model's information flow during inference. Using this taxonomy, we conduct a comprehensive analysis of various representative and effective methods from each level across different LLM families and sizes. Our analysis highlights several trade-offs and unresolved challenges in current approaches. We summarize key findings and limitations in the existing literature, and propose practical recommendations for balancing trustworthiness, utility, and robustness in LLMs without the need for additional training.

ai.tldr: 本文系统评估了无需训练的方法在提升大语言模型可信度方面的有效性、副作用及权衡，提出了基于信息流干预的三级分类法，并给出了实用建议。
ai.motivation: 随着大语言模型广泛应用，其生成有害/偏见内容、虚假声明及对抗攻击脆弱性等风险凸显。无需训练的方法作为低成本适配方案受到关注，但现有评估不一致、可信维度覆盖有限且可能带来效用下降等副作用，需要系统评估。
ai.method: 将现有无需训练方法按推理时信息流干预位置分为输入、内部、输出三级，基于该分类法对不同家族和规模的LLM中各类代表性方法进行全面分析，评估其在多种可信设置下的有效性以及对效用、鲁棒性和计算开销的影响。
ai.result: 分析揭示了当前方法在可信度、效用和鲁棒性之间的多重权衡及未解挑战，总结了现有文献的关键发现与局限，并提出了在不额外训练前提下平衡这些属性的实用建议。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究人员和实践者提供了无需训练方法在提升LLM可信度方面的系统评估框架、分类视角及权衡洞察，指导方法选择与改进。

idea_spark.idea: 开发动态混合干预策略，根据输入语境和模型状态在输入/内部/输出级方法间自适应切换，以优化可信-效用-开销的帕累托前沿。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖无需训练方法可能导致模型在分布外数据或复杂对抗场景下出现不可预测的失效模式；不同干预方法间的相互作用可能产生复合副作用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从控制论角度建模LLM推理信息流，设计轻量级监控与反馈回路；借鉴硬件安全中的分层防护思想，构建多级协同的可信增强架构。

idea_spark.idea: 开发动态混合干预策略，根据输入语境和模型状态在输入/内部/输出级方法间自适应切换，以优化可信-效用-开销的帕累托前沿。

en.tldr: This paper systematically evaluates the effectiveness, side effects, and trade-offs of training-free methods for enhancing trustworthiness in large language models, proposes a three-level taxonomy based on information flow intervention, and provides practical recommendations.
en.motivation: As LLMs are widely deployed, risks like generating harmful/biased content, unsupported claims, and vulnerability to adversarial attacks have gained attention. Training-free methods emerge as low-cost adaptation alternatives, but existing evaluations are inconsistent, cover limited trustworthiness dimensions, and may introduce side effects like utility degradation, necessitating systematic assessment.
en.method: Categorizes existing training-free methods into three levels (input, internal, output) based on where they intervene in the model's information flow during inference. Using this taxonomy, conducts comprehensive analysis of representative methods across different LLM families and sizes, evaluating their effectiveness under various trustworthiness settings and their impact on utility, robustness, and computational overhead.
en.result: The analysis highlights multiple trade-offs and unresolved challenges among trustworthiness, utility, and robustness in current approaches, summarizes key findings and limitations in existing literature, and proposes practical recommendations for balancing these attributes without additional training.
en.help_to_user: Provides researchers and practitioners with a systematic evaluation framework, a categorical perspective, and insights into trade-offs for training-free methods in enhancing LLM trustworthiness, guiding method selection and improvement.

idea_spark.idea: Develop dynamic hybrid intervention strategies that adaptively switch among input/internal/output-level methods based on input context and model state to optimize the Pareto frontier of trustworthiness-utility-overhead.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on training-free methods may lead to unpredictable failure modes under out-of-distribution data or complex adversarial scenarios; interactions between different intervention methods could produce compounded side effects.
idea_spark.inspiration: Model LLM inference information flow from a control theory perspective to design lightweight monitoring and feedback loops; borrow layered defense concepts from hardware security to construct multi-level collaborative trustworthiness enhancement architectures.

papers.title: T-RBFT: A Scalable and Efficient Byzantine Consensus Based on Trusted Execution Environment for Consortium Blockchain
papers.summary: With the continuous expansion of blockchain application scenarios, consortium chains have raised higher performance and security requirements for consensus mechanisms. Unlike public blockchains, consortium chains typically implement an admission mechanism that restricts participation to trusted entities, ensuring that most replicas are honest and the number of faulty nodes remains small under normal circumstances. In such settings, conventional Byzantine Fault Tolerant (BFT) protocols, which are designed for worst-case adversarial scenarios, incur excessive message exchanges and computational overhead, thereby limiting performance and scalability. To address this issue, this paper proposes T-RBFT, a two-layer consensus mechanism inspired by network sharding and enhanced by the trusted execution environment (TEE). In T-RBFT, consensus nodes are first dynamically grouped based on their runtime characteristics. Then, inter-group consensus is achieved through a TEE-assisted BFT protocol, while each group internally reaches agreement using an improved Raft-based mechanism. Experimental evaluation shows that T-RBFT reduces communication overhead and latency, and achieves higher throughput compared to existing two-layer consensus protocols, providing a scalable and communication-efficient consensus protocol for permissioned blockchain networks.

ai.tldr: T-RBFT是一种基于可信执行环境的两层拜占庭共识协议，用于联盟链，通过动态分组和TEE辅助的BFT提升可扩展性和效率。
ai.motivation: 联盟链对共识机制的性能和安全性要求更高，传统BFT协议在正常信任环境下因过度消息交换和计算开销而限制性能。
ai.method: 首先根据运行时特性动态分组共识节点；组间通过TEE辅助的BFT协议达成共识，组内使用改进的Raft机制。
ai.result: 实验评估显示，T-RBFT降低了通信开销和延迟，相比现有两层共识协议实现了更高吞吐量。
ai.help_to_user: 为许可制区块链网络提供了一种可扩展且通信高效的共识协议设计方案。

idea_spark.idea: 在部分可信环境中，用轻量级协议处理内部共识，仅对关键跨组交互使用强安全机制。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖TEE硬件假设，若TEE被攻破则安全模型失效；动态分组可能引入协调开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索将类似分层思想应用于物联网边缘共识，其中设备群组内部使用简单协议，群组间通过安全锚点同步。

en.tldr: T-RBFT is a two-layer Byzantine consensus protocol based on Trusted Execution Environment for consortium blockchain, improving scalability and efficiency through dynamic grouping and TEE-assisted BFT.
en.motivation: Consortium chains demand higher performance and security from consensus mechanisms; conventional BFT protocols incur excessive message exchange and computational overhead in normally trusted settings.
en.method: First, consensus nodes are dynamically grouped based on runtime characteristics; inter-group consensus uses a TEE-assisted BFT protocol, while each group internally employs an improved Raft mechanism.
en.result: Experimental evaluation shows T-RBFT reduces communication overhead and latency, and achieves higher throughput compared to existing two-layer consensus protocols.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable and communication-efficient consensus protocol design for permissioned blockchain networks.

idea_spark.idea: In partially trusted environments, use lightweight protocols for intra-group consensus and reserve strong security mechanisms only for critical cross-group interactions.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on TEE hardware assumptions—security model fails if TEE is compromised; dynamic grouping may introduce coordination overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore applying similar hierarchical ideas to IoT edge consensus, where device clusters use simple protocols internally and synchronize via secure anchors across clusters.

papers.title: Small Yet Configurable: Unveiling Null Variability in Software
papers.summary: Many small-scale software systems, that is, with limited codebase or binary size, are widely used in everyday tasks, yet their configurability remains largely unexplored. At the same time, studies on modern software systems show a trend toward increasing configurability, alongside growing interest in building immutable, specialized, and reproducible software. In this paper, we present the first empirical study on the extent of configurability in small-scale software systems. By analyzing 108 programs from GNU coreutils, we show that even small programs can exhibit significant compile-time and run-time variability, with up to 76 options per program. Then, there is a high correlation (0.78) between run-time variability and codebase size. Furthermore, an analysis of the 20 smallest programs across 85 releases reveals that variability tends to increase over time, primarily due to the added compile-time variability. This suggests that shifting options between run-time and compile-time, removing unnecessary run-time variability, or resolving compile-time variability early, can help reduce codebase complexity and size. We also introduce, for the first time, the concept of null-variable software system, one with no configurability beyond mandatory features. Our findings show that high configurability is not exclusive to large-scale systems and that reducing unnecessary variability can lead to lightweight, smaller, and more maintainable software. We hope this effort contributes to designing new software by understanding how to balance its configurability with codebase size.

ai.tldr: 本文首次对小型软件系统的可配置性进行实证研究，发现即使小型程序也能表现出显著的编译时和运行时变异性，且变异性与代码库大小高度相关。
ai.motivation: 小型软件系统广泛使用，但其可配置性尚未被充分探索。现代软件系统呈现可配置性增加的趋势，同时人们对构建不可变、专用和可复现软件的兴趣也在增长。
ai.method: 通过分析GNU coreutils中的108个程序，测量其编译时和运行时变异性，并分析20个最小程序在85个版本中的变化趋势。
ai.result: 小型程序最多可拥有76个选项；运行时变异性与代码库大小高度相关（0.78）；变异性随时间增加，主要源于编译时变异性增加。
ai.help_to_user: 揭示了高可配置性并非大型系统独有，减少不必要的变异性可帮助设计更轻量、更小、更易维护的软件。

idea_spark.idea: 开发自动化工具，在软件构建过程中识别并消除冗余的编译时或运行时选项。
idea_spark.risk: 过度减少变异性可能降低软件适应不同环境的灵活性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“零变异性软件”概念出发，探索在特定领域（如安全关键系统）中构建最小化可变组件的设计模式。

en.tldr: This paper presents the first empirical study on configurability in small-scale software systems, revealing that even small programs exhibit significant compile-time and run-time variability, with high correlation between variability and codebase size.
en.motivation: Small-scale software systems are widely used, but their configurability remains largely unexplored. Modern software shows a trend toward increasing configurability alongside growing interest in immutable, specialized, and reproducible software.
en.method: Analyzed 108 programs from GNU coreutils to measure compile-time and run-time variability, and examined 20 smallest programs across 85 releases for trends.

idea_spark.idea: Develop automated tools to identify and eliminate redundant compile-time or run-time options during software construction.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reducing variability may decrease software flexibility to adapt to different environments.
idea_spark.inspiration: Starting from the 'null-variable software' concept, explore design patterns for building minimally variable components in specific domains like safety-critical systems.

papers.title: C-Mining: Unsupervised Discovery of Seeds for Cultural Data Synthesis via Geometric Misalignment
papers.summary: Achieving cultural alignment in Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly depends on synthetic data generation. For such synthesis, the most vital initial step is seed curation; however, current methods lack quantifiable standards for selecting these seeds. Existing approaches rely on unscalable manual curation or bias-prone LLM extraction, treating cultural specificity as an abstract concept rather than a measurable signal. In this paper, we address this "quantification gap" by proposing C-Mining, an unsupervised framework that transforms the discovery of cultural seeds from a subjective selection process into a computable data mining formulation. Our approach exploits a novel geometric insight, leveraging the cross-lingual misalignment of cultural concepts within pre-trained embedding spaces as a quantifiable discovery signal. By systematically identifying these regions characterized by pronounced linguistic exclusivity and geometric isolation, while actively filtering out noise, C-Mining automatically extracts high-fidelity Culture Points (CPs) from raw multilingual corpora without reliance on human or LLM supervision, reducing preparation costs by more than 150-fold. We further leverage the mined knowledge to steer the synthesis of diverse instruction-tuning datasets. Extensive experiments demonstrate that this seed-centric approach significantly enhances cultural understanding and reasoning capabilities, achieving a +6.03 point improvement on CulturalBench-Hard and surpassing state-of-the-art baselines, providing a scalable, quantifiable solution for high-quality cultural data synthesis.

ai.tldr: C-Mining是一个无监督框架，通过量化跨语言嵌入空间中的几何错位，自动从多语料库中发现高质量文化种子，用于合成文化对齐数据。
ai.motivation: 当前大语言模型的文化对齐依赖合成数据，而种子选择缺乏量化标准，现有方法依赖人工或易产生偏见的LLM提取，将文化特异性视为抽象概念而非可测量信号。
ai.method: 利用预训练嵌入空间中文化概念的跨语言几何错位作为可量化发现信号，系统识别具有显著语言排他性和几何隔离的区域，同时主动过滤噪声，自动提取高保真文化点。
ai.result: 将准备成本降低150倍以上，在CulturalBench-Hard上提升6.03分，超越现有基线，显著增强文化理解和推理能力。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究人员提供可扩展、可量化的文化数据合成解决方案，无需人工或LLM监督即可自动发现高质量文化种子。

idea_spark.idea: 利用跨语言嵌入中的不一致性作为文化特异性的代理指标。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于预训练嵌入的质量和偏差，可能无法捕捉细微或新兴的文化概念。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将主观概念（如文化）转化为可计算几何问题的思路，适用于其他需要量化抽象属性的领域。

en.tldr: C-Mining is an unsupervised framework that automatically discovers high-quality cultural seeds from multilingual corpora by quantifying geometric misalignment in cross-lingual embedding spaces for cultural data synthesis.
en.motivation: Cultural alignment in LLMs increasingly depends on synthetic data, but seed selection lacks quantifiable standards. Current methods rely on manual curation or bias-prone LLM extraction, treating cultural specificity as an abstract concept rather than a measurable signal.
en.method: Exploits cross-lingual geometric misalignment of cultural concepts in pre-trained embedding spaces as a quantifiable discovery signal. Systematically identifies regions with pronounced linguistic exclusivity and geometric isolation while filtering noise to automatically extract high-fidelity Culture Points (CPs).
en.result: Reduces preparation costs by more than 150-fold, achieves a +6.03 point improvement on CulturalBench-Hard, surpasses state-of-the-art baselines, and significantly enhances cultural understanding and reasoning capabilities.
en.help_to_user: Provides researchers with a scalable, quantifiable solution for high-quality cultural data synthesis, enabling automatic discovery of cultural seeds without human or LLM supervision.

idea_spark.idea: Using inconsistencies in cross-lingual embeddings as a proxy indicator for cultural specificity.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the quality and biases of pre-trained embeddings; may fail to capture subtle or emerging cultural concepts.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the transformation of subjective concepts (like culture) into computable geometric problems, applicable to other domains requiring quantification of abstract attributes.

papers.title: HyCal: A Training-Free Prototype Calibration Method for Cross-Discipline Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning
papers.summary: Pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) like CLIP show promise in continual learning, but existing Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning (FSCIL) methods assume homogeneous domains and balanced data distributions, limiting real-world applicability where data arises from heterogeneous disciplines with imbalanced sample availability and varying visual complexity. We identify Domain Gravity, a representational asymmetry where data imbalance across heterogeneous domains causes overrepresented or low-entropy domains to disproportionately influence the embedding space, leading to prototype drift and degraded performance on underrepresented or high-entropy domains. To address this, we introduce Cross-Discipline Variable Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning (XD-VSCIL), a benchmark capturing real-world heterogeneity and imbalance where Domain Gravity naturally intensifies. We propose Hybrid Prototype Calibration (HyCal), a training-free method combining cosine similarity and Mahalanobis distance to capture complementary geometric properties-directional alignment and covariance-aware magnitude-yielding stable prototypes under imbalanced heterogeneous conditions. Operating on frozen CLIP embeddings, HyCal achieves consistent retention-adaptation improvements while maintaining efficiency. Experiments show HyCal effectively mitigates Domain Gravity and outperforms existing methods in imbalanced cross-domain incremental learning.

ai.tldr: HyCal是一种无需训练的原型校准方法，用于解决跨学科、数据不平衡的少样本类增量学习问题，通过结合余弦相似度和马氏距离来稳定原型表示。
ai.motivation: 现有的少样本类增量学习方法假设同质领域和平衡数据分布，而现实世界数据来自异质学科，存在样本可用性不平衡和视觉复杂度差异，导致'领域引力'问题，即过代表或低熵领域对嵌入空间产生不成比例的影响。
ai.method: 提出混合原型校准（HyCal），一种无需训练的方法，在冻结的CLIP嵌入上操作，结合余弦相似度（捕捉方向对齐）和马氏距离（捕捉协方差感知的幅度），以捕获互补的几何特性，在异质不平衡条件下产生稳定的原型。
ai.result: 实验表明，HyCal有效缓解了领域引力问题，在不平衡跨领域增量学习中优于现有方法，同时保持了效率。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理现实世界中跨学科、数据不平衡的少样本增量学习场景提供了一个高效、无需训练的解决方案，可直接应用于冻结的视觉语言模型嵌入。

idea_spark.idea: 利用预训练模型的冻结嵌入，通过几何校准而非参数更新来适应新任务，减少计算开销并避免灾难性遗忘。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于预训练模型嵌入的质量和通用性；在领域差异极大或视觉特征极其复杂的情况下，几何校准可能不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从'表征不对称性'角度分析跨领域学习问题，并探索更多互补的几何或统计度量来增强原型的鲁棒性。

en.tldr: HyCal is a training-free prototype calibration method for cross-discipline few-shot class-incremental learning, stabilizing prototype representations by combining cosine similarity and Mahalanobis distance.
en.motivation: Existing few-shot class-incremental learning methods assume homogeneous domains and balanced data distributions, whereas real-world data arises from heterogeneous disciplines with imbalanced sample availability and varying visual complexity, leading to 'Domain Gravity'—representational asymmetry where overrepresented domains disproportionately influence the embedding space.
en.method: Proposes Hybrid Prototype Calibration (HyCal), a training-free method operating on frozen CLIP embeddings. It combines cosine similarity (capturing directional alignment) and Mahalanobis distance (capturing covariance-aware magnitude) to capture complementary geometric properties, yielding stable prototypes under imbalanced heterogeneous conditions.
en.result: Experiments show HyCal effectively mitigates Domain Gravity and outperforms existing methods in imbalanced cross-domain incremental learning while maintaining efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient, training-free solution for real-world cross-discipline, imbalanced few-shot incremental learning scenarios, directly applicable to frozen vision-language model embeddings.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage frozen embeddings of pretrained models and adapt to new tasks via geometric calibration rather than parameter updates, reducing computational cost and avoiding catastrophic forgetting.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on the quality and generality of the pretrained model embeddings; geometric calibration may be insufficient for extremely divergent domains or highly complex visual features.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires analyzing cross-domain learning problems from the perspective of 'representational asymmetry' and exploring more complementary geometric or statistical measures to enhance prototype robustness.

papers.title: MacWilliams Identities for Intrinsic Quantum Codes
papers.summary: We develop an intrinsic enumerator framework for quantum error correction in unitary representations of symmetry groups. An intrinsic quantum code is a subspace of a representation $V$ of a group $G$, and errors are organized by the decomposition of the conjugation representation on $\mathcal{L}(V)$ into isotypic subspaces. Associated with any orthogonal decomposition of $\mathcal{L}(V)$ we introduce two families of quadratic enumerators, called projector and twirl enumerators, which satisfy positivity, normalization, and Knill--Laflamme type inequalities. When the conjugation representation is multiplicity--free, these enumerators are related by a linear transform that we interpret as an intrinsic MacWilliams identity. For $G=\mathrm{SU}(2)$, we compute this transform explicitly in terms of Wigner $6j$-symbols. Applied to symmetric-power representations, this gives linear programming bounds for permutation-invariant qubit and qudit codes, including extremality results for the four-qubit, seven-qubit, and three-qutrit examples treated here. We also develop the general equivariant theory in the presence of multiplicities, where the enumerators become matrix-valued, the MacWilliams transform becomes block unitary, and the resulting feasibility problem becomes semidefinite; we illustrate this theory in a first non-multiplicity-free $\mathrm{SU}(3)$ example.

ai.tldr: 本文为对称群酉表示中的量子纠错码建立了内禀枚举框架，引入了投影和旋转枚举器，并在共轭表示无重数时推导出内禀MacWilliams恒等式，特别对SU(2)群用Wigner 6j符号显式计算该变换，应用于对称幂表示得到置换不变量子码的线性规划界。
ai.motivation: 传统量子纠错理论缺乏对具有对称性结构的量子码的系统代数刻画，需要发展内禀于群表示的枚举理论来统一处理对称约束下的量子码性能界限。
ai.method: 在群G的表示空间V中定义内禀量子码，基于共轭表示在算子空间L(V)上的等变分解构造投影枚举器和旋转枚举器两类二次型枚举器，建立其正定性、归一化和Knill-Laflamme型不等式。在无重数情形证明枚举器间存在线性变换关系（MacWilliams恒等式），对SU(2)群用Wigner 6j符号显式计算变换矩阵。
ai.result: 1. 建立了无重数共轭表示下的内禀MacWilliams恒等式；2. 对SU(2)群给出显式变换公式；3. 应用于对称幂表示得到置换不变量子比特/量子dit码的线性规划界，验证了四比特、七比特和三qutrit码的极值性；4. 发展了有重数情形的矩阵值枚举器理论，MacWilliams变换成为分块酉矩阵，可行性问题转化为半定规划。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究具有对称性的量子纠错码提供了系统的代数工具，特别是置换不变码的线性规划界计算方法，以及群表示论与量子信息交叉的新理论框架。

idea_spark.idea: 利用群表示论中的等变分解统一处理各类对称约束量子码的枚举问题
idea_spark.risk: 有重数情形的矩阵值枚举器计算复杂度可能随表示维数快速增长
idea_spark.inspiration: 该框架可扩展至其他李群或有限群表示，用于设计新型对称保护量子码

en.tldr: We develop an intrinsic enumerator framework for quantum error correction in unitary group representations, introducing projector and twirl enumerators that satisfy MacWilliams identities when the conjugation representation is multiplicity-free, explicitly computing the transform for SU(2) using Wigner 6j-symbols, and applying it to symmetric-power representations to obtain linear programming bounds for permutation-invariant codes.
en.motivation: Traditional quantum error correction lacks systematic algebraic characterization for codes with symmetry structures, necessitating an intrinsic enumerator theory grounded in group representations to unify performance bounds under symmetry constraints.
en.method: Define intrinsic quantum codes in representation space V of group G, construct quadratic projector and twirl enumerators based on equivariant decomposition of the conjugation representation on L(V), establish positivity, normalization, and Knill-Laflamme-type inequalities. Prove linear transform relations (MacWilliams identities) between enumerators in multiplicity-free cases, explicitly compute transform matrices for SU(2) using Wigner 6j-symbols.
en.result: 1. Established intrinsic MacWilliams identities for multiplicity-free conjugation representations; 2. Derived explicit transform formulas for SU(2); 3. Applied to symmetric-power representations to obtain linear programming bounds for permutation-invariant qubit/qudit codes, verifying extremality for four-qubit, seven-qubit, and three-qutrit examples; 4. Developed matrix-valued enumerator theory for general multiplicity cases where MacWilliams transforms become block unitary and feasibility reduces to semidefinite programming.
en.help_to_user: Provides systematic algebraic tools for studying quantum error-correcting codes with symmetries, particularly linear programming bound methods for permutation-invariant codes, and a new theoretical framework bridging group representation theory and quantum information.

idea_spark.idea: Unifying enumeration problems for symmetry-constrained quantum codes through equivariant decomposition in group representation theory
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity of matrix-valued enumerators in multiplicity cases may grow rapidly with representation dimension
idea_spark.inspiration: Framework extensible to other Lie groups or finite group representations for designing novel symmetry-protected quantum codes

papers.title: GAViD: A Large-Scale Multimodal Dataset for Context-Aware Group Affect Recognition from Videos
papers.summary: Understanding affective dynamics in real-world social systems is fundamental to modeling and analyzing human-human interactions in complex environments. Group affect emerges from intertwined human-human interactions, contextual influences, and behavioral cues, making its quantitative modeling a challenging computational social systems problem. However, computational modeling of group affect in in-the-wild scenarios remains challenging due to limited large-scale annotated datasets and the inherent complexity of multimodal social interactions shaped by contextual and behavioral variability. The lack of comprehensive datasets annotated with multimodal and contextual information further limits advances in the field. To address this, we introduce the Group Affect from ViDeos (GAViD) dataset, comprising 5091 video clips with multimodal data (video, audio and context), annotated with ternary valence and discrete emotion labels and enriched with VideoGPT-generated contextual metadata and human-annotated action cues. We also present Context-Aware Group Affect Recognition Network (CAGNet) for multimodal context-aware group affect recognition. CAGNet achieves 63.20\% test accuracy on GAViD, comparable to state-of-the-art performance. The dataset and code are available at github.com/deepakkumar-iitr/GAViD.

ai.tldr: 本文介绍了GAViD数据集和CAGNet模型，用于在真实场景下进行上下文感知的群体情感识别。
ai.motivation: 真实社交系统中群体情感的计算建模面临大规模标注数据缺乏和多模态交互复杂性高的挑战。
ai.method: 构建了包含5091个视频片段的多模态数据集（视频、音频、上下文），并提出了结合上下文感知的群体情感识别网络CAGNet。
ai.result: CAGNet在GAViD数据集上取得了63.20%的测试准确率，与现有先进方法性能相当。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个大规模、多模态、带上下文注释的群体情感数据集和一个有效的基准模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将行为线索与上下文信息共同建模，以捕捉群体情感涌现的动态过程。
idea_spark.risk: 在复杂真实场景中，上下文和行为变量的高维度可能增加模型过拟合风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索将类似的上下文感知框架应用于其他社会计算任务，如群体意图识别或协作行为分析。

en.tldr: This paper introduces the GAViD dataset and CAGNet model for context-aware group affect recognition in in-the-wild scenarios.
en.motivation: Computational modeling of group affect in real-world social systems is hindered by the lack of large-scale annotated datasets and the complexity of multimodal interactions.
en.method: Constructed a multimodal dataset of 5091 video clips (video, audio, context) and proposed the Context-Aware Group Affect Recognition Network (CAGNet).
en.result: CAGNet achieves 63.20% test accuracy on GAViD, comparable to state-of-the-art performance.
en.help_to_user: Provides a large-scale, multimodal, context-annotated dataset for group affect and an effective baseline model.

idea_spark.idea: Jointly modeling behavioral cues and contextual information to capture the emergent dynamics of group affect.
idea_spark.risk: High dimensionality of contextual and behavioral variables in complex real-world scenes may increase model overfitting risk.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore applying similar context-aware frameworks to other social computing tasks, such as group intention recognition or collaborative behavior analysis.

papers.title: PLAF: Pixel-wise Language-Aligned Feature Extraction for Efficient 3D Scene Understanding
papers.summary: Accurate open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding requires semantic representations that are both language-aligned and spatially precise at the pixel level, while remaining scalable when lifted to 3D space. However, existing representations struggle to jointly satisfy these requirements, and densely propagating pixel-wise semantics to 3D often results in substantial redundancy, leading to inefficient storage and querying in large-scale scenes. To address these challenges, we present \emph{PLAF}, a Pixel-wise Language-Aligned Feature extraction framework that enables dense and accurate semantic alignment in 2D without sacrificing open-vocabulary expressiveness. Building upon this representation, we further design an efficient semantic storage and querying scheme that significantly reduces redundancy across both 2D and 3D domains. Experimental results show that \emph{PLAF} provides a strong semantic foundation for accurate and efficient open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding. The codes are publicly available at https://github.com/RockWenJJ/PLAF.

ai.tldr: PLAF是一个像素级语言对齐特征提取框架，用于高效、准确的开放词汇3D场景理解，通过减少冗余提升存储和查询效率。
ai.motivation: 现有方法难以同时实现像素级语言对齐、空间精确和3D可扩展性，且密集的像素语义传播到3D会导致大量冗余，影响大规模场景效率。
ai.method: 提出PLAF框架，在2D中实现密集准确的语言对齐特征提取，不牺牲开放词汇表达能力，并设计高效的语义存储与查询方案，减少2D和3D域的冗余。
ai.result: 实验表明PLAF为准确高效的开放词汇3D场景理解提供了强大的语义基础。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要高精度、可扩展开放词汇3D语义理解的场景（如机器人导航、AR/VR）提供高效的像素级语义表示方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将开放词汇语义对齐与空间效率解耦，先保证2D对齐质量，再优化3D存储结构
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于2D视觉语言模型的性能，在复杂遮挡或光照变化场景中可能退化
idea_spark.inspiration: 可应用于3D语义地图构建、具身智能场景交互、大规模点云语义标注等任务

en.tldr: PLAF is a pixel-wise language-aligned feature extraction framework for efficient and accurate open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding, improving storage and query efficiency through redundancy reduction.
en.motivation: Existing methods struggle to jointly achieve pixel-level language alignment, spatial precision, and 3D scalability, while dense pixel-wise semantic propagation to 3D creates substantial redundancy, hindering efficiency in large-scale scenes.
en.method: Proposes the PLAF framework for dense and accurate language-aligned feature extraction in 2D without sacrificing open-vocabulary expressiveness, and designs an efficient semantic storage and querying scheme that reduces redundancy across both 2D and 3D domains.
en.result: Experiments show that PLAF provides a strong semantic foundation for accurate and efficient open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient pixel-level semantic representation solution for scenarios requiring high-precision, scalable open-vocabulary 3D semantic understanding (e.g., robotic navigation, AR/VR).

idea_spark.idea: Decouple open-vocabulary semantic alignment from spatial efficiency—ensure 2D alignment quality first, then optimize 3D storage structure
idea_spark.risk: Relies on 2D vision-language model performance; may degrade in scenes with complex occlusion or lighting variations
idea_spark.inspiration: Applicable to tasks like 3D semantic map construction, embodied AI scene interaction, and large-scale point cloud semantic annotation

papers.title: QMutBench: A Dataset of Quantum Circuit Mutants
papers.summary: Quantum software testing has attracted interest in recent years, prompting the development of various techniques to automate the testing of quantum software. These techniques generate test cases that must be assessed for their effectiveness in detecting faults. Such an assessment requires benchmarks of faulty programs. However, there is a lack of benchmarks containing faults. In this data showcase, we propose QMutBench, a dataset that contains over 700,000 quantum circuit mutants representing different faults. The dataset is accessible via an online interface with selection criteria, such as the original quantum circuit(s) from which mutants are generated, the desired survival rate of the selected mutants, and other mutation characteristics (e.g., the type of faulty quantum gate). QMutBench provides quantum software developers and testers with an accessible online dataset to obtain benchmarks of mutants necessary to assess either the quality of the test cases generated by their testing technique or to compare different testing techniques. It also enables the development of new mutation-guided quantum software testing techniques.

ai.tldr: QMutBench是一个包含超过70万个量子电路变异体的数据集，用于评估量子软件测试技术的有效性。
ai.motivation: 量子软件测试缺乏包含故障的基准程序，难以评估测试用例的故障检测能力。
ai.method: 构建一个可通过在线界面访问的数据集，允许用户根据原始电路、变异体存活率、故障门类型等标准筛选变异体。
ai.result: 提供了超过70万个代表不同故障的量子电路变异体，支持测试质量评估和技术比较。
ai.help_to_user: 为量子软件开发者提供易于访问的变异体基准，用于评估测试用例质量或比较不同测试技术。

idea_spark.idea: 利用变异体存活率作为测试充分性指标，指导测试用例生成。
idea_spark.risk: 数据集可能无法覆盖所有现实世界的量子硬件故障类型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可开发基于变异指导的测试技术，或扩展数据集以包含噪声引起的量子错误。

en.tldr: QMutBench is a dataset containing over 700,000 quantum circuit mutants for evaluating the effectiveness of quantum software testing techniques.
en.motivation: There is a lack of benchmarks containing faults for quantum software testing, making it difficult to assess test case fault detection capability.
en.method: Construct an online-accessible dataset allowing users to filter mutants by criteria such as original circuit, mutant survival rate, and faulty gate type.
en.result: Provides over 700,000 quantum circuit mutants representing different faults, supporting test quality assessment and technique comparison.
en.help_to_user: Offers quantum software developers an accessible mutant benchmark to evaluate test case quality or compare different testing techniques.

idea_spark.idea: Use mutant survival rate as a test adequacy metric to guide test case generation.
idea_spark.risk: Dataset may not cover all real-world quantum hardware fault types.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could develop mutation-guided testing techniques or extend dataset to include noise-induced quantum errors.

papers.title: Think Multilingual, Not Harder: A Data-Efficient Framework for Teaching Reasoning Models to Code-Switch
papers.summary: Recent developments in reasoning capabilities have enabled large language models to solve increasingly complex mathematical, symbolic, and logical tasks. Interestingly, while reasoning models are often trained to generate monolingual text, these models have also been observed to code-switch (i.e., mix languages). Prior works have either viewed code-switching as an undesirable error, attempted to control code-switching through modifications to input prompts or the output decoding process, or focus on narrow subsets of languages, domains, tasks, and models. We address these gaps by introducing the first linguistically and behaviorally motivated fine-tuning framework for identifying beneficial code-switched reasoning behaviors in large language models and teaching these models to code-switch more effectively for reasoning. First, we create and systematically analyze a dataset of reasoning traces from diverse models, languages, tasks, and domains to understand the types of code-switching behaviors found in existing reasoning models. Then, we develop fine-tuning interventions that teach reasoning models to code-switch based on our observations of helpful behaviors in existing models. We find that our framework can significantly increase beneficial code-switched reasoning behaviors in a data-efficient manner. Interestingly, we also find that code-switching behaviors in reasoning models can be modified by fine-tuning for tasks that do not directly demonstrate code-switching in reasoning (e.g., machine translation). Our work suggests that data-efficient interventions can instill helpful forms of code-switching behavior in reasoning models.

ai.tldr: 提出首个数据高效的微调框架，用于识别和教授大语言模型在推理任务中进行有益的语码转换行为。
ai.motivation: 现有研究要么将推理模型中的语码转换视为错误，要么试图控制它，或仅关注狭窄的语言/任务子集，缺乏系统性的有益行为识别与教学框架。
ai.method: 首先创建并系统分析多模型、多语言、多任务的推理轨迹数据集，理解现有模型中的语码转换行为；然后基于观察到的有益行为，设计微调干预措施来教授模型进行语码转换。
ai.result: 该框架能以数据高效的方式显著增加有益的语码转换推理行为；有趣的是，即使微调任务（如机器翻译）不直接展示推理中的语码转换，也能修改模型的语码转换行为。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究者和开发者提供了一种系统的方法来理解和引导大语言模型在复杂推理任务中的多语言混合行为，提升其跨语言推理能力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用不直接相关的任务（如翻译）微调来间接塑造模型在目标任务（如推理）中的语言行为模式。
idea_spark.risk: 对“有益”语码转换行为的定义可能具有主观性或任务依赖性，需谨慎评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考模型内部的多语言表征如何被外部任务微调所影响和重构。

idea_spark.idea: 利用不直接相关的任务（如翻译）微调来间接塑造模型在目标任务（如推理）中的语言行为模式。
idea_spark.risk: 对“有益”语码转换行为的定义可能具有主观性或任务依赖性，需谨慎评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考模型内部的多语言表征如何被外部任务微调所影响和重构。

en.tldr: Proposes the first data-efficient fine-tuning framework for identifying and teaching beneficial code-switching behaviors to large language models in reasoning tasks.
en.motivation: Prior work either treats code-switching in reasoning models as an error, attempts to control it, or focuses on narrow subsets, lacking a systematic framework for identifying and teaching beneficial behaviors.
en.method: First, create and systematically analyze a dataset of reasoning traces from diverse models, languages, tasks, and domains to understand existing code-switching behaviors. Then, develop fine-tuning interventions based on observed helpful behaviors to teach models to code-switch.
en.result: The framework significantly increases beneficial code-switched reasoning behaviors in a data-efficient manner. Interestingly, fine-tuning on tasks not directly demonstrating code-switching in reasoning (e.g., machine translation) can also modify such behaviors.
en.help_to_user: Provides researchers and developers with a systematic approach to understand and guide multilingual mixing behavior in LLMs for complex reasoning, enhancing cross-lingual reasoning capabilities.

idea_spark.idea: Using fine-tuning on indirectly related tasks (e.g., translation) to indirectly shape a model's language behavior patterns in a target task (e.g., reasoning).
idea_spark.risk: The definition of 'beneficial' code-switching behavior may be subjective or task-dependent, requiring careful evaluation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking about how a model's internal multilingual representations can be influenced and reconfigured by external task fine-tuning.

papers.title: From Vulnerable Data Subjects to Vulnerabilizing Data Practices: Navigating the Protection Paradox in AI-Based Analyses of Platformized Lives
papers.summary: This paper traces a conceptual shift from understanding vulnerability as a static, essentialized property of data subjects to examining how it is actively enacted through data practices. Unlike reflexive ethical frameworks focused on missing or counter-data, we address the condition of abundance inherent to platformized life-a context where a near inexhaustible mass of data points already exists, shifting the ethical challenge to the researcher's choices in operating upon this existing mass. We argue that the ethical integrity of data science depends not just on who is studied, but on how technical pipelines transform "vulnerable" individuals into data subjects whose vulnerability can be further precarized. We develop this argument through an AI for Social Good (AI4SG) case: a journalist's request to use computer vision to quantify child presence in monetized YouTube 'family vlogs' for regulatory advocacy. This case reveals a "protection paradox": how data-driven efforts to protect vulnerable subjects can inadvertently impose new forms of computational exposure, reductionism, and extraction. Using this request as a point of departure, we perform a methodological deconstruction of the AI pipeline to show how granular technical decisions are ethically constitutive. We contribute a reflexive ethics protocol that translates these insights into a reflexive roadmap for research ethics surrounding platformized data subjects. Organized around four critical junctures-dataset design, operationalization, inference, and dissemination-the protocol identifies technical questions and ethical tensions where well-intentioned work can slide into renewed extraction or exposure. For every decision point, the protocol offers specific prompts to navigate four cross-cutting vulnerabilizing factors: exposure, monetization, narrative fixing, and algorithmic optimization. Rather than uncritically...

ai.tldr: 本文提出从将脆弱性视为数据主体的静态属性，转向研究数据实践如何主动构建脆弱性。通过一个AI4SG案例，揭示了旨在保护脆弱主体的数据驱动努力可能无意中导致新的计算暴露、简化论和提取，即“保护悖论”。
ai.motivation: 平台化生活产生了近乎无穷的数据点，伦理挑战从数据缺失转向研究者对现有海量数据的操作选择。需要审视技术流程如何将“脆弱”个体转化为数据主体，并可能加剧其脆弱性。
ai.method: 通过一个具体案例（记者请求使用计算机视觉量化YouTube家庭视频博客中的儿童存在以进行监管倡导）作为切入点，对AI技术流程进行方法论解构，展示细粒度技术决策的伦理构成性。
ai.result: 开发了一个反思性伦理协议，围绕数据集设计、操作化、推断和传播四个关键节点，识别技术问题和伦理张力，并提供具体提示以应对暴露、货币化、叙事固定和算法优化这四个交叉的脆弱化因素。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理平台化数据主体的研究伦理提供了一个结构化的反思路线图，帮助研究者识别和导航技术决策中潜在的伦理风险。

idea_spark.idea: 将“保护悖论”的分析框架应用于其他AI4SG或数据驱动的研究场景，评估干预措施本身可能带来的新风险。
idea_spark.risk: 协议的有效性依赖于研究者的自觉应用，可能无法完全捕捉动态平台环境中的所有意外后果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对技术工具“双重作用”（既可用于保护也可能导致伤害）的系统性审视，推动超越意图的、关注实践过程的伦理评估。

idea_spark.idea: Apply the 'protection paradox' analytical framework to other AI4SG or data-driven research scenarios to evaluate how interventions themselves might introduce new risks.
idea_spark.risk: The protocol's effectiveness relies on researchers' conscientious application and may not fully capture all unintended consequences in dynamic platform environments.

papers.title: FineCog-Nav: Integrating Fine-grained Cognitive Modules for Zero-shot Multimodal UAV Navigation
papers.summary: UAV vision-language navigation (VLN) requires an agent to navigate complex 3D environments from an egocentric perspective while following ambiguous multi-step instructions over long horizons. Existing zero-shot methods remain limited, as they often rely on large base models, generic prompts, and loosely coordinated modules. In this work, we propose FineCog-Nav, a top-down framework inspired by human cognition that organizes navigation into fine-grained modules for language processing, perception, attention, memory, imagination, reasoning, and decision-making. Each module is driven by a moderate-sized foundation model with role-specific prompts and structured input-output protocols, enabling effective collaboration and improved interpretability. To support fine-grained evaluation, we construct AerialVLN-Fine, a curated benchmark of 300 trajectories derived from AerialVLN, with sentence-level instruction-trajectory alignment and refined instructions containing explicit visual endpoints and landmark references. Experiments show that FineCog-Nav consistently outperforms zero-shot baselines in instruction adherence, long-horizon planning, and generalization to unseen environments. These results suggest the effectiveness of fine-grained cognitive modularization for zero-shot aerial navigation. Project page: https://smartdianlab.github.io/projects-FineCogNav.

ai.tldr: FineCog-Nav是一个受人类认知启发的无人机视觉语言导航框架，通过细粒度认知模块实现零样本导航，并在新基准上超越现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有零样本导航方法依赖大型基础模型和通用提示，模块协调松散，在复杂3D环境和长时程模糊指令下表现有限。
ai.method: 提出自上而下的框架，将导航分解为语言处理、感知、注意力、记忆、想象、推理和决策等细粒度模块，每个模块由中等规模基础模型驱动，使用角色特定提示和结构化输入输出协议。
ai.result: 在构建的AerialVLN-Fine基准（300条轨迹）上，FineCog-Nav在指令遵循、长时程规划和未见环境泛化方面持续优于零样本基线。
ai.help_to_user: 为无人机视觉语言导航提供了一种模块化、可解释的零样本解决方案，通过认知分解提升复杂任务处理能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将复杂导航任务分解为人类认知启发的独立功能模块，通过结构化协议协调中等规模模型。
idea_spark.risk: 模块间通信开销可能增加，且各模块的提示工程需要针对具体任务精心设计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 认知科学中的功能分解为构建可解释、协作式AI系统提供了新思路，可应用于自动驾驶、服务机器人等领域。

idea_spark.idea: 将复杂导航任务分解为人类认知启发的独立功能模块，通过结构化协议协调中等规模模型。

en.tldr: FineCog-Nav is a human cognition-inspired framework for UAV vision-language navigation that achieves zero-shot navigation through fine-grained cognitive modules, outperforming existing methods on a new benchmark.
en.motivation: Existing zero-shot navigation methods rely on large base models and generic prompts with loosely coordinated modules, showing limitations in complex 3D environments with ambiguous multi-step instructions over long horizons.
en.method: Proposes a top-down framework that decomposes navigation into fine-grained modules for language processing, perception, attention, memory, imagination, reasoning, and decision-making. Each module is driven by a moderate-sized foundation model with role-specific prompts and structured input-output protocols.
en.result: On the constructed AerialVLN-Fine benchmark (300 trajectories), FineCog-Nav consistently outperforms zero-shot baselines in instruction adherence, long-horizon planning, and generalization to unseen environments.
en.help_to_user: Provides a modular, interpretable zero-shot solution for UAV vision-language navigation, enhancing complex task handling through cognitive decomposition.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose complex navigation tasks into independent functional modules inspired by human cognition, coordinated through structured protocols using moderate-sized models.
idea_spark.risk: Inter-module communication overhead may increase, and prompt engineering for each module requires careful task-specific design.
idea_spark.inspiration: Functional decomposition from cognitive science offers new avenues for building interpretable, collaborative AI systems applicable to autonomous driving, service robots, and similar domains.

papers.title: PIIBench: A Unified Multi-Source Benchmark Corpus for Personally Identifiable Information Detection
papers.summary: We present PIIBench, a unified benchmark corpus for Personally Identifiable Information (PII) detection in natural language text. Existing resources for PII detection are fragmented across domain-specific corpora with mutually incompatible annotation schemes, preventing systematic comparison of detection systems. We consolidate ten publicly available datasets spanning synthetic PII corpora, multilingual Named Entity Recognition (NER) benchmarks, and financial domain annotated text, yielding a corpus of 2,369,883 annotated sequences and 3.35 million entity mentions across 48 canonical PII entity types. We develop a principled normalization pipeline that maps 80+ source-specific label variants to a standardized BIO tagging scheme, applies frequency-based suppression of near absent entity types, and produces stratified 80/10/10 train/validation/test splits preserving source distribution. To establish baseline difficulty, we evaluate eight published systems spanning rule-based engines (Microsoft Presidio), general purpose NER models (spaCy, BERT-base NER, XLM-RoBERTa NER, SpanMarker mBERT, SpanMarker BERT), a PII-specific model (Piiranha DeBERTa), and a financial NER specialist (XtremeDistil FiNER). All systems achieve span-level F1 below 0.14, with the best system (Presidio, F1=0.1385) still producing zero recall on most entity types. These results directly quantify the domain-silo problem and demonstrate that PIIBench presents a substantially harder and more comprehensive evaluation challenge than any existing single source PII dataset. The dataset construction pipeline and benchmark evaluation code are publicly available at https://github.com/pritesh-2711/pii-bench.

ai.tldr: PIIBench是一个用于个人身份信息检测的统一多源基准语料库，整合了十个公开数据集，包含240万标注序列和48种PII实体类型，系统评估显示现有检测系统性能普遍低下。
ai.motivation: 现有的PII检测资源分散在领域特定的语料库中，标注方案互不兼容，阻碍了检测系统的系统性比较。
ai.method: 整合十个公开数据集，开发规范化流程将80多种源标签映射到标准BIO标注方案，应用基于频率的实体类型抑制，并生成保持源分布的分层训练/验证/测试分割。
ai.result: 评估的八个系统在跨度级F1得分均低于0.14，最佳系统（Presidio，F1=0.1385）对大多数实体类型的召回率为零，直接量化了领域孤岛问题。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个更全面、更难的PII检测评估基准，其数据集构建管道和基准评估代码已公开。

idea_spark.idea: 将领域特定的标注方案映射到统一框架，并应用频率抑制处理罕见实体
idea_spark.risk: 标准化过程可能丢失源特定的细微差别，频率抑制可能影响罕见但重要的实体类型
idea_spark.inspiration: 该方法可应用于其他需要整合异构标注资源的NLP任务，如事件抽取或关系分类
