en.tldr: PIIBench is a unified multi-source benchmark corpus for Personally Identifiable Information detection, consolidating ten public datasets with 2.4 million annotated sequences and 48 PII entity types, with systematic evaluation showing poor performance of existing detection systems.
en.motivation: Existing resources for PII detection are fragmented across domain-specific corpora with mutually incompatible annotation schemes, preventing systematic comparison of detection systems.
en.method: Consolidate ten publicly available datasets, develop a normalization pipeline mapping 80+ source-specific labels to a standardized BIO tagging scheme, apply frequency-based suppression of near absent entity types, and produce stratified train/validation/test splits preserving source distribution.
en.result: All eight evaluated systems achieve span-level F1 below 0.14, with the best system (Presidio, F1=0.1385) producing zero recall on most entity types, directly quantifying the domain-silo problem.
en.help_to_user: Provides a substantially harder and more comprehensive evaluation challenge for PII detection, with dataset construction pipeline and benchmark evaluation code publicly available.

idea_spark.idea: Map domain-specific annotation schemes to a unified framework and apply frequency suppression for rare entities
idea_spark.risk: Standardization may lose source-specific nuances; frequency suppression may affect rare but important entity types
idea_spark.inspiration: This approach can be applied to other NLP tasks requiring integration of heterogeneously annotated resources, such as event extraction or relation classification

papers.title: CIG: Measuring Conversational Information Gain in Deliberative Dialogues with Semantic Memory Dynamics
papers.summary: Measuring the quality of public deliberation requires evaluating not only civility or argument structure, but also the informational progress of a conversation. We introduce a framework for Conversational Information Gain (CIG) that evaluates each utterance in terms of how it advances collective understanding of the target topic. To operationalize CIG, we model an evolving semantic memory of the discussion: the system extracts atomic claims from utterances and incrementally consolidates them into a structured memory state. Using this memory, we score each utterance along three interpretable dimensions: Novelty, Relevance, and Implication Scope. We annotate 80 segments from two moderated deliberative settings (TV debates and community discussions) with these dimensions and show that memory-derived dynamics (e.g., the number of claim updates) correlate more strongly with human-perceived CIG than traditional heuristics such as utterance length or TF--IDF. We develop effective LLM-based CIG predictors paving the way for information-focused conversation quality analysis in dialogues and deliberative success.

ai.tldr: 提出对话信息增益（CIG）框架，通过建模语义记忆动态来评估审议对话中每个话语对集体理解主题的推进程度。
ai.motivation: 评估公共审议质量需要衡量对话的信息进展，而不仅仅是礼貌或论证结构。
ai.method: 从话语中提取原子主张，逐步整合到结构化记忆状态中，基于记忆从新颖性、相关性和隐含范围三个维度对每个话语进行评分。
ai.result: 在电视辩论和社区讨论数据上，基于记忆的动态指标（如主张更新数量）比传统启发式方法（如话语长度或TF-IDF）与人类感知的CIG相关性更强；开发了有效的基于LLM的CIG预测器。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种量化对话信息价值的方法，可用于分析审议对话的质量和成功度。

idea_spark.idea: 将对话视为一个逐步构建的集体知识库，通过跟踪其更新状态来度量信息流动。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于主张提取的准确性；在高度隐含或情感化对话中，信息增益的量化可能具有挑战性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将对话过程可视化为知识图谱的演化，或用于实时反馈系统以促进更信息丰富的讨论。

zh.result: 在电视辩论和社区讨论数据上，基于记忆的动态指标（如主张更新数量）比传统启发式方法（如话语长度或TF-IDF）与人类感知的CIG相关性更强；开发了有效的基于LLM的CIG预测器。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种量化对话信息价值的方法，可用于分析审议对话的质量和成功度。

idea_spark.idea: 将对话视为一个逐步构建的集体知识库，通过跟踪其更新状态来度量信息流动。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于主张提取的准确性；在高度隐含或情感化对话中，信息增益的量化可能具有挑战性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将对话过程可视化为知识图谱的演化，或用于实时反馈系统以促进更信息丰富的讨论。

en.tldr: Proposes a Conversational Information Gain (CIG) framework that measures how each utterance advances collective understanding in deliberative dialogues by modeling semantic memory dynamics.
en.motivation: Assessing the quality of public deliberation requires evaluating the informational progress of a conversation, beyond just civility or argument structure.
en.method: Extracts atomic claims from utterances and incrementally consolidates them into a structured memory state; scores each utterance along three dimensions: Novelty, Relevance, and Implication Scope, based on this memory.
en.result: On TV debates and community discussions, memory-derived dynamics (e.g., number of claim updates) correlate more strongly with human-perceived CIG than traditional heuristics like utterance length or TF-IDF; effective LLM-based CIG predictors are developed.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method to quantify the informational value of conversations, useful for analyzing the quality and success of deliberative dialogues.

idea_spark.idea: Treat dialogue as a collectively built knowledge base and measure information flow by tracking its update states.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accuracy of claim extraction; quantifying information gain may be challenging in highly implicit or emotional dialogues.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking about visualizing dialogue processes as evolving knowledge graphs or using it in real-time feedback systems to foster more informative discussions.

papers.title: AgentV-RL: Scaling Reward Modeling with Agentic Verifier
papers.summary: Verifiers have been demonstrated to enhance LLM reasoning via test-time scaling (TTS). Yet, they face significant challenges in complex domains. Error propagation from incorrect intermediate reasoning can lead to false positives for seemingly plausible solutions, while lacking external grounding makes verifiers unreliable on computation or knowledge-intensive tasks. To address these challenges, we propose Agentic Verifier, a framework that transforms reward modeling into a multi-turn, tool-augmented deliberative process. We introduce complementary forward and backward agents: one traces solutions from premises to conclusions, while the other re-checks conclusions against their underlying premises. This bidirectional process enables a comprehensive, reliable, and interpretable assessment of solutions. To facilitate practical deployment, we propose AgentV-RL. Through proactive exploration and reinforcement learning, the verifier autonomously interleaves tool-use with internal reasoning. Extensive experiments show that Agentic Verifier yields consistent performance gains under both parallel and sequential TTS. Notably, our 4B variant surpasses state-of-the-art ORMs by 25.2%, positioning it as a promising paradigm for agentic reward modeling.

ai.tldr: AgentV-RL：通过智能验证器扩展奖励建模，将验证转化为多轮工具增强的审慎过程，实现可靠、可解释的解决方案评估。
ai.motivation: 传统验证器在复杂领域面临挑战：错误的中间推理会导致错误传播，缺乏外部基础使其在计算或知识密集型任务中不可靠。
ai.method: 提出智能验证器框架，引入互补的前向和后向智能体进行双向验证，并通过强化学习自主交织工具使用与内部推理。
ai.result: 实验表明，智能验证器在并行和顺序测试时扩展下均带来一致的性能提升，4B变体超越最先进ORMs 25.2%。
ai.help_to_user: 为复杂领域中的LLM推理验证提供了更可靠、可解释的评估范式，可应用于需要高可靠性判断的任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将静态验证转化为动态、多轮、工具增强的审慎过程，通过智能体交互提升判断深度。
idea_spark.risk: 框架复杂度可能增加计算开销；智能体间的协调失败可能导致验证循环或效率下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将验证任务设计为协作智能体系统，结合外部工具与内部推理，平衡自动化与可靠性。

en.tldr: AgentV-RL: Scaling reward modeling with an Agentic Verifier that transforms verification into a multi-turn, tool-augmented deliberative process for reliable and interpretable solution assessment.
en.motivation: Traditional verifiers face challenges in complex domains: error propagation from incorrect intermediate reasoning leads to false positives, and lack of external grounding makes them unreliable on computation or knowledge-intensive tasks.
en.method: Proposes the Agentic Verifier framework with complementary forward and backward agents for bidirectional verification, and employs reinforcement learning to autonomously interleave tool-use with internal reasoning.
en.result: Extensive experiments show consistent performance gains under both parallel and sequential test-time scaling, with the 4B variant surpassing state-of-the-art ORMs by 25.2%.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more reliable and interpretable evaluation paradigm for LLM reasoning verification in complex domains, applicable to tasks requiring high-reliability judgment.

idea_spark.idea: Transforming static verification into a dynamic, multi-turn, tool-augmented deliberative process through agent interactions to enhance judgment depth.
idea_spark.risk: Framework complexity may increase computational overhead; coordination failures between agents could lead to verification loops or efficiency degradation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires designing verification tasks as collaborative agent systems, balancing automation and reliability by combining external tools with internal reasoning.

papers.title: Towards Ultra-High-Rate Quantum Error Correction with Reconfigurable Atom Arrays
papers.summary: Quantum error correction is widely believed to be essential for large-scale quantum computation, but the required qubit overhead remains a central challenge. Quantum low-density parity-check codes can substantially reduce this overhead through high-rate encodings, yet finite-size instances with practical logical error rates often achieve encoding rates only around or below $1/10$. Here, building on a recent ultra-high-rate construction by Kasai, we identify new structural conditions on the underlying affine permutation matrices that make encoding rates exceeding $1/2$ compatible with efficient implementation on reconfigurable neutral atom arrays. These conditions define a co-designed family of ultra-high-rate quantum codes that supports efficient syndrome extraction and atom rearrangement under realistic parallel control constraints. Using a hierarchical decoder with high accuracy and good throughput, we study the performance under a circuit-level noise model with $p=0.1\%$, achieving per-logical-per-round error rates of $1.3_{-0.9}^{+3.0} \times 10^{-13}$ with a $[[2304,1156,\leq 14]]$ code and $2.9_{-1.5}^{+3.1} \times 10^{-11}$ with a $[[1152,580,\leq 12]]$ code. These results approach the teraquop regime, highlighting the promise of this code family for practical ultra-high-rate quantum error correction.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种与可重构中性原子阵列协同设计的超高速率量子纠错码族，在电路级噪声模型下实现了接近teraquop量级的逻辑错误率。
ai.motivation: 大规模量子计算需要量子纠错，但现有纠错码的编码率通常较低（约1/10），导致量子比特开销巨大。
ai.method: 基于Kasai的超高速率构造，识别了底层仿射置换矩阵的新结构条件，使其与可重构原子阵列的并行控制约束兼容，并采用分层解码器进行性能评估。
ai.help_to_user: 为在可重构原子阵列上实现编码率超过1/2的量子纠错提供了具体的设计条件和性能基准。

idea_spark.idea: 将超高速率LDPC码的数学结构与特定硬件的并行控制能力进行协同设计。
idea_spark.risk: 实际原子阵列的串扰、退相干时间可能影响理论性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发其他量子平台（如超导、离子阱）探索硬件感知的超高速率纠错码设计。

en.tldr: This paper presents a co-designed family of ultra-high-rate quantum error correction codes for reconfigurable neutral atom arrays, achieving logical error rates approaching the teraquop regime under a circuit-level noise model.
en.motivation: Quantum error correction is essential for large-scale quantum computation, but existing codes often have low encoding rates (~1/10), leading to high qubit overhead.
en.method: Building on Kasai's ultra-high-rate construction, new structural conditions on underlying affine permutation matrices are identified to ensure compatibility with parallel control constraints of reconfigurable atom arrays, and a hierarchical decoder is used for performance evaluation.
en.result: Under a circuit-level noise model with p=0.1%, the [[2304,1156,≤14]] code achieves a per-logical-per-round error rate of 1.3_{-0.9}^{+3.0}×10^{-13}, and the [[1152,580,≤12]] code achieves 2.9_{-1.5}^{+3.1}×10^{-11}, approaching the teraquop regime.
en.help_to_user: Provides concrete design conditions and performance benchmarks for implementing quantum error correction with encoding rates exceeding 1/2 on reconfigurable atom arrays.

idea_spark.idea: Co-design the mathematical structure of ultra-high-rate LDPC codes with the parallel control capabilities of specific hardware.
idea_spark.risk: Crosstalk and coherence times in real atom arrays may affect theoretical performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires other quantum platforms (e.g., superconducting, ion trap) to explore hardware-aware ultra-high-rate error correction code design.

papers.title: Watching Movies Like a Human: Egocentric Emotion Understanding for Embodied Companions
papers.summary: Embodied robotic agents often perceive movies through an egocentric screen-view interface rather than native cinematic footage, introducing domain shifts such as viewpoint distortion, scale variation, illumination changes, and environmental interference. However, existing research on movie emotion understanding is almost exclusively conducted on cinematic footage, limiting cross-domain generalization to real-world viewing scenarios. To bridge this gap, we introduce EgoScreen-Emotion (ESE), the first benchmark dataset for egocentric screen-view movie emotion understanding. ESE contains 224 movie trailers captured under controlled egocentric screen-view conditions, producing 28,667 temporally aligned key-frames annotated by multiple raters with a confidence-aware multi-label protocol to address emotional ambiguity. We further build a multimodal long-context emotion reasoning framework that models temporal visual evidence, narrative summaries, compressed historical context, and audio cues. Cross-domain experiments reveal a severe domain gap: models trained on cinematic footage drop from 27.99 to 16.69 Macro-F1 when evaluated on realistic egocentric screen-view observations. Training on ESE substantially improves robustness under realistic viewing conditions. Our approach achieves competitive performance compared with strong closed-source multimodal models, highlighting the importance of domain-specific data and long-context multimodal reasoning.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了首个面向具身智能体的自我中心屏幕视角电影情感理解基准数据集ESE，并构建了多模态长上下文情感推理框架，以解决电影原生镜头与真实观看场景间的领域差距问题。
ai.motivation: 现有电影情感理解研究几乎全部基于电影原生镜头，而具身机器人等智能体通常通过自我中心屏幕视角感知电影，这带来了视角畸变、尺度变化等领域偏移，限制了模型在真实观看场景中的泛化能力。
ai.method: 构建了EgoScreen-Emotion (ESE)数据集，包含224个在受控条件下捕获的电影预告片，生成28,667个时序对齐的关键帧，并采用置信度感知的多标签协议进行标注。提出了一个多模态长上下文情感推理框架，建模时序视觉证据、叙事摘要、压缩历史上下文和音频线索。
ai.result: 跨领域实验揭示了严重的领域差距：在电影原生镜头上训练的模型在真实自我中心屏幕视角观测上的Macro-F1分数从27.99降至16.69。在ESE上训练显著提高了模型在真实观看条件下的鲁棒性。所提方法相比强大的闭源多模态模型取得了有竞争力的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究具身智能体在真实世界观看场景下的情感理解提供了首个基准数据集和评估框架，强调了领域特定数据和长上下文多模态推理的重要性。

idea_spark.idea: 将ESE的采集与标注范式扩展到更广泛的视频内容类型（如教育视频、游戏实况）。探索领域自适应或元学习技术，以更高效地弥合电影原生与自我中心视角间的差距。
idea_spark.risk: 数据集规模有限（仅预告片），可能无法完全覆盖长片电影的完整叙事情感弧线。受控采集条件与真实用户观看环境（如手持设备晃动、环境光干扰）仍存在差异。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从“机器如何看待人类内容”的角度重新思考多媒体理解任务的设计，推动面向真实部署场景而非理想化数据分布的研究。

en.tldr: This paper introduces ESE, the first benchmark dataset for egocentric screen-view movie emotion understanding for embodied agents, and builds a multimodal long-context emotion reasoning framework to address the domain gap between native cinematic footage and real-world viewing scenarios.
en.motivation: Existing research on movie emotion understanding is conducted almost exclusively on cinematic footage. However, embodied agents like robots typically perceive movies through an egocentric screen-view interface, introducing domain shifts such as viewpoint distortion and scale variation, which limits cross-domain generalization to real-world viewing scenarios.
en.method: Constructed the EgoScreen-Emotion (ESE) dataset containing 224 movie trailers captured under controlled egocentric screen-view conditions, yielding 28,667 temporally aligned key-frames annotated with a confidence-aware multi-label protocol. Proposed a multimodal long-context emotion reasoning framework that models temporal visual evidence, narrative summaries, compressed historical context, and audio cues.
en.result: Cross-domain experiments reveal a severe domain gap: models trained on cinematic footage drop from 27.99 to 16.69 Macro-F1 when evaluated on realistic egocentric screen-view observations. Training on ESE substantially improves robustness under realistic viewing conditions. The proposed approach achieves competitive performance compared to strong closed-source multimodal models.
en.help_to_user: Provides the first benchmark dataset and evaluation framework for studying emotion understanding of embodied agents in real-world viewing scenarios, highlighting the importance of domain-specific data and long-context multimodal reasoning.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the ESE collection and annotation paradigm to broader video content types (e.g., educational videos, game live streams). Explore domain adaptation or meta-learning techniques to bridge the gap between native cinematic and egocentric views more efficiently.
idea_spark.risk: Limited dataset scale (only trailers) may not fully capture the complete narrative emotional arcs of full-length movies. Controlled capture conditions still differ from real user viewing environments (e.g., handheld device shake, ambient light interference).
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires rethinking the design of multimedia understanding tasks from the perspective of 'how machines view human content,' promoting research oriented toward real deployment scenarios rather than idealized data distributions.
